title: CI: auto-deploy to fly.io on release tag
description:
## Goal

Wire `.github/workflows/release.yml` to deploy warren to fly.io after the existing `release` job cuts a `v$VERSION` tag, so production tracks tagged releases (not every push to main).

## Why

- Warren is used to build warren; main moves fast, but prod should only run versioned/changelog-documented code.
- Today there is no CD — deploys are manual (`flyctl deploy` from a laptop).
- Target infra: fly.io container + Supabase Postgres. This issue is blocked on R-13 (warren-942c) shipping the Postgres backend, since the fly container needs to point `WARREN_DB_URL` at Supabase.

## Plan

Two edits to `.github/workflows/release.yml`:

1. Expose version-check decision as a job output on the existing `release` job:

```yaml
jobs:
  release:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: write
    outputs:
      released: ${{ steps.version-check.outputs.release }}
      version:  ${{ steps.version-check.outputs.version }}
    steps:
      # existing steps unchanged
```

2. Append a new `deploy` job:

```yaml
  deploy:
    needs: release
    if: needs.release.outputs.released == 'true'
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    concurrency:
      group: fly-deploy-production
      cancel-in-progress: false
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6
        with:
          ref: v${{ needs.release.outputs.version }}
      - uses: superfly/flyctl-actions/setup-flyctl@master
      - run: flyctl deploy --remote-only --strategy rolling
        env:
          FLY_API_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.FLY_API_TOKEN }}
```

## Design notes

- `needs: release` + `if: released == 'true'` — only deploys when a tag was actually cut. Skips workflow_dispatch re-runs and pushes that do not bump `package.json` + `src/index.ts`.
- `ref: v${{ version }}` — checks out the tag, not main HEAD. Guarantees the deployed image matches the GitHub release even if main moves between tag push and deploy step.
- `concurrency: cancel-in-progress: false` — back-to-back releases queue rather than cancelling an in-flight rolling restart.
- `--remote-only` — build on Fly builder, not the GH runner. Matches Fly docs.
- `--strategy rolling` — explicit (default depends on `fly.toml`). Single-machine safe; revisit `bluegreen` if scaled out.

## Pre-merge setup

- Create a fly deploy token scoped to the warren app: `fly tokens create deploy -a <app>`. Do NOT reuse the user-scoped token from `flyctl auth token`.
- Add as repo secret: `FLY_API_TOKEN` (Settings → Secrets → Actions).
- Confirm `fly.toml` is shaped for prod (image name, regions, mounts for any state, [http_service] section). Today's `fly.toml` exists at repo root but predates the Supabase plan — review before first deploy.

## Acceptance

1. Pushing a non-version-bump commit to main runs `release` job, which short-circuits (`release=false`); `deploy` job is skipped (visible as "skipped" in the run UI, not "failed").
2. Bumping `package.json` + `src/index.ts` to a new version on main:
   - `release` job tags `vX.Y.Z`, creates GitHub release from CHANGELOG.
   - `deploy` job checks out `vX.Y.Z`, runs `flyctl deploy --remote-only`, exits 0.
   - `fly status` shows the new release ID; `curl https://<app>.fly.dev/healthz` reports the new VERSION.
3. Two version bumps merged back-to-back: deploys run serially (queued by concurrency group), neither cancels the other.
4. A broken release (e.g., failing migration on first boot against Supabase) leaves the previous machine running because `--strategy rolling` will not promote a failing replacement. Document the rollback recipe (`fly releases list` + `fly deploy --image <prev-image>`) in CLAUDE.md or DEPLOY.md.

## Out of scope

- Preview environments per-PR or per-run (that is R-19, pl-2c59 — warren-1bcb).
- Staging environment. Single prod app for now; revisit if needed.
- Supabase migration automation (Postgres migrations apply on warren boot per R-13 step 5; no separate Supabase CLI step needed).

## Files

- Edit: `.github/workflows/release.yml` (the two changes above)
- Review/verify: `fly.toml`
- Maybe edit: `CLAUDE.md` or new `DEPLOY.md` (rollback recipe, secret setup checklist)

title: PR-template configurability — .warren/pr-template.md + named fragments
description:
<!-- seeds:plan-backref:start -->
Adopted into plan pl-2c59.

Parent seed: warren-1bcb — R-19 — Per-run preview environments
Plan template: feature
Plan approach: Phase along the natural seam: lock the cross-repo design and burrow contract first, then ship the foundational layers (`.warren/defaults.json` schema, `runs` columns, port allocator) in parallel, then the reap-time launch sub-step that…

Run `sd plan show pl-2c59` for the full plan (context, alternatives, sibling steps, acceptance criteria).
<!-- seeds:plan-backref:end -->

Sibling of R-19 (plan pl-2c59) — surfaces the broader PR-template configurability that R-19's `preview_url_or_placeholder` fragment is the integration point for.

## Problem

`buildPrContent` in `src/runs/reap.ts:552` hardcodes the PR body shape (title, summary, commits, files-changed, seeds, trailer). Projects can't customize what goes into the PR their warren runs open. R-19 adds a need for a conditional "preview URL" footer that only renders when the project has opted into preview environments — and the cleanest place to land that conditional is a configurable template system, not another hardcoded branch in `buildPrContent`.

## Shape

Make the PR body template-driven from a project-local `.warren/pr-template.md` file (or `.warren/defaults.json` `prTemplate` field for V1, with the move to a standalone markdown file aligned with the `.warren/` reorg follow-up).

Template fragments named, no-op when their data isn't present:

- `title` — defaults to the existing 70-char-budgeted title
- `summary` — what the agent reports
- `commits` — list of commits in the branch
- `files_changed` — diff stat
- `seeds` — linked seeds
- `preview_url_or_placeholder` — added by R-19; renders `<!-- warren:preview-placeholder -->`, the live URL, or the failure tail depending on `preview_state`
- `trailer` — Co-Authored-By etc.

Built-in default template ships in-tree. Project override replaces named sections only; unspecified sections fall back to the default. Template language is the same partial/inheritance system canopy uses, so authors don't learn a second syntax.

## Acceptance

- `.warren/pr-template.md` accepted; missing-is-not-error.
- `buildPrContent` consumes template fragments via the named-section system, not string concatenation.
- R-19's `preview_url_or_placeholder` fragment integrates as a named section, not a special case.
- Doctor warns on malformed template (unknown fragment names, unclosed tags).
- Documented in DEPLOY + README with an example override.

## Why under pl-2c59 (not a loose seed)

R-19's design lock explicitly defers PR-template configurability here so this plan stays focused. Keeping it under the same plan tracks the dependency: R-19's reap step (warren-f156) ships the `preview_url_or_placeholder` fragment as a hardcoded conditional today, this seed pluralizes it later. Order: this seed unblocks the conversion from "hardcoded conditional" → "named fragment in the configurable template."

title: .warren/ YAML reorg — one file per concern + backcompat from defaults.json
description:
<!-- seeds:plan-backref:start -->
Adopted into plan pl-2c59.

Parent seed: warren-1bcb — R-19 — Per-run preview environments
Plan template: feature
Plan approach: Phase along the natural seam: lock the cross-repo design and burrow contract first, then ship the foundational layers (`.warren/defaults.json` schema, `runs` columns, port allocator) in parallel, then the reap-time launch sub-step that…

Run `sd plan show pl-2c59` for the full plan (context, alternatives, sibling steps, acceptance criteria).
<!-- seeds:plan-backref:end -->

Follow-up of R-19 (plan pl-2c59). Cleans up `.warren/` so it stays human-friendly as more knobs accumulate.

## Problem

`.warren/` today is a one-file-per-major-concern mix:

    .warren/
      defaults.json   (global defaults: defaultRole, defaultBranch, defaultProvider, defaultModel)
      triggers.yaml   (scheduler triggers)

R-19 adds a `preview` block. R-15-ish work will add MCP config. The PR-template configurability seed (warren-bd49) wants `pr-template.md`. Continuing to cram everything into `defaults.json` produces a massive, hard-to-diff JSON blob — antithetical to how `.mulch/`, `.canopy/`, `.seeds/` are organized.

## Target layout

    .warren/
      config.yaml          (was defaults.json — global defaults in YAML)
      triggers.yaml        (unchanged)
      preview.yaml         (R-19 preview block lives here, not in config.yaml)
      pr-template.md       (warren-bd49 PR template lives here)

YAML over JSON across the board. One file per concern. Comments allowed (JSON's biggest weakness). Easier code review when a project's warren config changes.

## Backcompat

- `defaults.json` keeps working — loader prefers `config.yaml` when both exist, falls back to `defaults.json` with a deprecation warning surfaced in `warren doctor`.
- `preview.yaml` is loaded if present; otherwise the loader looks for a `preview` block in `config.yaml` (or in legacy `defaults.json`).
- Doc the migration in README + DEPLOY + a `.warren/MIGRATION.md`. No code-side break: existing installs keep running on `defaults.json` until they migrate at leisure.

## Acceptance

- Loader prefers per-concern YAML files; falls back to `config.yaml`; falls back to `defaults.json` with a deprecation warning.
- New installs scaffold with the YAML layout.
- Doctor surfaces the `defaults.json` deprecation warning with a one-shot migration command (`warren config migrate`).
- `.warren/MIGRATION.md` ships with before/after examples.

## Why under pl-2c59

R-19 chose to keep its `preview` block in `defaults.json` for V1 to avoid expanding scope. This seed is the explicit follow-up that hoists `preview` into its own file as part of the broader reorg. Sequenced after R-19 lands and after warren-bd49 (PR-template) since both add files that benefit from the new layout.

title: PR-close webhook → preview teardown (V2, depends on webhook infra)
description:
<!-- seeds:plan-backref:start -->
Adopted into plan pl-2c59.

Parent seed: warren-1bcb — R-19 — Per-run preview environments
Plan template: feature
Plan approach: Phase along the natural seam: lock the cross-repo design and burrow contract first, then ship the foundational layers (`.warren/defaults.json` schema, `runs` columns, port allocator) in parallel, then the reap-time launch sub-step that…

Run `sd plan show pl-2c59` for the full plan (context, alternatives, sibling steps, acceptance criteria).
<!-- seeds:plan-backref:end -->

Follow-up of R-19 (plan pl-2c59). Adds a fifth eviction signal — preview teardown on GitHub PR close/merge — once warren has GitHub webhook infrastructure.

## Problem

R-19's design lock (SPEC §11.L) settled on four eviction signals: idle TTL, max lifetime, global LRU, manual teardown. There's a fifth signal that's the *cleanest* one — "the reviewer merged or closed the PR, the preview is no longer needed" — but warren doesn't have GitHub webhook infra yet (R-18 territory). Filing this seed locks in the eventual behavior so when webhook infra lands, this just turns on.

## Shape

- On `pull_request.closed` (merged OR closed-without-merge) webhook for a PR that matches `runs.pr_url`, schedule the preview for teardown.
- Teardown re-uses the manual-teardown route (step warren-d725) so all eviction paths funnel through one idempotent action.
- Emit `preview_evicted` with `reason='pr_closed'` (new reason value alongside `idle_ttl`, `max_lifetime`, `lru`).
- Behaves correctly when the preview is already `torn-down` (no-op) or `failed` (no-op, log).

## Why deferred to V2-of-R-19

R-19 explicitly does not add a GitHub webhook surface; webhooks are a sibling track (~R-18). Building webhooks just for preview teardown would be a wrong-shaped seam. When the webhook surface lands for other reasons (label triggers, issue events), this seed adds one new event handler.

## Why under pl-2c59

Keeps the R-19 lifecycle story in one place rather than scattering eviction signals across plans. Sequenced explicitly after webhook infrastructure (no immediate dependency on R-19 step closure — could even land before R-19 ships if webhook infra arrives first).

title: Widen repo layer to dialect-aware (pg execution path)
description:
Scenario 19 (warren-480a) ships as a skip-gated harness contract because warren's repo layer is still sqlite-bound today. Every repo (agents, projects, runs, events, triggers, workers, burrows) uses drizzle-orm/bun-sqlite's synchronous `.run()` / `.get()` / `.all()` / sync `.transaction` builders (mx-994fa5). drizzle-orm/node-postgres builders are thenable-async (`.execute()` or `await db.insert(...)`); they do not expose `.run()` etc.

To unblock scenario 19 end-to-end on pg:

1. Widen every repo's `db` field from `DrizzleDb` (= `SqliteDrizzleDb`) to a dialect-aware handle. Options:
   - Branch internally on a passed-in `dialect` discriminator.
   - Introduce a thin RepoDb interface and provide sqlite + pg adapters.
2. Replace `.run()` / `.get()` / `.all()` with the dialect-correct call (e.g. `await db.insert(...).values(...)` for both, since drizzle's pg builders are PromiseLike and bun-sqlite builders' chainable result is acceptable via the typed result).
3. Convert sync `.transaction((tx) => {...})` callbacks to async-callback form so pg's signature is satisfied without breaking sqlite.
4. Update ~30 test files to await the changed call sites (mx-994fa5 lists them).
5. Lift the `createReposForDialect` gate at src/server/main.ts:336-344 — that gate explicitly references warren-480a as the unlock; the actual unlock is the repo widening, not the scenario.
6. Run `bun test` with WARREN_TEST_DIALECT=postgres + WARREN_TEST_PG_URL=… to validate the repos against pg through withDb() (mx-73932c).

Acceptance: scenario 19 (scripts/acceptance/scenarios/19-warren-on-postgres.ts) flips from skipped → passed when WARREN_TEST_PG_URL is set against a pg with CREATEDB. Plan pl-f17e acceptance #4 is then satisfied.

The plan pl-f17e bundled this work implicitly into step 7 (warren-480a). Splitting it out so warren-480a stays scoped to the scenario file + harness machinery; this seed owns the repo widening.

title: Complete R-13: widen repo layer to be dialect-polymorphic (pg + sqlite)
description: R-13 (Postgres backend) was announced shipped in v0.3.2 CHANGELOG, but discovery during dogfood deploy (2026-05-14) revealed the repo layer is still sqlite-coupled at the implementation level. Outer wiring (URL parsing, openDatabase, migrations, schema parity) is real, but src/db/repos/*.ts use sqlite-only drizzle APIs: tx.select()...get(), .run(), .all(), sync transactions. WARREN_DB_URL=postgres:// boots, runs migrations, then crash-loops the moment any repo method is called. Two guards (src/server/main.ts:422 createReposForDialect and src/cli/context.ts:64 dialect check) backstop this with a fail-fast error pointing to pl-f17e step 7 (warren-480a) — that's the marker; remove them once repos are widened. Acceptance scenario 19 was skip-gated on WARREN_TEST_PG_URL which CI never sets, so the gap was never caught. See ml record build/postgres-half-shipped-r13 for full context.

title: CHANGELOG correction: v0.3.2 R-13 entry overstates Postgres support
description: The v0.3.2 entry says 'warren now supports Postgres as an opt-in backend via WARREN_DB_URL=postgres://...'. In practice, boot crashes the moment any repo method runs because repos are sqlite-coupled (.get/.run/.all/sync transactions). Either: (a) amend the v0.3.2 entry to call out the gap and point at the follow-up plan (preferred), or (b) add a v0.3.4 entry that explicitly retracts. Also: fly.toml and README mention Supabase + WARREN_DB_URL as a documented path — those need a 'not yet operational' caveat or removal until warren-2680 ships. Depends on: warren-2680 (real fix).
closeReason: Folded into warren-2680 (pl-f1be step warren-670f). v0.3.2 carries the Correction blockquote pointing at the follow-up plan; [Unreleased] has the real fix entry that closes this seed; fly.toml + README correctly mark Postgres path as 'shipped 2026-05-14' now that the dialect-polymorphic repo layer is live.

title: Add WARREN_TEST_PG_URL postgres service to CI matrix so scenario 19 actually runs
description: Acceptance scenario 19 (warren-on-postgres) is skip-gated on WARREN_TEST_PG_URL. .github/workflows/ci.yml runs only 'bun test' (no acceptance) — scenario 19 has never actually executed in CI. This is why the half-shipped R-13 (warren-2680) was not caught at merge time. Need: (1) add a postgres:16 service container to ci.yml or a parallel matrix job, (2) set WARREN_TEST_PG_URL and WARREN_TEST_DIALECT=postgres on that job, (3) run acceptance scenarios OR at minimum unit tests in pg mode, (4) ensure the dialect-aware withDb() helper picks up the env var correctly. Depends on warren-2680 (otherwise tests will still hit the guard).
closeReason: Folded into warren-627b: postgres:16 service + WARREN_TEST_DIALECT=postgres + WARREN_TEST_PG_URL wired into .github/workflows/ci.yml as a parallel job (ci-postgres). Repo tests opt in via isPostgresTestEnabled(), so 'bun test' now exercises both dialects on every PR.

title: Dogfood retry: warren on Fly + Supabase with CI auto-deploy (2026-05-14 session resumption)
description: Picks up the cloud-dogfood work paused on 2026-05-14 once pl-f1be (warren-2680) lands real Postgres support. The original session ran phases 1-3 (Fly app + Supabase project + secrets + first deploy) and discovered R-13 was half-shipped before phase 4 (CI auto-deploy) was wired. Resume from a known state: warren-deployed.fly.dev exists, machine e82656da0747d8 stopped, WARREN_DB_URL staged unset, Supabase project warren-dogfood (ref biaxgkadzaruhrqsjnmi) intact at vxnihfymkolbgmtodkyb org, .env.deployed retains tokens + assembled session-pooler URL with uselibpqcompat=true (see ml record build convention from same session). Scope: (1) restore WARREN_DB_URL via 'fly secrets import < .env.deployed' (or regen if rotated), run fly deploy, verify /healthz green and /readyz db_reachable.dialect=postgres against Supabase. (2) Wire auto-deploy: extend .github/workflows/release.yml with a 'deploy' job depending on the existing release job — runs flyctl deploy on tag push. Requires FLY_API_TOKEN GitHub secret (create via 'flyctl tokens create deploy -a warren-deployed' then 'gh secret set FLY_API_TOKEN'). Use superfly/flyctl-actions/setup-flyctl@master. (3) Validate end-to-end: log into UI with WARREN_API_TOKEN, register warren itself + key os-eco siblings as projects, dispatch a test run, verify event stream + PR open + reap. (4) Document the path in README operator notes + retire the 'org-scale path' caveat once green. Blocked-by warren-2680 because Fly will crash-loop again the moment any repo method runs against Supabase until pl-f1be ships.

title: Enable RLS on Supabase Postgres tables (warren-deployed dogfood cleanup)
Dogfood: per-run preview sidecar on jayminwest.com via warren-deployed
R-XX: path-based preview routing for hosts without wildcard DNS
Bake pnpm + npm into warren Dockerfile (preview sidecar dep)
Port preview port-allocator + RunPreviewsRepo to dialect-polymorphic adapter (R-13 follow-up; blocks pg dogfood preview)
description:
## Problem

Warren-deployed runs on Supabase Postgres. Dispatching a run against any project with `.warren/preview.yaml` (e.g. jayminwest.com under prj_sfhnyyjxf7e4) reaches terminal `state:succeeded`, but reap silently skips `preview_launch` and `previewState` stays `null`. Verified end-to-end against warren-deployed on 2026-05-14 with run `run_0ef4bk1j58ks` — `reap.completed` event shows `previewState:null, previewPort:null, previewUrl:null` with no preview events.

## Root cause

`src/server/main.ts:160-161`:

```ts
const portAllocator =
    db.dialect === "sqlite" ? new PreviewPortAllocator(db, previewPortRange) : undefined;
```

Allocator is only constructed for sqlite. On pg, `portAllocator` is undefined, so `resolveProjectPreviewConfig` (`src/server/bridges.ts:358`) returns undefined, and the reap launch gate (`src/runs/reap.ts:534-540`) skips the sidecar spawn.

The skip is silent — no event, no doctor warning. The R-13 plan deferred this explicitly at `src/preview/port-allocator.ts:18-24` (\"when the repo layer becomes dialect-aware (pl-f17e follow-up) the pg branch will swap the lock primitive for SELECT ... FOR UPDATE\"). pl-f17e and pl-f1be both closed without porting the allocator.

## Scope

Two sqlite-coupled modules need the same treatment R-13 step 1 gave the seven repos:

1. **`src/preview/port-allocator.ts`** — `PreviewPortAllocator`
   - Takes `WarrenDb` (sqlite-only union); should take `DrizzleAdapter`
   - `db.drizzle.transaction(fn, { behavior: 'immediate' })` — port to `runInTransaction(adapter, fn)`
   - Sync `.get()` / `.all()` / `.run()` → adapter's `pickOne` / `pickAll` / `runWrite`
   - PG locking: swap `BEGIN IMMEDIATE` for `SELECT ... FOR UPDATE` on the candidate run row inside the transaction, OR a pg advisory lock keyed on the range. Allocation conflict story is the candidate row, not the range.
   - Add the missing observability: when `portAllocator` ends up undefined (e.g. dialect-skip), emit `reap_failed` with reason `port_allocator_unavailable` instead of silently no-op, so future deploys notice.

2. **`src/preview/eviction.ts`** — `RunPreviewsRepo` (per mulch `mx-6b6db0`: \"two BEGIN IMMEDIATE CAS methods that share serialization\")
   - Same adapter migration
   - PG: `SELECT ... FOR UPDATE` or `UPDATE ... WHERE ... RETURNING` for the CAS

3. **Server wiring** (`src/server/main.ts:160-161`): construct the allocator unconditionally once it's dialect-aware. Delete the `db.dialect === 'sqlite'` ternary.

4. **Tests**: extend the existing dialect-polymorphic test pattern (mx-1d9f7a) to both modules. Acceptance scenario `20-preview.ts` should run against pg too (per `mx-1d31f0` it currently skips macOS; the pg run is Linux-only via the `WARREN_TEST_PG_URL` substrate from mx-c1cd3a).

## Mulch context

- `mx-9391c9` — allocator decision (runs table is sole source of truth)
- `mx-6b6db0` — RunPreviewsRepo CAS pattern
- `mx-1d9f7a` — `dialect-polymorphic-repo` pattern + DrizzleAdapter usage
- `mx-d5cf19` — async sqlite transaction via raw BEGIN/COMMIT exec (for the pg vs sqlite tx parity)

## Verification

- Acceptance scenario `20-preview.ts` passes on pg
- Manual: dispatch a run on jayminwest.com (`prj_sfhnyyjxf7e4`) via warren-deployed; `reap.completed` reports `previewState:live` and `preview_launched` event fires with the allocated port

## Closes

Unblocks `warren-724e` (per-run preview sidecar dogfood on jayminwest.com).

title: R-19 follow-up: path-based preview mode (default for zero-domain operators)
description:
## Motivation

R-19 previews ship today on a wildcard-subdomain contract (`run-<id>.<warren-host>`) that requires the operator to own a domain, point a wildcard CNAME at warren, and provision a wildcard TLS cert via DNS-01. For an operator who just `fly deploy`s warren and wants to try the feature, that's a closed door — previews-as-selling-point only land for operators who already self-host other webapps.

Path-based mode is the only option that adds **zero** DNS/cert work for the common self-hoster case. It reuses the single hostname + cert the operator already has for the warren UI itself.

## Design sketch

- New env var `WARREN_PREVIEW_MODE=path|subdomain` (default `path`). Subdomain mode stays as the production/multi-tenant opt-in.
- URL shape in path mode: `https://<warren-host>/p/<run-id>/...`
- Proxy preamble (`src/preview/proxy.ts`, mx-787718) gets a sibling match: path-prefix `^/p/(?<runId>[a-z0-9-]+)(/.*)?$` resolves `runs.preview_port → 127.0.0.1:<port>` and forwards. Host-match path stays for subdomain mode.
- `runs.preview_last_hit_at` debounce (mx-411a6f) lifts unchanged.
- HTML response rewriting (the only real complexity):
  - Inject `<base href="/p/<run-id>/">` into `<head>` on `Content-Type: text/html` responses so root-relative asset URLs (`/assets/foo.js`) resolve correctly.
  - Rewrite `Location:` headers on 3xx that start with `/` → `/p/<run-id>/...` so dev-server redirects don't escape the prefix.
  - JSON/JS/CSS responses pass through untouched.
- Signed-cookie auth (`src/preview/cookie.ts`, mx-c38965): in path mode the cookie scope drops from `Domain=.<warren-host>` to `Path=/p/<run-id>/` so one signed cookie still authorizes one run. Login route gains a path-mode variant: `GET /p/<run-id>/__warren/login?token=…` (or keep `/runs/:id/preview/login` and redirect to `/p/<run-id>/`).
- PR annotation (`src/runs/pr-annotate.ts`, mx-ba79c4): emits `https://<warren-host>/p/<run-id>/` instead of `https://run-<id>.<warren-host>` when mode=path. `<!-- warren:preview-* -->` markers unchanged.
- Doctor check: in path mode, the `WARREN_PREVIEW_HOST` + placeholder-`WARREN_API_TOKEN` warning still applies; subdomain-only checks become mode-gated.
- Acceptance: scenario `20-preview.ts` clones into a `mode=path` variant.

## Trade-offs

- Apps with hardcoded absolute URLs (`<a href=\"https://example.com/foo\">`) work fine; apps with hardcoded path URLs that aren't root-relative are unaffected. Apps that compute URLs from `window.location.host` and assume host == app may need a `--base` flag passed at preview command time (already operator-configurable via `.warren/preview.yaml` command).
- Some SPA dev servers (Vite, Next, CRA) honor `<base href>` correctly; others need explicit base-path config. Document the known-good shapes; ship `<base>` injection as best-effort.
- Path mode is single-tenant per warren host. Subdomain mode remains the right answer for org-scale previews.

## Where this lands

R-19 sibling follow-up under plan pl-2c59 (warren-1bcb). Roughly 6-8 plan steps:
1. SPEC §11.L addendum + design lock (`WARREN_PREVIEW_MODE`, URL contract, cookie scope, HTML rewrite contract)
2. `PreviewModeSchema` + env loader + `.warren/preview.yaml` field
3. Proxy preamble path-match branch (`src/preview/proxy.ts`)
4. HTML `<base>` injection + `Location:` header rewrite middleware
5. Signed-cookie path-scope variant (`src/preview/cookie.ts`)
6. PR annotation URL shape branch (`src/runs/pr-annotate.ts`)
7. UI: RunDetail badge URL + teardown affordance honor mode
8. Acceptance scenario variant (`scripts/acceptance/scenarios/20-preview-path.ts`)

## Unblocks

- Self-hoster `fly deploy → fly secrets set ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=… → previews work` quickstart story.
- Removes the domain-purchase requirement from warren-724e (dogfood-on-warren-deployed) — that seed can fire once path mode lands, no jayminwest.com or other domain needed.

## References

- Related plan: pl-2c59 (R-19 root)
- Related seed: warren-724e (dogfood blocked on domain requirement)
- ROADMAP entry: R-19 — Per-run preview environments
- SPEC: §11.L (preview design lock)
- Mulch records: mx-787718 (proxy preamble), mx-411a6f (last_hit_at debounce), mx-c38965 (cookie auth), mx-ba79c4 (PR annotation), mx-062a6d (env loader)

<!-- seeds:plan-backref:end -->

title: Plan-run mode: dispatch sd plan as serial sequence of warren runs
description:
Add a "plan run" mode to warren so an operator can hand it an `sd plan` id and walk away. Warren picks the next-ready child via `sd ready --plan <id>`, dispatches a normal warren run with prompt "work on sd <id>", waits for the PR to land, then loops. Same agent/model/project config across the whole sequence.

## Motivation

Today the workflow is: plan locally with `sd plan`, push, then run `scripts/run-plan.sh pl-xxx` on a workstation that has to stay awake. Warren is the natural home for the "walk away" half — it already owns ephemeral runs, PR annotation, eviction. This closes the local-machine dependency for plan execution.

## Scope (V1: serial only)

- New `PlanRun` entity owning an ordered list of child run ids + plan id + dispatch config (agent, model, project, branch base).
- Coordinator: pick next-ready child via `sd ready --plan <id>`, dispatch one warren run, wait for its PR to merge, repeat. Stop on first failure (matches `run-plan.sh`). Idempotent on re-run: closed children are skipped.
- Each child run branches off the latest main after the prior child's PR merges — so children build on each other even when `blockedBy` would theoretically allow parallelism. Sequential composition is the feature, not a limitation.
- UI: PlanRun detail page lists children with live status (queued / running / pr-open / merged / failed) and links to the underlying run pages.
- Dispatch UI: project picker → agent picker → model picker → "plan id" input → go.

## Gating

Surface only when the seeds feature is active (`.seeds/` present in the project), same opt-in shape as the existing mulch/seeds integrations. Don't tie warren's core run primitive to seeds — the coordinator is a feature on top.

## Explicitly out of scope (file as follow-ups)

- Parallel / DAG-aware fan-out (`blockedBy` graph → stacked PRs). Sequential-with-merge-between is the V1 contract; parallel siblings cause merge messes and pull in stacked-PR tooling.
- Resuming a partially-run plan from a specific child (manual: just close completed children and re-dispatch).
- Cron-triggered plan runs (`.warren/triggers.yaml` integration).

## Relationship to Plot (warren-000b)

PlanRun is the natural execution mechanism for a Plot that has reached `active` status. The Plot adoption epic (warren-000b) tracks the broader coordination/UI shift; PlanRun is the dispatch primitive it composes on top of. See `../plot/SPEC.md` for the Plot data model.

Composition (post warren-000b lands, and post `@os-eco/plot` v1):
- A Plot reaches `ready` status with an attached `sd plan` (the post-V1 planner-agent flow on Plot status transition)
- User clicks "run plan" on the Plot detail page; warren launches a PlanRun against the attached plan
- Each child run's sandbox has `PLOT_ID` set; child-run events (`decision_made`, `question_posed`, `artifact_produced`) stream back into the originating Plot's event log
- Plot status auto-transitions to `done` when all children close

V1 of PlanRun (this seed) does **not** require Plot to exist — it works with a plain plan id input from the dispatch UI. The Plot integration is purely additive: a PlanRun grows an optional `plot_id` field, the dispatcher reads/writes Plot events when set, and a "run plan" button appears on Plot detail pages. None of that blocks shipping serial plan execution standalone.

## Acceptance

- Operator can dispatch pl-xxx from the UI with one agent/model/project selection and walk away; warren executes children serially, each PR merging before the next starts.
- Failures stop the sequence and surface in the PlanRun view; re-dispatching the same plan id resumes from the next open child.
- Existing single-run dispatch path is unchanged; PlanRun is purely additive.
- Available only when `.seeds/` is present in the selected project.

title: Show warren version number next to 'warren' in UI sidebar
description:
The sidebar header currently shows just the warren logo and 'warren' text. Add the version number (e.g. 'v0.3.6') alongside the name so users can see at a glance which version is running.

The version is already exported as VERSION from src/index.ts and surfaced via the /v1/version endpoint, so the UI can either import it directly (if bundled) or fetch it on mount. Style as a subtle muted tag next to 'warren' in the sidebar header.

title: Guard against agent edits to .github/workflows/ that PAT can't push
description:
Background
----------
Run `run_hpyz0yd73vzc` failed at `branch_push` because the agent committed a
change to `.github/workflows/ci.yml` and the operator's `GITHUB_TOKEN` PAT
lacked the `workflow` scope. GitHub rejected the ref update server-side:

  ! [remote rejected] HEAD -> burrow/run_hpyz0yd73vzc
    (refusing to allow a Personal Access Token to create or update workflow
     `.github/workflows/ci.yml` without `workflow` scope)

The operator fixed their PAT (added `workflow` scope), but the failure mode
will recur on any host whose token lacks it — and the agent has no way to
know its commit will be unpushable until reap tries to push.

Proposed fix (two-layer)
------------------------
1. Prompt-level guardrail. Add a line to the built-in agent system prompts
   in `src/registry/builtins/{claude-code,sapling,pi}.ts` telling agents not
   to modify files under `.github/workflows/` unless the user has explicitly
   asked them to. This is the cheap part.

2. Pre-push detection in reap. In `src/runs/reap.ts`, before
   `git push origin HEAD:<branch>`, diff the workspace against the base
   branch and look for changes under `.github/workflows/`. If any are
   present AND `GITHUB_TOKEN` is set, emit a `reap.workflow_change_warning`
   event so the operator sees the cause in the run timeline without having
   to grep stderr. (Don't fail pre-emptively — a properly-scoped PAT *can*
   push these; we just want clearer surface area when the push fails.)

Out of scope
------------
GitHub App auth (SPEC R-18, post-V1) replaces PATs entirely and lets us
grant Workflows: write at install time — that's the real fix. This seed is
the V1 stopgap.

Evidence
--------
- run id: run_hpyz0yd73vzc
- reap code: src/runs/reap.ts:25, ~:435 (branch_push step)
- token wiring: src/supervisor/git-credentials.ts:65
- prompts to update: src/registry/builtins/{claude-code,sapling,pi}.ts

title: Cost tracking for claude-code runtime — parallel to pi (warren-a7dc / warren-17a4)
description:
Wire claude-code into the same cost/token columns pi populates (`runs.cost_usd`, `runs.tokens_*`), so the existing RunsRepo.attachStats + RunDetail cost badge + Runs cost column surfaces light up for claude-code runs instead of staying null.

## Background

Pi runs already populate cost/token columns via `accumulatePiUsage` in `src/runs/stream.ts:553` — it reads pi's inline `turn_end` envelopes (`message.usage.cost.total` + token counts) and persists the delta on terminal via `RunsRepo.attachStats`. See warren-a7dc (in-stream + out-of-band PiStatsClient paths) and warren-17a4 (scenario 16 assertion).

Claude-code emits the equivalent at run end: a single `{"type":"result", "subtype":"success", "total_cost_usd": <n>, "usage": {input_tokens, output_tokens, cache_read_input_tokens, cache_creation_input_tokens, ...}}` envelope. Burrow's `jsonl-claude.ts` already maps this to `kind: state_change`, `stream: system` (see `../burrow/src/runtime/parsers/jsonl-claude.ts:73`), and warren's `detectRuntimeTerminal` already keys off it as the claude-code terminal. The cost/token fields are sitting in the payload — we just don't read them.

## Scope

1. Add a sibling extractor next to `accumulatePiUsage` (e.g. `extractClaudeUsage`) that pulls `total_cost_usd` + the `usage.*` token counts off a claude-code `result` envelope. Single-shot (no accumulation needed — claude-code emits the cumulative total once).
2. Plumb it into the bridge loop in `src/runs/stream.ts` so on terminal it calls `repos.runs.attachStats` with the same shape pi uses. Same best-effort posture (try/catch, log, columns stay null on failure).
3. Decide whether to dispatch by event shape (sniff the payload) or by `runs.agent_name` — pi went the shape-sniff route; consistency argues for the same here.
4. Tests: unit test for the extractor against a fixture `result` envelope; bridge test confirming attachStats is called once with the right values; existing pi tests must still pass unchanged.
5. Acceptance: extend scenario 16 (or add scenario 17) to assert non-null `cost_usd`/`tokens_input`/`tokens_output` after a real claude-code run — mirror what warren-17a4 did for pi.
6. Sapling runs stay null for now (no terminal cost emission) — document the gap in `SPEC.md` if §pi already lists pi-only.

## Files to touch (likely)

- `src/runs/stream.ts` — new extractor + dispatch
- `src/runs/stream.test.ts` — unit + bridge tests
- `scripts/acceptance/scenarios/16-pi-parity-smoke.ts` or new `17-*-claude-code-cost.ts`
- `scripts/acceptance/lib/fixtures.ts` if a claude-code `result` fixture is needed
- `SPEC.md` §pi (or equivalent) to broaden the cost-tracking statement beyond pi-only

## Acceptance criteria

- Claude-code run finishes → `runs.cost_usd`, `runs.tokens_input`, `runs.tokens_output`, `runs.tokens_cache_read`, `runs.tokens_cache_write` are all non-null.
- Run cost badge (warren-a7ec UI surface) renders for claude-code runs the same way it does for pi.
- All three quality gates pass (`bun test && bun run lint && bun run typecheck`).
- Acceptance scenario asserts the cost columns are non-null after a claude-code run.

## Out of scope

- Sapling cost tracking (separate seed once sapling emits terminal cost).
- Re-pricing tokens from a local pricing table — same plan-alternative #5 rejection as pi; we trust the runtime's reported `total_cost_usd`.
- UI changes beyond what already exists for pi.

title: Preview sidecar runs Node projects on Bun (node-shim for pi compat) — crashes on missing built-ins
description:
## Problem

Per-run preview sidecar crashes on startup for any Node-targeted JS project whose
dep tree references built-ins Bun doesn't ship. Verified end-to-end on warren-deployed
2026-05-15 with run `run_jegavdzfbrpc` against `prj_sfhnyyjxf7e4` (jayminwest.com,
Next.js 14.2.24). The `previewFailureMessage` captured by `captureFailureTail`:

```
readiness probe did not return 2xx within 60000ms (probed http://127.0.0.1:30000/)

error: No such built-in module: node:sqlite

Bun v1.2.23 (Linux x64 baseline)
```

## Root cause

`Dockerfile:89` (warren-810f / pi compat):

```dockerfile
RUN ln -s /usr/local/bun-node-fallback-bin/node /usr/local/bin/node
```

Comment block at lines 53-56 spells out the design: pnpm + npm are baked in so
preview sidecars can boot `pnpm dev` / `npm run dev` for non-bun projects, but
**they share the same `/usr/local/bin/node` bun-shim symlink installed for pi
compat**. So:

```
sh -c "pnpm install --frozen-lockfile && pnpm dev"
  → node_modules/.bin/next   (shell stub, #!/bin/sh, exec node next.js)
  → /usr/local/bin/node      ← bun-shim symlink
  → bun loads Next.js
  → Next.js requires("node:sqlite")  ← Bun v1.2.23 has no built-in
  → process exits
  → 60s readiness probe times out, `previewState:failed`
```

Every "real Node" project under warren runs on Bun, which has differing built-in
module coverage. node:sqlite is the trigger this round; any other missing built-in
(node:test, node:wasi corners, etc) will hit the next project.

## Why this wasn't caught earlier

- Acceptance scenario `20-preview*.ts` uses `python3 -m http.server` (mx-87d34c) —
  no Node runtime in the loop, so the symlink never matters.
- warren-810f baked pnpm/npm into the image but reused the existing pi-compat
  node-shim instead of installing real Node. The decision predated the dogfood.
- jayminwest.com's `.warren/preview.yaml` comment notes pnpm install persistence
  is unsolved (warren-810f reference) but doesn't surface the Bun runtime quirk.

## Scope of fix

Two real options:

1. **Install real Node alongside Bun** (recommended). Add `nodejs` to the apt-get
   layer in `Dockerfile:36-43` (or use NodeSource for a recent LTS). Move the
   pi-compat shim to a non-PATH location, then either:
   - Patch pi's shebang at install time to `#!/usr/bin/env bun`, or
   - Keep the symlink but put it somewhere only pi looks for it.

   Trade-off: image grows by Node runtime (~50MB). pi compat stays intact.

2. **Bump Bun** (whack-a-mole). Bun v1.2.42+ added `node:sqlite`. Bumping
   `oven/bun:1.2` → a newer tag would paper over *this* error but leaves the
   architectural mismatch in place — any Node API Bun lags on resurfaces this
   class of bug.

Option 1 closes the door; option 2 is a band-aid.

## Verification

- `pnpm dev` / `npm run dev` on a Next.js / Remix / Vite project succeeds inside
  the burrow sandbox (`node` is real Node, not bun-shim).
- Pi runs continue to work (regression check; pi shebang resolution unchanged).
- A new acceptance scenario (or extension of `20-preview*.ts`) exercises an
  actual Node dev server, not python http.server.

## Mulch context

- mx-87d34c — acceptance fixture pattern (python3 -m http.server)
- warren-810f (closed) — pnpm + npm bake-in decision; this is its blind spot.

## References

- Dockerfile:53-56 (design comment)
- Dockerfile:89 (the symlink)
- src/preview/launch.ts:115 (DEFAULT_READINESS_TIMEOUT_MS=60s — incidental, not
  the cause here; the dev server crashed instantly, didn't need 60s)
- Failed run: warren-deployed run_jegavdzfbrpc, 2026-05-15T03:38:23Z–03:40:44Z
- API: `GET /runs/run_jegavdzfbrpc` includes the full `previewFailureMessage`

## Blocks

- warren-724e (dogfood per-run preview sidecar on jayminwest.com via warren-deployed)

title: UI: light/dark mode toggle above Log out button
description:
Add an explicit theme toggle to the sidebar in `src/ui/src/components/Layout.tsx`, placed directly above the existing "Log out" Button (line 76). Today the UI follows `@media (prefers-color-scheme: dark)` only (see `src/ui/src/index.css:24`), so users can't override their OS preference from within warren.

## Goal

User can click a single control in the sidebar to cycle/select between **Light**, **Dark**, and **System** modes. Choice persists across reloads. With no override stored, behavior is identical to today (follows OS).

## Scope

### 1. CSS refactor — `src/ui/src/index.css`

Move the dark-mode token block out of `@media (prefers-color-scheme: dark)` so it can be triggered by either the media query *or* an explicit class/attribute on `<html>`. Two acceptable shapes:

- `:root[data-theme="dark"] { … }` plus `@media (prefers-color-scheme: dark) { :root:not([data-theme]) { … } }`, **or**
- Tailwind v4 `@custom-variant dark` driven by `data-theme="dark"` on `<html>`, with the system fallback gated by absence of the attribute.

Pick whichever cleanly preserves the current default (no attribute → follows OS). Keep all token names identical — do not rename `--color-*` variables.

### 2. Theme hook — new `src/ui/src/hooks/useTheme.ts`

- State: `"light" | "dark" | "system"`, default `"system"`.
- Persistence: `localStorage` key `warren.theme`. Read once on init; ignore + clear on invalid values.
- Side effect: writes `data-theme` on `document.documentElement` (`"light"`, `"dark"`, or removes the attribute for `"system"`).
- Listens for `prefers-color-scheme` changes only when mode is `"system"` (no-op when explicit) — needed so the effective theme stays correct if the OS flips while warren is open.
- Exposes `{ theme, setTheme, resolvedTheme }` where `resolvedTheme` is `"light"|"dark"` after collapsing `"system"`.
- SSR-safe guards aren't required (warren UI is client-only Vite), but don't crash if `window`/`localStorage` is missing.

### 3. UI control — `src/ui/src/components/ThemeToggle.tsx`

- Reuses the existing `Button` (`variant="ghost"`, `size="sm"`, `className="mt-2 justify-start"`) so it visually matches the Log out button.
- Icon from `lucide-react` (already imported in Layout): `Sun` for light, `Moon` for dark, `Monitor` (or `Laptop`) for system. Show the icon for the *current* selection.
- Label text mirrors the Log out row: "Light" / "Dark" / "System".
- Clicking cycles `light → dark → system → light`. Acceptable alternative: a small dropdown (shadcn-style) with the three options — only do this if a dropdown component already exists in `src/ui/src/components/ui/`; otherwise stick with click-to-cycle to avoid pulling in a new primitive.
- `aria-label` reflects the current mode (e.g. `"Theme: dark. Click to switch to system."`).

### 4. Wire-up — `src/ui/src/components/Layout.tsx`

Insert `<ThemeToggle />` immediately above the existing `<Button … onClick={handleLogout} …>` on line 76, inside the same `<aside>` flex column. Do not change any other layout. Keep both controls visually aligned (same padding, same icon size `h-4 w-4`).

## Out of scope

- No new theming primitives (next-themes, etc.). Roll the hook by hand — it's ~30 lines.
- No theme-aware logo / illustration swaps.
- No per-page overrides.
- No telemetry / event reporting on toggle.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] With `localStorage.warren.theme` absent, the app renders identically to today (light in light OS, dark in dark OS), with no `data-theme` attribute on `<html>`.
- [ ] Selecting Light forces light tokens regardless of OS; selecting Dark forces dark; selecting System removes the override and re-follows OS.
- [ ] Reloading the page preserves the explicit selection.
- [ ] Flipping the OS theme while warren is open updates the UI **only** when mode is System.
- [ ] No FOUC on initial load: the `data-theme` attribute (or its absence) is set before React paints. If needed, inline a tiny `<script>` in `index.html` that reads `localStorage.warren.theme` and sets `documentElement.dataset.theme` synchronously — keep it under 10 lines and free of dependencies.
- [ ] `bun test && bun run lint && bun run typecheck` clean.
- [ ] Manual check in `bun run ui:dev`: toggle cycles through all three states, icon and label update, sidebar layout unchanged.

## Files touched (expected)

- `src/ui/src/index.css` — refactor dark-mode selector
- `src/ui/src/hooks/useTheme.ts` — new
- `src/ui/src/components/ThemeToggle.tsx` — new
- `src/ui/src/components/Layout.tsx` — insert toggle above Log out
- `src/ui/index.html` — optional FOUC-prevention inline script

## Notes

- The Tailwind v4 `@theme` block in `index.css` is tokens-as-CSS-vars, not Tailwind config. The override block must produce the *same* CSS variables on `:root` (or `html[data-theme="dark"]`) — keep the `oklch(...)` values unchanged.
- `localStorage` key is namespaced `warren.theme` to avoid collisions if warren is ever embedded.

title: auto-merge.yml uses GITHUB_TOKEN, so PR merges don't trigger Release/CI on main
description:
PR #12 (warren-d340 theme toggle) bumped 0.3.7 -> 0.3.8 in package.json + CHANGELOG and was auto-merged, but no workflow runs fired for the merge commit (b1b1183). Result: no v0.3.8 tag, no GitHub release, no Fly deploy.

Root cause: .github/workflows/auto-merge.yml:26 uses secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN to call 'gh pr merge --auto --squash'. GitHub deliberately suppresses workflow triggers for push events authored by GITHUB_TOKEN to prevent recursive loops. Every prior release that worked (0.3.7, 0.3.6, ...) was a direct push to main, not a PR merge.

Symptom check: gh api repos/jayminwest/warren/actions/runs returns zero runs for the merge SHA.

Fix options:
- Replace secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN in auto-merge.yml with a PAT stored as a repo secret (e.g. AUTO_MERGE_PAT) with repo + workflow scopes. PAT-authored pushes trigger downstream workflows.
- Or use actions/create-github-app-token with a GitHub App installed on the repo.

Both burrow and overstory likely have the same workflow file — check and fix consistently across the ecosystem.

Acceptance: open a throwaway PR that bumps a paths-watched file, let auto-merge land it, confirm both CI and Release fire on the resulting main commit.

title: Add acceptance scenario exercising a real Node dev server (post-warren-a82b verification)
Add Warren logo to tab icon (favicon) and sidebar header
description:
Branding pass: wire the Warren logo into two spots in the UI. ## What ships today - **Favicon:** `src/ui/index.html` has no `` at all — only `warren` and the theme bootstrap script (lines 1-25). The browser shows the default blank/Vite favicon. - **Sidebar header:** `src/ui/src/components/Layout.tsx` lines 35-42 render: ```jsx
warren {version.data ? ( v{version.data.version} ) : null}
``` The placeholder is the `Boxes` lucide-react icon — that's what to replace. ## Assets already in the repo `branding/` contains: - `icon.png` (~256×256, geometric network nodes on dark bg) - `logo.png`, `logo@2x.png` (high-res variants) - `generate-logo.py` (regen script) None are wired into the UI yet. Consider exporting an SVG (or a transparent-bg PNG) so it composites on both themes. ## Theme strategy (don't break #12) The theme toggle uses `data-theme="dark"` on `` plus CSS variables defined in `src/ui/src/index.css` (lines 3-22 light, 34-47 dark, 49-64 `prefers-color-scheme` fallback). The icon must be legible against both `--color-muted`/40 (sidebar bg in light) and the dark counterpart. Options: - SVG using `currentColor` — adapts automatically. - Two PNG variants swapped via CSS `[data-theme=dark] img.logo-dark` selectors. - A single image with sufficient contrast on both backgrounds (acceptable if the mark is monochrome-friendly). ## Implementation outline 1. **Add a public dir for static assets:** `src/ui/public/` doesn't exist yet. Vite will copy its contents to `dist/` root automatically (no config change needed; `vite.config.ts` already outputs to `src/ui/dist/`). 2. **Favicon:** drop `favicon.svg` (or `.png` + `.ico`) into `src/ui/public/` and add `` to `src/ui/index.html` head. 3. **Sidebar logo:** import the SVG as a React component (or ``), replace the `` on Layout.tsx:36, keep the `h-5 w-5 self-center` sizing so the version chip stays aligned with the baseline. 4. Verify in both light and dark themes using the sidebar toggle. ## Out of scope - No backend / API changes. - Don't touch the `branding/generate-logo.py` script — just consume its output. - No restyling of the sidebar beyond swapping the icon.

title: Sort runs list by cost
description:
Add cost as a sortable column in the runs list so users can spot expensive runs at a glance. Currently the list has no sortable headers at all — only filters.

## What ships today

### UI — `src/ui/src/pages/Runs.tsx`
- React table built with shadcn `Table`/`TableHeader`/`TableHead` (lines 150-200).
- **Headers are static text, not clickable** — no sort affordance anywhere on the page.
- Columns: State, ID, Agent, Project, Started, Cost (Cost is toggle-hidden via localStorage `warren.runsList.showCostColumn`, line 24).
- Cost cell renders at lines 188-196, right-aligned monospace; null displays as `—` (line 193).
- A "total: $X.XX" badge sums non-null `costUsd` in-memory (lines 63-73).
- Cost formatting helper lives in `RunDetail.tsx:622-627` (`formatCostUsd()`): sub-$1 → three decimals, ≥$1 → two decimals.

### Server — `src/server/handlers.ts`
- `listRunsHandler()` at lines 615-630 accepts only `?project=<id>` and `?agent=<name>` filters. **No sort params today.**
- Routes to `repos.runs.listAll()` / `listByProject()` / `listByAgent()` based on query.

### Query layer — `src/db/repos/runs.ts` (lines 137-167)
All three list methods hard-code:
```ts
.orderBy(desc(this.runs.startedAt), asc(this.runs.id))
```
Drizzle + bun:sqlite. The `runsProjectStarted` index (line 117) covers `startedAt DESC` — no covering index exists for `cost_usd` yet, but the table is small enough that a scan is acceptable to start.

### Type — `src/db/schema/sqlite.ts` line 96
`costUsd: real("cost_usd")` — nullable. UI type `RunRow.costUsd: number | null` in `src/ui/src/api/types.ts` (lines 52-103).

## Why null cost is common
- Pi runtime populates `costUsd` from session-stats deltas (warren-a7dc).
- Non-pi runtimes leave it `NULL`.
- In-progress / failed runs may be `NULL` if stats RPC never ran.

So a meaningful chunk of runs have no cost — null handling is not a corner case.

## Suggested implementation

1. **Server:**
   - Add `?sort=cost|started` (default `started`) and `?dir=asc|desc` (default `desc`) to `listRunsHandler` (handlers.ts:615).
   - Thread through `listAll` / `listByProject` / `listByAgent` (runs.ts:137-167); build the `orderBy()` chain conditionally, always keeping `asc(this.runs.id)` as the stable tiebreaker.
   - For `sort=cost`: SQLite sorts NULLs first on ASC, last on DESC by default — explicitly `NULLS LAST` in both directions so unbilled runs always sink to the bottom (predictable, matches the "spot expensive runs" goal).

2. **Client:**
   - Extend `runsApi.list()` in `src/ui/src/api/client.ts` (~line 159) to take `{ sort, dir }`.
   - Make the table headers clickable in Runs.tsx:150-200; render a chevron when active. State can live in `useState` and feed the query — no need for URL persistence in V1 unless trivial.
   - Don't break the existing cost-column toggle (localStorage key `warren.runsList.showCostColumn`).

3. **Tests:** repo-level test for `listAll({ sort: 'cost' })` ordering with mixed null/non-null values; handler test for the query param parsing.

## Out of scope
- Sorting on other columns (agent, project, state) — single-column scope keeps the diff small. File a follow-up if useful.
- Changes to how `costUsd` is calculated, stored, or formatted.
- No new indexes unless EXPLAIN shows a problem on realistic data.

title: preview: 60s readiness timeout too tight for cold-cache pnpm installs
description:
## Symptom

Run `run_wzjysp61kj72` (jayminwest.com, deployed instance) failed preview with `readiness_timeout` after exactly 60.15s. Stderr tail at deadline showed pnpm still mid-download: `resolved 850, reused 0, downloaded 139, added 47`. The dev server never started.

## Root cause

`DEFAULT_READINESS_TIMEOUT_MS = 60_000` in `src/preview/launch.ts:115` is hardcoded. The timeout *is* overridable (`readinessTimeoutMs` on `LaunchPreviewInput`), but `src/runs/reap.ts:551` doesn't pass it — there's no plumb-through from `.warren/preview.yaml` to the launcher. The duration parser already exists (`src/preview/duration.ts`).

The 60s window covers install + build + dev-server bind, which is hopeless on a cold pnpm store for projects with ~850 deps.

## Plan

Plumb a per-project `readiness_timeout` through config, and raise the default from 60s to 5m so the common case (cold install) succeeds without operator intervention. The probe returns on first 2xx, so a larger ceiling only delays failure reporting — it doesn't slow happy-path startup.

1. **Schema.** Add `readiness_timeout?: string` to `ServerPreviewConfigSchema` in `src/warren-config/schema.ts`. Reuse the duration grammar from `src/preview/duration.ts` (same one `idle_ttl` / `max_lifetime` use). Validate at load time; reject `<1s` and `>1h`.
2. **Plumb.** In `src/runs/reap.ts:551`, parse the config field (when set) and pass `readinessTimeoutMs` into `launchPreview`. When unset, fall through to the new default.
3. **Default.** Bump `DEFAULT_READINESS_TIMEOUT_MS` in `src/preview/launch.ts:115` from `60_000` to `300_000` (5m).
4. **Tests.**
   - `src/warren-config/schema.test.ts`: accepts `"5m"`, rejects `"500ms"` and `"2h"`.
   - `src/preview/launch.test.ts`: respects injected `readinessTimeoutMs`; default is 5m.
   - `src/runs/reap.test.ts`: config value reaches the launcher.
5. **Docs.** Add the field to the documented stub in `.warren/preview.yaml` with the new default called out.

## Out of scope (file separately if it becomes a priority)

Pre-warming pnpm/npm/yarn stores in the burrow image or a shared volume. That's an operator-side optimization with its own design surface (cache locality, invalidation, package manager selection) and shouldn't gate the fix for this run.

## Repro

- Run `pi` on https://github.com/jayminwest/jayminwest.com from the deployed warren
- Project's `.warren/preview.yaml` runs a pnpm install + dev server
- First run on a fresh burrow → readiness probe times out at 60s

## References

- run id: `run_wzjysp61kj72`
- PR: https://github.com/jayminwest/jayminwest.com/pull/14
- launcher: `src/preview/launch.ts:115` (constant), `:172-202` (probe loop)
- reap call site: `src/runs/reap.ts:551`
- duration parser: `src/preview/duration.ts`
- schema: `src/warren-config/schema.ts` (PreviewConfigSchema / ServerPreviewConfigSchema)

title: R-03: Per-project .canopy/ role tier
description: Add a third tier to the canopy agent registry that scans each project's local .canopy/ for agent prompts in addition to built-ins and the shared canopy library. Tier precedence: per-project > library > built-in. The renderedJson stamps a source that distinguishes 'project:<projectId>' from 'library' and 'builtin'. Spawn-time freezing of renderedAgentJson is unchanged. Unblocks R-05's roles tab UI and lets users create project-tuned roles without forking a shared canopy repo.
