title: PR-merge polling helper: extend src/runs/pr.ts with `checkPullRequestMerged({owner, repo, number, fetch, token}): Promise<CheckPrMergedResult>` where the result is a discriminated union {kind:'merged', mergedAt: string} | {kind:'open'} | {kind:'closed_unmerged'} | {kind:'missing_token'} | {kind:'http_error', status, message}; call GitHub REST `GET /repos/:owner/:repo/pulls/:number` with `Accept: application/vnd.github+json` and `Authorization: Bearer <token>` matching the existing posture in src/runs/pr.ts (line 13 'hits the GitHub REST API directly', auth via GITHUB_TOKEN per line 70); a new helper `parsePullRequestUrl(prUrl: string): {owner, repo, number} | null` regex-parses `https://github.com/<owner>/<repo>/pull/<num>` returns null on mismatch (covers GHE-hosted shapes returning a null so the coordinator treats them as 'cannot verify merge' rather than 'merged'); add src/runs/pr.test.ts cases: missing token → kind:'missing_token' with the same skip-cleanly message style as opens (line 70); 200 with merged_at non-null → kind:'merged'; 200 with merged_at null + state:'open' → kind:'open'; 200 with state:'closed' + merged_at null → kind:'closed_unmerged'; 404 → kind:'http_error'; injected fetch matches the BurrowClient seam pattern (mx-b08612). Keep the helper a pure function — caller (coordinator step 5) decides what 'open' vs 'closed_unmerged' means for plan progression (open=wait, closed_unmerged=fail the plan with reason 'pr_closed_without_merge'). Step 6 (server API) does not need this; only the coordinator does.

title: Coordinator: build src/plan-runs/ module modeled on src/triggers/ (mx-fb4a85 single-flight tick is the load-bearing pattern). Files: index.ts (public exports), errors.ts (PlanRunDispatchError), tick.ts (top-level walker), coordinator.ts (per-PlanRun decision), dispatch.ts (spawn wrapper), pr-merge.ts (thin retry wrapper over the helper from step 4 that survives transient http_error). The per-PlanRun decision in coordinator.ts is `advancePlanRun({planRun, repos, listChildren, showPlan, showSeed, checkPrMerged, spawn, now, emit}): Promise<AdvanceResult>` returning a discriminated union {kind:'dispatched', childRunId} | {kind:'waiting_for_run'} | {kind:'waiting_for_merge'} | {kind:'advanced', mergedChildSeq, dispatchedChildSeq?} | {kind:'plan_failed', failedSeq, reason} | {kind:'plan_succeeded'} | {kind:'noop', reason}; state machine: (a) if planRun.state == 'queued' → transition to 'running', set startedAt, then fall through; (b) load the lowest-seq child whose state is in ('dispatched','running','pr_open'); if present and the linked run is non-terminal → waiting_for_run; if terminal-succeeded and child.state != 'pr_open' → flip child to 'pr_open' (or 'merged' if run.prUrl is null AND reap's empty-push event fired, treated as 'trivially merged' per mx-fd8619); if 'pr_open' → call checkPrMerged(run.prUrl); merged → flip child to 'merged' with mergedAt, fall through to dispatch next; open → waiting_for_merge; closed_unmerged or http 4xx → plan_failed with reason 'pr_closed_without_merge'; if terminal-failed/cancelled → plan_failed with reason `child_${reason ?? 'failed'}`; (c) if no in-flight child, call repos.planRuns.pickNextPending; if null → plan_succeeded (transition planRuns row to 'succeeded', set endedAt); if non-null → call showSeed against the child's seedId; status='closed' → flip to 'skipped' (resume semantics, warren-fcc9 acceptance), loop to (b); status='open' → spawn via dispatch.ts which calls spawnRun with {agentName: planRun.agentName, projectId: planRun.projectId, prompt: substitute(planRun.promptTemplate, {seed_id: child.seedId}), trigger: 'plan-run', seedId: child.seedId, providerOverride, modelOverride, ref: planRun.ref ?? project.defaultBranch, metadata: {planRunId, planId, childSeq}}; on success write child.runId, child.state='dispatched', child.startedAt; on RunSpawnError → plan_failed with reason `dispatch_failed:${err.message}`. tick.ts exports `runPlanRunTick(deps): Promise<PlanRunTickResult>` that lists all active planRuns via repos.planRuns.listActive(), advances each (catch-per-PlanRun like the scheduler's runProjectTick, mx-fb4a85 risk #9, no one bad row tears down the loop), and returns the result array for diagnostics. `bootPlanRunCoordinator(deps): {stop}` returns the same single-flight wrapper shape as `bootScheduler` (src/server/scheduler.ts) — drops overlapping ticks instead of stacking; honors `WARREN_PLAN_RUN_DISABLED=1` like `WARREN_SCHEDULER_DISABLED`; default cadence is `WARREN_PLAN_RUN_TICK_MS=10000` (10s, faster than scheduler's 60s). Surface every state transition as a warren event on the most recently-dispatched child run via the existing emit/event seam (mirror the scheduler's `trigger.*` system events) — kinds: `plan_run.advanced`, `plan_run.dispatched`, `plan_run.waiting_for_merge`, `plan_run.merged`, `plan_run.failed`, `plan_run.succeeded`. Unit tests in src/plan-runs/coordinator.test.ts cover all eight state-transition branches with stub repos + stub spawn + stub checkPrMerged; tick.test.ts covers the per-PlanRun isolation (one PlanRun throwing doesn't stop another). NO direct integration with sd update --extensions on plan-run dispatch — that's the seed back-link (pl-bb70) and is already handled by spawnRun's existing updateExtensions call when seedId is set (mx-ed7fb5 / src/runs/spawn.ts lines 395–401). This step depends on the DB repo (step 2), the seeds-cli readers (step 3), and the PR-merge helper (step 4).

title: Server API surface: extend src/server/handlers.ts with five new handlers + register routes. POST /plan-runs body: {project: string, planId: string, agent: string, promptTemplate?: string, ref?: string, providerOverride?: string, modelOverride?: string, dispatcherHandle?: string}; handler order: (1) load project via repos.projects.get; 404 if missing; (2) if !project.hasSeeds → ValidationError code:'project_lacks_seeds' with recoveryHint 'add a .seeds/ directory to the project clone and refresh' (mirror plot's reject shape at warren-a8c3); (3) call showPlan(seedsCli, project.localPath, planId); plan.status must be in ('approved','active','done'); empty open-children list → ValidationError code:'plan_has_no_open_children'; (4) resolve agent via existing repos.agents lookup with the scoped fallback (mx-644fb5); (5) build the plan_runs row with state='queued' + plan_run_children rows enumerated from plan.children in order, all state='pending'; (6) persist via repos.planRuns.create; (7) return 201 with the full row + children. GET /plan-runs?project=&state= lists with the same options-bag shape as listRunsHandler (mx-f1a881); GET /plan-runs/:id returns the row with embedded children AND a `runs` array fanned out from runs.listByIds(child.runId for each non-null) so the UI's detail page renders in one round-trip; POST /plan-runs/:id/cancel sets state='cancelled' and, if there is an in-flight child, also issues cancelRun against that child (existing seam, src/runs/cancel.ts); GET /plan-runs/:id/events tails the union of every child run's events via the broker (use the existing broker.subscribe + the events repo's listByRunIds; this is read-only so don't open a writer). Add the routes to the ROUTES table in handlers.ts (lines 1370–1378) and wire planRuns into ServerDeps in src/server/types.ts. Boot wiring in src/server/main.ts: instantiate the coordinator (`bootPlanRunCoordinator`) alongside bootScheduler with the same shutdown semantics (await coordinator.stop() before bridges.shutdown). Tests: extend src/server/handlers.test.ts (or a new handlers.plan-runs.test.ts following the workers/burrows split convention) covering each handler's happy path + the `project_lacks_seeds` rejection + the `plan_has_no_open_children` rejection + a CANCEL during in-flight that calls cancelRun. Depends on step 1 (hasSeeds flag), step 2 (DB), step 3 (seeds-cli readers), step 5 (the coordinator's bootPlanRunCoordinator + listActive shape).

commit + refresh; (3) commit a real `.seeds/plans.jsonl` row pl-acc-26 with children [ah-acc-26-a, ah-acc-26-b, ah-acc-26-c] and status='approved' (mirror the schema in seeds-cli/schema.ts via a seed write rather than shelling sd plan submit so the harness stays deterministic, scenario 22 takes the same posture for issues); events stream surfaces `plan_run.advanced` + `plan_run.merged` + `plan_run.succeeded`; (8) a SECOND POST /plan-runs against the SAME plan id after closing ah-acc-26-b out of band must return 201 then immediately advance ah-acc-26-b to 'skipped' state without dispatching a child run, asserting the resume semantics (warren-fcc9 acceptance: 're-dispatching the same plan id resumes from the next open child'); title: refreshProject (git reset --hard) wipes uncommitted .plot/ host-side appends between children
description: Host-side Plot appenders write to project_clone/.plot/<id>.{events.jsonl,json} WITHOUT committing — defaultPlotAppender (src/runs/spawn.ts:597 / warren-e848), defaultPlanRunPlotAppender (src/plan-runs/plot-appender.ts / warren-b89f), and autoTransitionPlotToDone (src/plan-runs/plot-transition.ts / warren-b290) all just call PlotHandle.append/setStatus which writes the file but never commits. refreshProject (src/projects/refresh.ts:139) runs git reset --hard origin/<ref> on every spawnRun and discards all of those uncommitted edits before each child dispatches. Observed in scenario 27 (warren-97a3): only the LAST child's run_dispatched line and the post-final-child status_changed line survive in the on-disk .plot/<id>.events.jsonl; the plan_run_dispatched line and the prior children's run_dispatched lines are wiped. The .json snapshot's auto-done status='done' is similarly reverted to 'active' on the next refresh. Scenario 27 works around this by tailing the events.jsonl every 100ms to accumulate the transient writes. Production impact: an operator inspecting a project's .plot/ at rest sees only the last surviving writes, and the Plot status seen in .json is not durable across PlanRuns or any subsequent spawnRun against the same project. Fix shapes to consider: (a) commit .plot/ changes alongside the next workspace branch_push (analogous to how mulch records are merged + committed via reap), (b) skip git reset of paths under .plot/ in refreshProject when the project hasPlot, (c) store host-side Plot appender writes in an out-of-tree side-table (mirroring warren events) and rebuild .plot/ from it. Cross-cuts pl-2047 (Phase 1) and pl-7937 (Phase 2). Related: warren-7e0f (reap's plot merge), mx-239786 (per-child run_dispatched appender).
closeReason: All plan steps (pl-d4d6) closed; shape (b) snapshot/restore landed in v0.4.2 (commit 172eef5). Follow-up shape (a) commit-through-reap filed as warren-343a.

title: NewPlanRun: link to project refresh from the missing-.seeds/ error
description:
When the NewPlanRun page detects `!hasSeeds` on the selected project, the error card (src/ui/src/pages/NewPlanRun.tsx:149-158) tells the user to 'add .seeds/ and refresh the project' but offers no action. In practice the most common cause is a stale hasSeeds flag: the project was registered before the warren-9990 detection landed (or before .seeds/ was committed), and the row's has_seeds column is still false even though the on-disk clone has .seeds/. The fix is a one-click POST /projects/:id/refresh — which works today, but only if the user knows to navigate to the Projects tab and find the refresh icon.

Surface that path from the error itself. Either: (a) add a 'Refresh project' button inside the destructive card that calls projectsApi.refresh(project) and invalidates the projects query on success, or (b) link to /projects with the row highlighted. Option (a) is the more direct fix and matches the surrounding mutation pattern (see Projects.tsx:49-52).

Repro: register a project on a warren version predating 0.3.17, upgrade warren, open /plan-runs/new, select the project — error shows even though .seeds/ exists in the repo.

Discovered while investigating the warren project itself failing this gate in prod today.

title: src/plots/: server-side Plot aggregation module — typed facade over UserPlotClient.list/query/rebuildIndex across hasPlot projects, returning PlotSummary[] {id, name, status, intent_goal_preview, attachments_count, last_event_ts, last_event_actor, project_id}. Parallel per-project queries; best-effort rebuildIndex+retry once on miss (mx-239786 pattern); 5s in-memory cache keyed by project_id. PlotResolver helper resolves owning project for a given plot_id by querying every hasPlot project's index. Unit tests pin the byte-identical-empty-array contract when zero projects have hasPlot=true.

title: scripts/acceptance/scenarios/28-plot-list-and-create.ts: in-proc warren-stack scenario mirroring scenarios 25 + 27 (mx-af2627, mx-15e4da). title: POST /plots/:id/intent in src/server/handlers.ts: accepts {goal?, non_goals?, constraints?, success_criteria?, dispatcher_handle?}; resolves the Plot via PlotResolver, opens a UserPlotClient (dispatcher_handle → user:<handle> actor with operator fallback), calls handle.editIntent. Reject with typed error when current status is done or archived (SPEC §6 — intent is frozen at done). The facade's compile-time ACL guard ensures agent-actor mistakes are unreachable from this code path (mx-bd4d67). Fire-and-log is NOT applied here — intent edits are user-driven and the user is waiting on the result; surface failures synchronously.

title: Re-bump @os-eco/burrow-cli double-pin once burrow-59cd ships
description: Follow-up to warren-a346 / pl-95dd which shipped warren 0.4.1 BEFORE burrow-59cd (the upstream body.env handler fix) was published to npm. The two bump-step seeds in pl-95dd (warren-0a6b: 'Bump @os-eco/burrow-cli double-pin', warren-73ee: 'Run scenario 25 end-to-end against a live stack') correctly detected the cascade-block at execution time and recorded notes rather than bumping to a non-existent version — see commits 9a12e6b and 983d77a. As a result: package.json (line 56) and Dockerfile (line 92) still pin @os-eco/burrow-cli at ^0.3.1 / 0.3.1, but scenario 25 (scripts/acceptance/scenarios/25-plot-dispatch-roundtrip.ts, warren-49d7, commit 19b7463) now hard-asserts that PLOT_ID/PLOT_ACTOR reach the sandbox — which production cannot deliver until the burrow bump lands. Scope of THIS seed (single mechanical step, no plan needed): (1) once burrow-59cd merges and the burrow release workflow publishes the new @os-eco/burrow-cli (likely 0.3.2 or 0.4.0), bump the pin in package.json + bun.lock (via bun install) + Dockerfile to that version, keeping all three locations in sync per the CLAUDE.md 'Relationship to burrow' double-pin convention; (2) run scenario 25 against a live warren+burrow stack to confirm the hard-required env-reach assertions pass; (3) hand-bump warren package.json version + src/index.ts VERSION to a new patch; (4) CHANGELOG entry noting this closes the cascade gap left by 0.4.1. Gating: do not start until burrow-59cd is visible at the burrow-cli version on npm. Related: pl-96ca (the burrow plan), warren-a346 (parent), pl-95dd (the plan that shipped half), warren-0a6b + warren-73ee (the cascade-block notes).

title: Shape (a): commit .plot/ through reap so Plot state round-trips to origin
description:
Follow-up to warren-6f25 / pl-d4d6 (shape b: snapshot+restore .plot/ across refreshProjectClone resets, mx-…). Shape (b) made .plot/ durable on the warren host's project clone but Plot state still never leaves the host — origin's main has no .plot/ commits, so a warren restart that re-clones loses everything, and operators inspecting the GitHub repo see nothing.

## The change
Make reap commit and push .plot/ alongside the agent's workspace edits.

Touchpoints:
- src/runs/reap.ts mergePlot() (~line 1236): after the events.jsonl + plot-*.json merges into the project clone, stage `.plot/plot-*.{events.jsonl,json}` via git add. Skip .plot/.index.db (gitignored derived state per ../plot/README.md).
- src/runs/reap.ts: the host-side appenders (defaultPlotAppender in src/runs/spawn.ts, defaultPlanRunPlotAppender in src/plan-runs/plot-appender.ts, autoTransitionPlotToDone in src/plan-runs/plot-transition.ts) write to .plot/ between spawns — those writes also need to be staged at reap time, not just the burrow-workspace-derived merges. Either reap stages everything in .plot/ at the end, or each appender stages its own write at append time.
- The branch_push step (reap.ts ~line 482) then carries the .plot/ commit upstream as part of the workspace branch.
- On merge to default branch, .plot/ is now durable on origin; next refreshProjectClone fetches it back and shape (b)'s snapshot becomes a no-op for already-committed files.

## Hard cases
1. **Trivial-merge children with no agent commits**: a child whose agent never ran `git commit` produces a branch with 0 commits ahead (reap.empty_push case, mx- / SPEC §11.G). Shape (a) needs reap to author its own commit ('chore(warren): plot state' or similar) when .plot/ has staged changes but the agent didn't commit — otherwise the Plot writes still don't ship. Identity: use a warren bot identity (e.g. 'warren <warren@os-eco.dev>') configured in the clone, not the agent's git identity.
2. **Two concurrent plan-run children both writing to the same Plot**: child A's reap pushes .plot/plot-X.events.jsonl with events 1..5; child B's branch was forked before A's merge, so B's refreshProjectClone fetches A's commit, then B appends events 6..7 on top. The append-only events.jsonl merges trivially via line-union (mergePlotEventsFile already does this). The plot-*.json LWW-on-updated_at logic (reap.ts mergePlot) handles status conflicts. But if A's PR hasn't merged yet when B starts, B forks from origin/main WITHOUT A's .plot/ commit — B's snapshot+restore (shape b) preserves its own local Plot writes, then B's reap re-merges with B's local view and commits. When A merges first then B merges, GitHub's merge produces a conflict on .plot/plot-X.events.jsonl. Mitigation: serialize via plan-run's existing 'gate next child on previous PR merge' semantics (SPEC §11.P) — covers the plan-run case. For ad-hoc parallel runs against the same project, document the conflict shape and rely on mergePlotEventsFile's idempotent line-union on the next refresh.
3. **PR auto-open with .plot/-only diffs**: a run where the agent did nothing but warren's appenders wrote a run_dispatched event produces a PR with only .plot/ changes. The auto-PR shape (mx-, src/runs/pr.ts) needs to decide whether to open one — current behavior keys on commitsAhead > 0, which a warren-authored .plot/ commit would satisfy. Probably fine to auto-PR, but worth a config knob (`autoOpenPrPlotOnly: false` default?) so .plot/-only changes don't spam PRs.
4. **Plot id collisions across branches**: two branches initializing the same plot id (rare; ids are uuid-shaped per ../plot/SPEC.md). Treat as user error.

## Acceptance
1. After a run that emits Plot events (host-side or agent-side), `git log -- .plot/` on origin/<workspace-branch> shows a commit by warren containing the events.jsonl + json updates.
2. A run where the agent never commits but warren wrote .plot/ entries still produces a non-empty branch push (warren-authored commit) — no reap.empty_push event in this case.
3. A plan-run with N>=2 children against a project with .plot/ produces N PRs (or 1 cumulative PR depending on PR mode) that each carry their child's .plot/ delta; after all merge, origin/main's .plot/ reflects the full plan-run history.
4. Wiping the warren host's project clone and re-cloning from origin recovers the full Plot state (events.jsonl + status.json) — shape (a)'s round-trip property, which shape (b) cannot provide.
5. Conflict shape documented in SPEC.md §11.O for the concurrent-children-same-plot case.
6. mergePlotEventsFile idempotency holds across the new commit-and-fetch loop — re-running reap against the same workspace adds nothing.
7. .plot/.index.db remains gitignored and never committed.
8. `bun test && bun run lint && bun run typecheck` passes.
9. CHANGELOG entry + version bump (likely minor — observable behavior change on the workspace branch).
10. SPEC.md §11.O updated: 'Plot state is committed through reap to the workspace branch and round-trips to origin on merge. The host-side .plot/ snapshot+restore in refreshProjectClone (warren-fdd2) remains as a belt-and-suspenders preservation for in-flight writes between dispatch and the next reap.'

## Why deferred from pl-d4d6
Shape (b) was the minimum to unblock the warren-on-warren dogfood (Plot state visible to the operator inspecting the host's project clone). Shape (a) extends durability to origin and across hosts, but the design has more surface area (warren-authored commits, PR-mode interaction, concurrent-branch conflicts) and needs its own plan.

title: Persist burrow's data dir on /data volume so deploys don't orphan in-flight runs
description: Symptom (observed 2026-05-18 on warren-deployed Fly app, machine e82656da0747d8): warren run run_dra64q8qt00z dispatched at 15:14:23Z with burrow_run_id run_9m0qs5m94srh on burrow bur_8py7wz7ndy4k. At 15:19:02Z the machine redeployed (v0.4.0 -> v0.4.1). After redeploy, warren's Supabase Postgres row still says state='running', but burrow returns 404 for run_9m0qs5m94srh on both POST /runs/:id/cancel and GET /runs/:id/stream. The stream-bridge has retried for 80+ attempts with 30s backoff and cannot finalize. Cancel from the UI surfaces 'run not found: run_9m0qs5m94srh' (the burrow-side id) through src/runs/cancel.ts:127 via withTransportMapping. Root cause: burrow's SQLite lives at /root/.local/share/burrow/db.sqlite (default XDG_DATA_HOME). That path is on the container's writable overlay, NOT on the /data Fly volume declared in fly.toml. Verified via flyctl ssh: 'stat /root/.local/share/burrow/db.sqlite' returns Birth: 2026-05-18 15:19:02.970334415 — matches the machine restart to the second. Every redeploy starts burrow with an empty DB, orphaning any in-flight runs whose state lives in warren's Postgres but whose execution state lived in burrow's wiped SQLite. Fix (warren-side, since warren owns Dockerfile + fly.toml + the supervisor that spawns 'burrow serve'): redirect burrow's data dir onto /data. Two viable approaches — (a) set ENV XDG_DATA_HOME=/data/xdg in Dockerfile (one-line, but co-locates any other XDG-using tool); (b) check whether 'burrow serve' accepts an explicit --data-dir flag (see ../burrow/SPEC.md and burrow's CLI; if it does, prefer wiring it through src/supervisor/main.ts:371 burrowCmd alongside --socket). Approach (b) is more surgical and survives unrelated XDG env changes. Verification: redeploy, confirm /data/xdg/burrow/db.sqlite (or the explicit path) exists and persists; dispatch a run, redeploy mid-flight, confirm warren's bridge reconnects and the run finalizes through the normal reap path rather than orphaning. Secondary gap worth a follow-up seed (not this one): warren has no reconciliation when burrow 404s a known burrow_run_id. The bridge in src/server/bridges.ts retries forever; cancel returns the burrow 404 verbatim. Even after the persistence fix, any future divergence (manual burrow DB reset, restored-from-backup, etc) will leave the same orphan state. A 'force-finalize' admin endpoint OR auto-finalize-on-persistent-404 in the bridge would cap the blast radius.

title: Plot UX: surface refresh affordance / auto-detect newly-committed .plot/ in registered projects
description:
First dogfood-discovered Plot UX gap (surfaced via plot-3e72876d / the housekeeping pass): Plots created via the plot 0.3.0 — coordination substrate for multi-agent work

Usage: plot <command> [options]

Commands:
  init           create a new Plot, return its ID
  list           list all Plots in .plot/
  show           pretty-print Plot fields + recent events
  edit           open intent in $EDITOR
  intent         non-interactive intent edit
  status         transition a Plot's status
  attach         attach a typed reference
  detach         remove an attachment
  answer         answer an agent-posed question
  get            render a view (agent-facing, default JSON)
  append         append an event (agent-facing)
  rebuild-index  wipe and regenerate the SQLite cache
  sync           stage + commit .plot/ source files
  doctor         check file integrity + event log replay

Global options:
  --help, -h     Show this help
  --version, -v  Print version
  --json         Machine-readable output (per command)

Env:
  PLOT_DIR       Plot directory (default: .plot)
  PLOT_ACTOR     Override actor (e.g. user:jw, agent:claude:run-1)
  PLOT_ID        Default Plot ID for agent commands (`get`, `append`)
  PLOT_DEBUG     Set to 1 to print stack traces on error CLI in a project repo are silently invisible in the warren UI until BOTH (a) the project is registered with warren AND (b) refreshProjectClone has run since the Plot files were committed. The /plots aggregator (src/plots/aggregate.ts:163 listSummaries) filters on projects.hasPlot, which detectProjectFeatures sets only during refresh. There is no UI affordance signaling 'I just committed a Plot, refresh to see it' — a user could push a Plot and stare at an empty /plots page indefinitely. The /plots/:id route 404s for the same reason (PlotResolver also scans only hasPlot projects). Symmetric failure: a user who git-pulls inside a deployed-warren-managed project clone (outside warren's process) would never see hasPlot flip until something else triggered refresh. Fix shapes (range from cheap to ambitious): (1) Cheap — when /plots returns an empty list AND any registered project has been git-touched recently OR the empty-state UI in /plots is hit, surface a 'Refresh projects to discover new Plots' CTA in src/ui/src/pages/PlotsList.tsx and src/ui/src/pages/PlotDetail.tsx empty/404 states. (2) Medium — periodic background refresh of registered projects (cron-style poll) gated by a config flag in .warren/config.yaml or warren-config domain, default off, opt-in for deployments where webhook infra isn't available. (3) Right — GitHub webhook on push → warren refreshes the project automatically; this is the same infrastructure R-18 (GitHub App auth) and V2 webhooks unlock, so this seed should reference R-18 and be considered subsumed when that lands. Evidence: plot-3e72876d (created locally 2026-05-18, committed to main as 2d7a75f, transitioned drafting → ready, attached to 9 housekeeping seeds, invisible in deployed warren UI until manual project refresh — user reported '/plots/plot-3e72876d returns plot not found' immediately after push). Recommended scope for V1 of this seed: ship (1) the empty-state CTA only — it's the smallest correct intervention, surfaces the next step inline, and doesn't commit warren to background polling or webhook infra. (2) and (3) become their own follow-up seeds once (1) is in production and we observe whether the affordance is enough.

title: Plot aggregator: rebuild index when query returns empty (not just on throw)
description: BLOCKS Plot UI dogfooding. queryWithRebuildRetry in src/plots/aggregate.ts:248-268 only triggers client.rebuildIndex() on a thrown error from client.query(). But .plot/.index.db is gitignored (warren commit 2d7a75f), so a freshly-refreshed project clone has the *.json + *.events.jsonl files but NO index DB. SQLitePlotIndex creates a fresh empty index file on first construction (does NOT throw), so query() returns rows=empty — no error — and the rebuild path is never hit. Result: /plots returns empty even when Plots exist on disk; /plots/:id 404s for the same reason because PlotResolver hits the same gated path. Discovered dogfooding plot-3e72876d (the housekeeping Plot) — invisible in /plots and /plots/plot-3e72876d after the warren project was refreshed in deployed warren. Reproduces locally too: delete .plot/.index.db files in any clone, call the /plots aggregator endpoint, get empty array. The mx-239786 record was titled 'cold-cache miss rebuild recovery' but the implementation only handled the error-throw case, not the empty-rows case. Tests at src/plots/aggregate.test.ts:269 pin the throw path; no test pins the empty-rows path — that is the gap. FIX SHAPE: in src/plots/aggregate.ts queryWithRebuildRetry, after the first successful query, if rows.length === 0 AND the .plot/ directory contains at least one non-dot non-index file (probe via fs.readdir filtered to *.json), call client.rebuildIndex() and re-query once before returning. Add a test to aggregate.test.ts mirroring the existing throw-retry test but pinning the empty-rows path: first query returns rows=[], the .plot/ directory probe sees a plot file, rebuildIndex is called once, second query returns the real row. The fs.readdir probe distinguishes 'index is stale, rebuild' from 'project legitimately has no plots, return empty' — without it, every empty-plots project pays a rebuild cost on every cache miss. ACCEPTANCE: (1) after a fresh refreshProjectClone on a hasPlot project whose .plot/ directory has *.json files but no .index.db, GET /plots returns the Plot summaries (was empty before fix); (2) GET /plots/:id resolves the same Plot (was 404 before); (3) a hasPlot project with .plot/ that contains zero *.json files returns empty without triggering a rebuild (zero rebuildCalls in the test); (4) the existing throw-path retry test at aggregate.test.ts:269 still passes unmodified; (5) new test pins the empty-rows + present-on-disk path; (6) bun test + bun run lint + bun run typecheck green with zero warnings. SCOPE: one file edit + one test. Do NOT bundle into the housekeeping plot-3e72876d arc — fix and ship as warren 0.4.4 ahead of housekeeping so dogfooding can proceed (warren-c106 already shipped as 0.4.3). Housekeeping then closes as 0.4.5.

title: Plot UX vision: surface the Plot→plan→seed→run flow end-to-end in the UI
description: VISION (filed as the dogfood signal from plot-3e72876d / the housekeeping pass): the Plot detail page should be the launchpad for all work bound to a Plot. Today the user must (a) navigate to /runs/new, (b) manually select project + agent + seed prompt, and (c) type the plot id by hand for every child seed — friction-heavy enough that for a 9-seed housekeeping arc the user described 'right now I can see the plot which is great, but now I need to manually go and trigger all these runs which isn't ideal.' The full vision is captured in the title: Plots become plans become seeds become runs, with the UI making that flow as easy as possible. Concrete shape: from PlotDetail with seeds_issue attachments, the user clicks one button and the housekeeping pass starts — N runs dispatched, each bound to plot_id, each appearing in the activity feed, optionally serial-gated on PR merge. Three child seeds: (1) per-seed Run button on PlotDetail; (2) batch Dispatch all attached seeds button on PlotDetail; (3) Plot→synthesized seeds plan→plan-run pipeline (cross-repo, needs seeds-side support for existing-seed children). Each child is independently shippable; we ship in order. Related: warren-bb22 (Plot discoverability, also dogfood signal #1), warren-ede7 (aggregator empty-rows, signal #2, shipped 0.4.4), the NewRun plot_id input prereq (will ship before this epic starts, as 0.4.5 or rolled into housekeeping). Out of scope here: anything that's not surfacing Plot-bound dispatch in the UI. Future Plot can be set up to track this epic itself once housekeeping closes.

title: PlotDetail: per-seeds_issue-attachment Run agent button
description: Step (1) of warren-e40a (Plot UX vision). On src/ui/src/pages/PlotDetail.tsx, in the SubstratePanel where seeds_issue attachments are rendered (currently a static row with detach button), add a small Run agent button next to each attachment. Click opens NewRun.tsx pre-filled with: project (from the Plot's owning project, resolved via PlotResolver), agent (the project default per .warren/defaults.json defaultRole), plot_id (this Plot's id), and prompt seeded from the seed's title + description via a project-default plot-run prompt template (or the .warren/defaults.json defaultPrompt with seed_id substitution). Pre-fill ALL of these — the user should be able to click Dispatch immediately without typing. The user can still edit any field before submitting. Implementation: pass query params or route state to NewRun, NewRun reads them on mount and sets initial form state. Test: click Run agent on a seeds_issue attachment, verify navigation to /runs/new with all four fields populated and a single Dispatch click creates a run that appears in the Plot's activity feed within 5s (the existing PlotDetail polling interval). Acceptance: (a) every seeds_issue attachment on PlotDetail renders a Run agent button, (b) clicking opens NewRun.tsx with project/agent/plot_id/prompt pre-filled, (c) the dispatched run lands in the Plot's activity feed within one poll tick, (d) detaching the attachment removes the button without re-render flicker, (e) no behavior change for non-seeds_issue attachments (mulch_record, canopy_prompt, etc. render unchanged). Depends on: the NewRun plot_id input prereq landing first. Parent: warren-e40a.

title: PlotDetail: batch Dispatch all attached seeds button
description: Step (2) of warren-e40a (Plot UX vision). On src/ui/src/pages/PlotDetail.tsx, add a Dispatch all attached seeds button to the page header (or to a dedicated 'Coordinate' section). Behavior: enumerates all seeds_issue attachments whose underlying seeds are still open (closed seeds skipped, matching plan-run resume semantics from pl-a258), then fires POST /runs for each one in parallel (default) or serial-gated-on-PR-merge (opt-in toggle). All dispatched runs are bound to this plot_id via the same path step (1) uses. Toggle UI: a checkbox 'Serial (gate next on prior PR merge)' next to the button, off by default. When serial mode is selected and the user clicks Dispatch all, warren creates ONE plan-run wrapping the open attachments (which is closer to what plan-runs already do but needs the seeds-side existing-seed-children support from step 3) OR — as a V1 fallback before step 3 lands — warren queues the runs internally and dispatches them in sequence as each prior PR merges. Confirm-dialog before firing N runs: 'Dispatch N runs against this Plot? Estimated cost:  (sum of per-run estimates).' Acceptance: (a) button visible whenever Plot has >=1 open seeds_issue attachment, (b) clicking (parallel mode) creates N runs all bound to plot_id, all visible in /runs and in this Plot's activity feed within one poll tick, (c) clicking (serial mode) creates 1 plan-run with N child runs, gated as in pl-a258, OR a stop-gap serial queue if step 3 hasn't shipped, (d) closed-seed attachments are skipped without spawning, (e) confirm-dialog blocks accidental fire. Depends on: step (1) landing first. Step (3) is a depends-on for the cleanest serial mode but a stop-gap is acceptable. Parent: warren-e40a.
closeReason: Adopted into pl-5310 as warren-7c3f (step 3: PlotDetail batch Dispatch all attached seeds button). Continue execution on warren-7c3f; this seed remains closed as the historical filing.

title: Plot→synthesized seeds plan→plan-run pipeline (needs seeds-side support for existing-seed children)
description: Step (3) of warren-e40a (Plot UX vision) — the deepest 'Plots become plans become seeds become runs' realization. Today sd plan submit spawns NEW children; you can't make existing seeds children of a new plan. That blocks the cleanest Plot-as-orchestrator shape: a Plot's seeds_issue attachments synthesize into a seeds plan whose children ARE those attached seeds, then plan-run drives them with all the existing serial-gating, PR-merge tracking, and auto-done-on-final-merge semantics for free. Two-part work: (A) cross-repo seeds change — extend sd plan submit (or add sd plan from-existing) to accept an existing_seeds: [warren-X, warren-Y, ...] field. Plan children become references to those seeds, not new spawns. Validation: every existing seed must already exist in .seeds/issues.jsonl; closed seeds rejected (plan-run already skips closed children but the plan creation should be intentional). (B) warren-side — PlotDetail surfaces a Dispatch as plan-run button (in addition to step 2's batch button). Click: warren server synthesizes a plan via the new seeds API, calls POST /plan-runs against the synthesized plan with plot_id pre-filled, navigates to /plan-runs/:id. Reuses every existing pl-7937 (warren-06dc) wiring — plan_run_dispatched event on the Plot, per-child PLOT_ID/PLOT_ACTOR injection, auto-done transition when the final child merges. Acceptance: (a) cross-repo: seeds ships sd plan submit support for existing-seed children with a unit test; (b) cross-repo: warren consumes the new seeds version (double-pin) and exposes it via a new endpoint POST /plot-plan-runs (or similar); (c) PlotDetail's 'Dispatch as plan-run' button fires the pipeline end-to-end against plot-3e72876d-shaped Plots (10 seeds_issue attachments → 1 plan-run with 10 children → auto-done on final merge); (d) existing pl-7937 / pl-a258 flows unchanged (no regression in current plan-run dispatch). Depends on: step (1) and the seeds-side change. Parent: warren-e40a. Largest of the three steps.
closeReason: Adopted into pl-5310 as warren-a4b7 (step 4: Plot→synthesized seeds plan→plan-run pipeline, cross-repo). Continue execution on warren-a4b7; this seed remains closed as the historical filing.

title: NewRun + server: validate plot_id format and reject malformed at dispatch
description: Step (4) of warren-e40a (Plot UX vision). Dogfood signal #4 from plot-3e72876d: a run was dispatched via the new NewRun.tsx plot_id input with the input field containing the literal string 'plot_id=plot-3e72876d' (the user pasted the prose form including the key= prefix instead of just the id). The run row (run_4cemtf8chga5) shows plot_id='plot_id=plot-3e72876d' in supabase. The server accepted it without complaint, the host-side defaultPlotAppender (src/runs/spawn.ts) tried to append on a Plot resolved by that malformed id, the lookup quietly failed (caught and logged best-effort, no impact on the run), and the only user-visible signal is the run detail page's PlotMetaCardContent (src/ui/src/components/PlotMetaCardContent.tsx:52) rendering 'Plot no longer available' — long after dispatch, far from the input that caused it. FIX SHAPE: (A) CLIENT — in src/ui/src/pages/NewRun.tsx and src/ui/src/pages/NewPlanRun.tsx, validate the trimmed plot_id against /^plot-[a-z0-9]+$/ on submit; if non-empty and non-matching, show an inline error 'Plot ID must look like plot-xxxxxxxx' and block the Dispatch button. Apply to BOTH forms — NewPlanRun has the same risk. (B) SERVER — in src/server/handlers.ts on POST /runs (and POST /plan-runs), when plot_id is present, validate format via the same regex AND resolve the Plot via PlotResolver BEFORE the run row is inserted; reject with a typed error (extend the existing src/runs/errors.ts shape — code='plot_id_not_found' or 'plot_id_invalid', recoveryHint='Verify the Plot exists at /plots') and return 400. This makes the dispatch surface honest: a malformed or non-existent plot_id fails at the operator-facing edge, not silently at the appender. (C) Acceptance: (1) submitting plot_id='plot_id=plot-3e72876d' (the actual user input that caused this seed) shows the inline error and blocks Dispatch on the client; (2) bypassing the client (curl) with the same malformed string gets a 400 with code='plot_id_invalid'; (3) submitting plot_id='plot-XXXXXXXX' (well-formed but non-existent) gets a 400 with code='plot_id_not_found'; (4) submitting plot_id='plot-3e72876d' (well-formed and exists) succeeds as today; (5) submitting no plot_id at all is byte-identical to current behavior; (6) the existing best-effort appender logging path is unchanged (it only fires on success now); (7) unit tests pin all five cases; (8) bun test && bun run lint && bun run typecheck green with zero warnings. Parent: warren-e40a. Sibling: warren-ff2a (per-seed Run button — will use the same validation when pre-filling plot_id), warren-ea66 (batch Dispatch — same), warren-5561 (plan-run pipeline — server-side validation reused). No cross-repo dependency — single warren PR.
closeReason: Adopted into pl-5310 as warren-bae5 (step 2: validate plot_id format and reject malformed at dispatch). Continue execution on warren-bae5; this seed remains closed as the historical filing.

Final step of pl-5310 (warren-e40a Plot UX vision). After steps 2 (warren-bae5 plot_id validation), 3 (warren-7c3f batch Dispatch button), and 4 (warren-a4b7 plan-run pipeline) all merge to main, cut a release that bundles the shipment. Procedure follows .claude/commands/release.md (/release slash command): (1) git describe --tags --abbrev=0 to find the prior tag; (2) git log/diff <tag>..HEAD to review what's landed since; (3) pick version bump level — default patch, but this shipment is plausibly a minor bump (new endpoint POST /plot-plan-runs from step 4, new UI surfaces from step 3, behavior change from step 2's typed-error rejection). Confirm bump level with the user before running; (4) bun run version:bump <level> to update package.json + src/index.ts in lockstep (the two-place pin verified by .github/workflows/release.yml); (5) update CHANGELOG.md — move [Unreleased] entries into the new version section with today's date, grouped by step (Plot batch dispatch / plot_id validation / Plot→plan-run pipeline); (6) update CLAUDE.md if command counts or structure changed (likely no — UI-only steps mostly); (7) update README.md if CLI reference or stats changed (likely yes — new POST /plot-plan-runs endpoint, possibly new UI screenshots if README has any); (8) present summary, then on user confirmation: commit, push, watch release.yml auto-tag v<version> and create the GitHub release from the CHANGELOG section. Acceptance: (a) version bumped in both package.json and src/index.ts and they agree; (b) CHANGELOG.md has a new version section dated today with all step 2-4 PRs called out; (c) bun test && bun run lint && bun run typecheck green on the release commit; (d) git push lands the version commit on main; (e) .github/workflows/release.yml runs green and produces a v<version> tag + GitHub release with the CHANGELOG body. Blocks epic warren-e40a closure. Adopted into pl-5310 post-hoc via sd plan adopt --step 5.

title: pi multi-provider: OPENAI_API_KEY (and other non-anthropic keys) not forwarded into sandbox
description:
## Symptom

Dispatching a pi run with `providerOverride: "openai"` (and `modelOverride: "gpt-4o"` or similar) composed the right argv (`pi --mode rpc --provider openai --model gpt-4o`), but the run failed on first API call because `OPENAI_API_KEY` was never forwarded into the burrow sandbox. Same for `GEMINI_API_KEY`, `GOOGLE_API_KEY`, `GROQ_API_KEY`, `MISTRAL_API_KEY`, `DEEPSEEK_API_KEY`.

End-user effect: the UI offered `provider/model` override inputs (NewRun.tsx, NewPlanRun.tsx, PlotDetail.tsx), `.env.example` documented the multi-provider keys, but the only provider that actually worked OOTB on pi was anthropic.

## Root cause (fixed in burrow v0.3.3)

`burrow/src/runtime/pi.ts:162` pinned `PI_ENV_PASSTHROUGH` to anthropic-only. The comment block at :155-160 explicitly rejected forwarding non-anthropic keys on the assumption that argv pins `--provider anthropic` — but warren's multi-provider surface (warren-f8c0, pl-4374) lets operators override the provider per run.

Fixed by burrow-6f3f (option 2): `AgentRuntime.envPassthrough` widened to `readonly string[] | ((ctx) => readonly string[])`; `piRuntime.envPassthrough = piEnvPassthrough` returns the anthropic triple plus the matching provider key from `PI_PROVIDER_ENV_KEYS` keyed off `frontmatter.provider`. The dispatcher's `applyRuntimeEnvPassthrough` re-invokes the function with the run's frontmatter and unions the delta onto the per-spawn `SandboxProfile.envPassthrough`. Non-pi runtimes (claude-code, sapling, codex) keep their static-array semantics — the widening is additive.

## Cross-repo PR links

**Burrow side (burrow-6f3f, all on `main` and shipped in v0.3.3):**

- `f2fef9e` fix(runtime): conditional envPassthrough by frontmatter.provider for pi (burrow-6f3f)
- `3df44f8` chore: release v0.3.3 — conditional envPassthrough by frontmatter.provider for pi (burrow-6f3f)
- Published to npm as `@os-eco/burrow-cli@0.3.3`

**Warren side:**

- Bump `@os-eco/burrow-cli` 0.3.2 → 0.3.3 (package.json + bun.lock + Dockerfile triple-pin per CLAUDE.md "Relationship to burrow")
- `scripts/acceptance/lib/burrow-with-stub.ts`: stub pi runtime swaps in `piEnvPassthrough` so the harness exercises the new function-form contract
- `scripts/acceptance/lib/stub-agent/pi-agent.sh`: emit `{"type":"env_keys_visible","keys":[...]}` at startup so scenarios can assert on what landed in the spawned env (collapses to `state_change` via pi parser's unknown-type fallthrough)
- `scripts/acceptance/scenarios/30-pi-multi-provider-env.ts`: new scenario — sets `OPENAI_API_KEY` in process.env, dispatches pi with `providerOverride='openai'`, waits for the `env_keys_visible` event and asserts `OPENAI_API_KEY` is in the list. Proves the warren↔burrow handoff (operator override → renderedAgentJson.frontmatter → burrow run metadata → dispatcher `readFrontmatter` → `piEnvPassthrough` → spawn env) is intact end-to-end.

Final warren quality gates after the bump: `bun test` 1777 pass / 0 fail; `tsc --noEmit` clean; `bun run lint` clean (342 files via biome).

## Closure gate — all conditions met

1. **burrow-6f3f is closed.** [met]
2. **Burrow ships a tagged release whose `piRuntime.envPassthrough` honors `frontmatter.provider`.** [met — v0.3.3 on npm]
3. **Warren bumps `@os-eco/burrow-cli` to that release in lockstep at all three pin sites.** [met — package.json + bun.lock + Dockerfile all on 0.3.3]
4. **Acceptance scenario 30 covers a pi+openai end-to-end and asserts the sandbox env contains OPENAI_API_KEY.** [met]
5. **Cross-repo PR links recorded back into this seed before closure.** [met — above]

## References

- `burrow/src/runtime/pi.ts:162-218` — `PI_ENV_PASSTHROUGH` + `PI_PROVIDER_ENV_KEYS` + `piEnvPassthrough`
- `burrow/src/runner/dispatch.ts:432` — `applyRuntimeEnvPassthrough`
- `warren/src/runs/spawn.ts:387` — `composeBurrowMetadata` (frontmatter → run metadata)
- `warren/scripts/acceptance/scenarios/30-pi-multi-provider-env.ts` — end-to-end coverage

title: V2 Plot-workbench roadmap: warm sandboxes, native diff/review UI, webhook-triggered Plots, cross-Plot references, plot.conflict UI
description:
Roadmap of Plot-workbench directions deferred from V1+V1.5 (pl-0344). Revisit once V1 has shipped and adoption signals show which of these is highest-leverage.

## V2 candidates

1. **Warm interactive sandboxes (with hibernation).** Replace respawn-per-turn with long-lived burrow containers that suspend to disk after N seconds idle. Preserves agent in-memory scout state across turns — eliminates the per-turn cold start and inconsistent-reasoning risk flagged in pl-0344 risks. Requires deep burrow contract change: idle-aware reaper, hibernation primitive, resource accounting. Priority depends on whether V1 interactive runs feel snappy enough.

2. **Native diff/review UI in warren.** Diff viewer, comment threads, approval UI, mergeable state machine — all in warren. Eliminates the GitHub context switch entirely (V1.5 click-to-merge still sends users to github.com for review). Months of work; only worth doing if users complain that review is the remaining friction after V1.5.

3. **Webhook-triggered Plot creation.** GitHub issue labeled 'plot' (or comment posted) → warren auto-creates a Plot with the issue attached. Strong adoption vector for teams already living in GitHub Issues. Couples to SPEC §11.I V2 trigger work that's also deferred — bundle these together.

4. **Cross-Plot references.** A Plot can link to another Plot ('this Plot supersedes pl-foo', 'depends on outcome of pl-bar'). Enables Plot-of-Plots patterns and meta-coordination across long-running initiatives. Not modeled in plot v1; needs spec work first.

5. **plot.conflict UI surfacing.** Today warren emits plot.conflict events when concurrent runs write conflicting .plot/<id>.json full-doc state; UI is silent. Surface conflict state on the Plot with a 'refresh & retry' affordance. Small; could pull forward into V1.5 if it bites in practice.

6. **True burrow pause/resume primitive (in-memory state preserved).** Replaces V1's timeout-respawn-with-state-lost mechanism for blocking question_posed. Cleaner UX (agent retains scout progress across human delay) but requires burrow-side hibernation. Likely lands together with warm sandboxes (#1).

7. **Plot summary export formats.** /plots/:id/summary in V1.5 is HTML; V2 adds Markdown export (for sharing in chat/issues), PDF export (for records), and JSON export (for programmatic post-processing). Cheap once the summary endpoint exists.

8. **Interactive run sub-types beyond brainstorm/planner.** PR review chat (agent loaded with PR diff + Plot context), debug-with-agent (agent loaded with failing test + recent runs), retro chat (agent loaded with done Plot's full event log, helps the user extract lessons → mulch records). Each is a new agent config, no new primitive — natural extensions once the chat surface exists.

## Sequencing intuition

Of these, #1 (warm sandboxes) and #5 (plot.conflict UI) have the clearest 'this is annoying' adoption signal that V1 will surface. #3 (webhooks) and #2 (native review) are bigger asks that need their own plan-of-plans once V1 is in users' hands. #4 (cross-Plot refs) needs spec work in the plot project first. #8 (new interactive sub-types) is opportunistic — pick one when a real user need surfaces.

Do not plan this seed via sd plan submit yet. Pick a candidate, file it as its own feature seed with its own plan when V1 adoption justifies it.

title: Interactive agents (brainstorm/planner) fail with 'agent not registered' — dispatch sends agent.name as burrow runtime id
description:
## Repro

Trigger the `brainstorm` agent from the Plot brainstorming UI (e.g. `POST /plots/:id/messages` on a fresh interactive run). Run finalizes as `failed / never_started` in <1s with no agent output.

Observed on deployed warren-deployed (Fly) on 2026-05-23:
- `run_vhjshg6atdw6` (plot-302d6f6a, agent=brainstorm) — failed
- `run_a4k2mgcnp3c5` (same plot, retry 24s later) — failed identically

## Root cause

`src/runs/spawn.ts:392` dispatches as:

    dispatchRun(placement.client, burrow.id, agent.name, ...)

i.e. warren sends `agent.name` (\"brainstorm\") as the burrow **runtime id**. Burrow looks that string up in `BUILT_IN_RUNTIMES` (`burrow/src/runtime/registry.ts:24`). The only registered runtimes are: `claude-code`, `sapling`, `codex`, `pi`. There is no `brainstorm` (or `planner`) runtime — those are warren-side canopy-style agent **definitions** (system prompt + sections) shipped in `src/registry/builtins/{brainstorm,planner}.ts`, intended to *layer on top of* a runtime like claude-code.

Burrow's dispatch returns:

    { state: \"failed\", errorMessage: \"agent 'brainstorm' is not registered\" }

Warren's reap sees state=queued on entry → classifies as `never_started` (`src/runs/reap.ts:1626`).

## Why this wasn't caught earlier

Every prior successful run on this project happened to use the `pi` agent, whose name coincidentally matches a burrow runtime. claude-code/sapling/pi all work by name-collision. brainstorm/planner are the first warren agents whose name does *not* match a burrow runtime — the Plot brainstorming UI is the first surface to exercise them.

## Proposed fix (warren-side, no burrow change)

1. Add an optional `runtime` field to agent frontmatter (e.g. `frontmatter.runtime: \"claude-code\"`).
2. Stamp `runtime: \"claude-code\"` on `BRAINSTORM_BUILTIN` and `PLANNER_BUILTIN` (the runtime they were always meant to run on — frontmatter already carries `provider: anthropic, model: claude-opus-4-7` via spawn-time injection).
3. In `dispatchRun` callers in `src/runs/spawn.ts`, send `agent.frontmatter.runtime ?? agent.name` instead of `agent.name`.

Compatibility:
- claude-code/sapling/pi builtins keep `agent.name === runtime`, so no behavior change.
- Library/project canopy agents that today rely on name=runtime keep working (fallback to `agent.name`).
- Library agents that want to compose on a runtime gain a clean opt-in (`runtime: \"claude-code\"` in their canopy frontmatter).

## Out of scope

- Per-tool ACLs / sandboxing differences between the brainstorm system-prompt-only contract and actual runtime enforcement (already noted in `brainstorm.ts` header).
- Adding `brainstorm`/`planner` as native burrow runtimes — explicitly avoided per user direction; we don't want to couple burrow to warren's interactive agent catalog.

## Acceptance

- POSTing a brainstorm interactive run reaches an agent process; events of kind `text`/`thinking`/`tool_use` show up on the run.
- Spawn unit test asserts dispatch uses `frontmatter.runtime` when set, falls back to `agent.name` when not.
- A built-in agents test asserts BRAINSTORM_BUILTIN and PLANNER_BUILTIN both carry `frontmatter.runtime === \"claude-code\"`.

title: Re-upsert builtin agents on boot when frontmatter has drifted
Interactive runs: claude-code exits 1 with no model output (no_model_response)
description:
After warren-ced0 / hand-patching the agents table to add frontmatter.runtime, interactive dispatch now correctly routes onto the claude-code burrow runtime — but the agent process exits with code 1 about 17s after spawn without emitting a single stdout event. Reap classifies the run as failed/no_model_response.

Repro: planner dispatch on plot-302d6f6a (warren-deployed). Run run_0e4qmpsrk1sh, burrow run run_6139hcpppapd, burrow bur_3swnf8749nhb. Frontmatter on the run is correct ({provider: anthropic, model: claude-opus-4-7, runtime: claude-code}). Same plot, same project, all the pi agent runs on this project succeed — so ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is fine and the burrow itself is healthy.

What we have:
- burrow run record: state=failed, exitCode=1, errorMessage='agent exited with code 1'
- warren bridge: written=0, skipped=1 (one stream chunk dropped — investigate why)
- events table: 0 stdout/stderr events for this run; only user_message + reap.* system events
- fly logs: no agent stderr surfaced (burrow may not be forwarding it to its own stderr, only to the events stream)

Next steps:
1. Pull the burrow per-run event log via 'burrow runs show <id>' on the fly machine (or GET /runs/:id/stream?follow=false) and capture what claude-code printed before exit. The 'skipped: 1' in the bridge log is the most interesting bread crumb — likely the stderr line that caused the exit.
2. Once we have the stderr, classify: (a) claude-code config error (e.g. composed prompt too large for the model, malformed system block prepend), (b) sandbox network/tool issue specific to interactive vs batch, or (c) something in the planner's burrow_config (network = 'open' is the only thing it sets).
3. Likely candidate worth checking first: the dispatch prompt is ~3500 chars of planner system body prepended via composeDispatchPrompt + a <plot_context> XML envelope. Verify claude-code-cli accepts that shape on the burrow side.

Tracker context: related to warren-ebca (the original brainstorm/planner routing fix) and warren-ced0 (the seed-on-boot upsert fix). Both runs that previously hit warren-ebca also dispatched to claude-code post-patch but failed with the same no_model_response, so this is reproducible.

title: Make brainstorm/planner runtime configurable via .warren/config.yaml
description:
Today the brainstorm and planner built-in canopy agents hard-code their underlying burrow runtime via frontmatter.runtime in src/registry/builtins/brainstorm.ts and src/registry/builtins/planner.ts (both currently 'pi', switched from 'claude-code' on 2026-05-22).

Problem: operators may want to drive these interactive scouts on a different runtime (claude-code, sapling, pi, or a library/project-tier override) without forking warren. A library-tier override by same name works today but requires standing up a canopy library just to change one frontmatter field.

Proposal: add an optional knob to the .warren/config.yaml DefaultsConfigSchema (src/warren-config/schema.ts) — e.g.

  interactiveAgents:
    brainstormRuntime: claude-code | sapling | pi
    plannerRuntime:    claude-code | sapling | pi

At dispatch time (or at registry seed time), resolve the runtime from config first and fall back to the builtin's stamped frontmatter.runtime. Keep the BUILT_IN_RUNTIMES allowlist (readRuntimeId) as the validator so typos don't silently fail at burrow boot.

Open questions:
- Resolve at seed time (mutate the agent row) or at dispatch time (overlay)? Dispatch-time keeps the row honest as 'builtin' and lets the operator flip without re-seeding.
- Should canopy-library overrides win over .warren/config.yaml, or the other way around? Current agentSourceTier order says library > builtin; config should probably slot between or override library on this single field only.
- Surface this in the UI agent picker so users can see which runtime backs the interactive role.

References:
- src/registry/builtins/brainstorm.ts, src/registry/builtins/planner.ts (the values)
- src/registry/schema.ts:readRuntimeId (the lookup)
- src/warren-config/schema.ts:DefaultsConfigSchema (where the knob lives)
- mx-77117c / warren-ebca history for why frontmatter.runtime exists

title: planner default prompt + system prompt don't produce a commit
description:
User dispatched the planner from the Plot UI with an explicit 'commit and sync when you're done!' opener; agent refused per system prompt and asserted 'warren itself reaps and commits the workspace branch.' That assertion is half-right — and the half that's missing is the bug.

## Root cause

src/runs/reap.ts auto-commits .plot/ deltas but not .seeds/ deltas:

- step 4 (lines 500-512, gated on `project.hasPlot`): `stagePlotForCommit` authors a `chore(warren): plot state` commit covering only `.plot/` (warren-343a, shape (a) commit-through-reap).
- step 2 (lines 471, 1182-1238, `mirrorClosedSeeds`): only mirrors **closed** seeds back to the project clone (line 1209: `if (incoming.status !== "closed") continue;`). New open child seeds the planner just spawned are dropped entirely.
- step 5 (`branch_push`): `git push origin HEAD` — but HEAD has no .seeds/ commit because the agent is forbidden from committing and warren never staged it.
- step 6 (line 545): warren detects `commitsAhead === 0` and fires `reap.empty_push`. This is exactly the 'nothing happened' UI signal the user sees.

Net effect: planner's `sd plan submit` writes `.seeds/issues.jsonl` + `.seeds/plans.jsonl` inside the burrow workspace; reap doesn't commit them; push lands no work; burrow tears down; plan is lost.

## Two layers to fix

1. **Default opener** (src/ui/src/pages/PlotDetail.tsx:2398): 'Read the Plot intent, scout the repo, and propose a structured plan.' — silent on persistence.

2. **System prompt** (src/registry/builtins/planner.ts:51): explicitly forbids git writes ('Run `git` write operations (commit, push, branch, tag, etc.).'). So opener instructions can't override this.

## Fix options to weigh

- (A) Extend warren-343a to also stage+commit `.seeds/` deltas on reap (mirror the .plot/ pattern, gated on hasSeeds). Keeps the planner narrowly read-only and the persistence guarantee on warren.
- (B) Relax the planner's system prompt to allow `git add .seeds/ .plot/ && git commit` only, then update PLANNER_OPENER to instruct it. Trade-off: gives the planner one more thing to get right.
- (C) Extend `mirrorClosedSeeds` to mirror *all* seeds the agent created in this run (not just closed), and have warren commit on the project clone side. Riskier — touches project clone state outside the workspace branch.

(A) is the smallest blast radius and lets the planner stay strictly read-only.

## Acceptance

- Dispatching the planner from a Plot ends with at least one commit on the run branch covering `.seeds/issues.jsonl` + `.seeds/plans.jsonl`.
- `reap.empty_push` does NOT fire for a successful planner run.
- The new plan id (`pl-XXXX`) and child seed ids are visible in the project clone after the run reaps (no more lost work).
- Plot activity feed shows the run_dispatched + the resulting attachment_added events for the spawned plan.

title: preservePlot overwrites remote .plot/ changes on project refresh
description:
## Bug

`defaultPreservePlot` in `src/projects/refresh.ts` snapshots the entire `.plot/` directory before `git reset --hard` and restores it after, blindly overwriting whatever git just fetched from the remote. This means any `.plot/` changes committed and pushed to GitHub (e.g. new attachments, intent edits) are silently clobbered by the stale local snapshot on every project refresh.

## Reproduction

1. Create a plot locally, attach seeds/plan, commit, push
2. Register the project in warren (or let it clone before the attachments exist)
3. Refresh the project from /projects/
4. The plot shows up in the UI but with 0 attachments — the pre-refresh snapshot overwrote the freshly-fetched JSON

## Root cause

`defaultPreservePlot` (line 286) assumes "snapshot wins on conflict — a host-appender write is strictly newer than anything committed into the project repo's `.plot/`" (line 271). That assumption is wrong when the remote has newer data than the local clone (user committed attachments, intent changes, etc. and pushed).

The restore copies every file from the snapshot back over the post-reset working tree, including `.json` plot files that may have been updated on the remote.

## Fix direction

The preserve mechanism should **merge** rather than blindly overwrite. Options:

- **Track dirty files**: only snapshot/restore files that warren's host-side appenders actually modified (append-only `.events.jsonl` files and status changes to `.json`). Files untouched by the host should take the remote version.
- **Merge `.json` files**: for plot JSON files, merge host-side fields (e.g. `status`) with remote-side fields (e.g. `attachments`) rather than winner-takes-all.
- **Commit host writes before refresh**: if warren committed its own `.plot/` writes before fetching, the `git reset --hard` would incorporate both via the remote, and no snapshot/restore would be needed at all. This is the cleanest long-term fix but changes the git history contract.

## Affected files

- `src/projects/refresh.ts` — `defaultPreservePlot`, `snapshotPlotDir`, `restorePlotDir`

title: Plot aggregator misses new plots added to disk after index was built
description:
## Bug

`queryWithRebuildRetry` in `src/plots/aggregate.ts:439` only rebuilds the `.plot/.index.db` when the index returns **zero rows** or throws an error. It does not detect new plot files added to disk after an existing index was built.

When a plot is committed to git and fetched by the server during project refresh, the `.plot/plot-*.json` file lands on disk but `.index.db` (gitignored) is never updated. The plot is invisible in the UI until someone manually deletes the index.

## Reproduction

1. Register a project in warren that already has `.plot/` with ≥1 plot (index gets built)
2. Commit a new plot file to the project's `.plot/` directory and push
3. Refresh the project in warren (git fetch + reset succeeds, new file lands on disk)
4. `GET /plots` — new plot is missing from the response

## Root cause

`queryWithRebuildRetry` short-circuits at line 443: if `r.rows.length > 0`, it returns immediately without checking whether disk has more files than the index knows about. The zero-rows rebuild (warren-ede7) only covers the cold-cache case (fresh clone, empty index), not the incremental case (index exists but is stale).

## Suggested fix

After the index query returns rows, compare `r.rows.length` against the count of `plot-*.json` files on disk (via `hasPlotFilesOnDisk` or a new `countPlotFiles` method). If disk has more files than the index, trigger `rebuildIndex()` and retry. This is the same rebuild path the zero-rows and error branches already use — the only change is adding a third trigger condition.

Alternatively, rebuild the index unconditionally during project refresh (`refreshProjectClone`), since that's the moment new files arrive from git.

title: Reap seeds mirror drops newly-created open issues from planner runs
description:
mirrorClosedSeeds (reap step 2) only propagates rows with status=closed from the burrow workspace back to the project clone. When the planner agent creates new open issues via sd plan submit, they are never copied to the project clone. stageSeedsForCommit (reap step 4) then copies the project clone's .seeds/ (which lacks the new issues) back into the workspace for the commit — so the new seeds are lost when the sandbox is destroyed.

Meanwhile mergePlot (reap step 3) eagerly copies .plot/ attachments referencing those seed IDs, leaving the plot with dangling refs to seeds that don't exist anywhere.

Observed in run_0wz54sybfdr4 / plot-1ce5ca91: planner created warren-7c75, warren-552c, warren-285f, warren-387a, warren-e0ed, warren-a4b9, warren-8ffc and plan pl-e4d7 — none exist in .seeds/.

Fix: broaden mirrorClosedSeeds (or add a companion step) to also propagate new seed rows from the workspace that don't exist in the project clone. The closed-only update guard for existing rows can stay; the gap is rows that are entirely new.

Key code: src/runs/reap.ts lines 1226-1301 (mirrorClosedSeeds), lines 1660-1729 (stageSeedsForCommit).

title: probeOnce misclassifies slow HTTP responses as not_connected during phase-1 connect
description:
## Problem

`probeOnce()` in `src/preview/launch.ts` uses a 2-second `AbortController` timeout on `fetch()`. When a dev server (e.g. Next.js) has accepted the TCP connection but is mid-first-compile and can't send response headers within 2s, the abort fires, `fetch()` throws, and the catch arm classifies the outcome as `not_connected`.

This prevents the phase-1 (connect) → phase-2 (readiness) transition from ever firing until the compile completes, because every probe returns `not_connected`. The connect_timeout budget is consumed by a server that IS listening — the probe just can't tell.

The code acknowledges the ambiguity:
> "We can't perfectly distinguish 'bound but hung mid-headers + abort' from 'never connected + abort' inside the catch arm; treating that case as not_connected is the conservative choice"

## Evidence

- `run_sjxddbjpg950` on jayminwest.com: Next.js reported "Ready in 41.5s" and started "Compiling / ..." but the connect phase timed out at 300s because every 2s probe was aborted mid-compile.
- Workaround: bumped `connect_timeout: "30m"` in `.warren/preview.yaml`.

## Proposed fix

Replace the HTTP-level probe in phase 1 with a raw TCP connect check (e.g. `Bun.connect()` or `net.createConnection()`). Phase 1 only needs to answer "is anything listening on this port?" — it doesn't need an HTTP response. Reserve the HTTP fetch for phase 2.

Alternative: increase `PROBE_PER_CALL_TIMEOUT_MS` for phase-1 probes only (e.g. 30s), but this slows down detection of genuinely dead servers.

## Files

- `src/preview/launch.ts` — `probeOnce()`, phase-1 loop logic

title: UI shows no indication when burrow socket is unreachable
description:
<!-- seeds:plan-backref:start -->
Adopted into plan pl-369d.

Parent seed: warren-8104 — Release-train batch: notable Medium + small quality wins
Plan template: feature
Plan approach: Submit minimal placeholders, release+close them, adopt the work seeds in execution order, adopt a release seed last so plan-run gates each on the previous PR merge and ends with a patch release.

Run `sd plan show pl-369d` for the full plan (context, alternatives, sibling steps, acceptance criteria).
<!-- seeds:plan-backref:end -->

## Problem

When burrow's unix socket times out (e.g. under CPU starvation on shared Fly VMs), the UI shows the run stuck at "starting" with no error feedback. The user sees a spinner indefinitely with no way to tell that the underlying infrastructure is hung vs. the run is just slow.

The stream bridge logs `burrow unreachable at unix:/var/run/burrow.sock: The operation timed out.` and reconnects with exponential backoff, but none of this surfaces to the user.

## Evidence

- `run_x2akk9x2zv9w` and `run_ymkvccja6qg7` (2026-05-25) both stuck in `state: running` for 25+ minutes with no UI indication of failure. Fly logs showed repeated burrow socket timeouts (`attempt:2`, `attempt:3`) with backoff, but the UI just showed "starting".
- Root cause was `shared-cpu-2x` Fly VM being CPU-starved under concurrent runs.

## Expected behavior

- After N consecutive bridge reconnect failures (or a wall-clock cap), the run should surface a warning or error state in the UI — e.g. "agent infrastructure unreachable" or "run stalled".
- The events stream should include a synthetic event when the bridge enters error/reconnect state, so the UI can show a degraded indicator.

## Files

- `src/runs/bridge.ts` (or wherever the stream bridge reconnect logic lives)
- UI run detail view
closeReason: PR #207 (commit 53fdef1) surfaces stalled burrow stream in the UI; merged on main.

title: Auto-dispatch plan-runs from reap when agent frontmatter declares auto_plan_run: true
description:
## Summary

When a run's agent has `auto_plan_run: true` in its canopy frontmatter and the run creates a new seeds plan during execution, reap should automatically create a plan-run for that plan. This enables the "patrol agent" pattern: a cron-triggered agent scans a repo for quality issues, files a declarative sd plan, and warren auto-dispatches a plan-run to execute it — no second cron, no plan_id handoff problem.

## Motivation

The patrol agent pattern:
1. Cron fires a patrol agent (e.g. `nightwatch`, `redhat-patroller`, `ui-patroller`) nightly
2. Agent scans code, creates an sd plan scoped to quality fixes (no feature creep), with a patch version bump as the final step
3. Reap merges the PR containing the new plan
4. Warren auto-creates a plan-run for the new plan
5. Plan-run walks children one at a time, auto-merging each, ending with the version bump

Without this feature, there's a plan_id handoff gap: the patrol agent creates the plan, but a second mechanism needs to discover the plan_id and call POST /plan-runs. This feature closes that gap by making reap the bridge.

## Design

### Frontmatter convention

Canopy agent definitions opt in via frontmatter:

```yaml
---
frontmatter:
  auto_plan_run: true
---
```

The flag lives on the agent, not the plan, because the intent to auto-execute is about who created it and why. A patrol agent always means "execute this." A general-purpose agent that happens to create a plan doesn't.

### Reap hook location

In `reapRun` (src/runs/reap.ts), after `stageSeedsForCommit` (currently ~line 549) and before `branch_push` (~line 561). At this point:
- `run.renderedAgentJson` is available with full frontmatter
- `.seeds/plans.jsonl` has been committed to the workspace
- The project's seeds data is accessible for diffing

### Detection flow

1. Early in reap (before mirrorSeeds), snapshot the project's `.seeds/plans.jsonl` plan ids as a baseline set
2. After `stageSeedsForCommit`, re-read the workspace's `.seeds/plans.jsonl`
3. Diff by plan id — new entries are plans created during this run
4. For each new plan: read its children via `sd plan show <id> --format json` or parse plans.jsonl directly
5. If `run.renderedAgentJson.frontmatter?.auto_plan_run === true`, call `repos.planRuns.create()` for each new plan

### Plan-run creation

```typescript
repos.planRuns.create({
  planId: newPlan.id,
  projectId: project.id,
  agentName: run.agentName,      // same agent executes the plan
  children: newPlan.children.map((seedId, i) => ({ seq: i + 1, seedId })),
  trigger: "auto_plan_run",
  ref: project.defaultBranch,     // run children against main (post-merge)
  plotId: run.plotId ?? undefined, // inherit if present
})
```

### Gating conditions

All must be true for auto-dispatch:
- `run.renderedAgentJson.frontmatter?.auto_plan_run === true`
- Run outcome is `succeeded` (don't auto-dispatch from failed patrol scans)
- `project !== null` and `project.hasSeeds === true`
- At least one NEW plan detected (not pre-existing plans)
- `seedsCli` is available (same gate as POST /plan-runs handler)

### Event emission

Emit `auto_plan_run_created` system event with `{ planId, planRunId, childCount }` so the UI can surface it and the event log captures the link between patrol run and plan-run.

### ReapRunResult extension

Add to the result interface:
- `autoPlanRunCreated: boolean`
- `autoPlanRunId: string | null`
- `autoPlanRunPlanId: string | null`

### Error handling

Non-fatal — wrap in try/catch using the existing `fail()` pattern. A failure to auto-dispatch should not block the rest of reap (branch push, PR creation). Log the error, emit a `system` event, continue.

### Idempotency

If reap runs twice against the same workspace:
- First call: new plans detected, plan-run created
- Second call: no new plans (workspace unchanged), no-op
- If the plan-run already exists for that planId, repos.planRuns.create should handle gracefully (reject duplicate or no-op)

## Key files

| File | Role |
|------|------|
| `src/runs/reap.ts` | Hook implementation — detect new plans, check frontmatter, create plan-run |
| `src/registry/schema.ts` | Document `auto_plan_run` in well-known frontmatter fields comment (lines 58-79) |
| `src/db/repos/plan-runs.ts` | Existing `create()` method — may need to accept `trigger` field |
| `src/seeds-cli/` | Plan parsing helpers if needed beyond raw JSONL parsing |
| `src/runs/reap.test.ts` | Tests for the new hook |

## Test cases

1. Agent WITHOUT `auto_plan_run` creates a plan → no plan-run created
2. Agent WITH `auto_plan_run: true` but run failed → no plan-run created
3. Agent WITH `auto_plan_run: true`, run succeeded, no new plans → no plan-run created
4. Agent WITH `auto_plan_run: true`, run succeeded, one new plan → plan-run created with correct children
5. Agent WITH `auto_plan_run: true`, run succeeded, multiple new plans → one plan-run per plan
6. Idempotent re-reap → no duplicate plan-runs
7. Project with no seeds → skip gracefully

## Out of scope

- The patrol agent canopy prompt itself (separate seed)
- Cron trigger configuration (separate seed — just a triggers.yaml entry)
- Any changes to plan-run coordinator or child dispatch logic
- UI surfacing of auto-dispatched plan-runs (follow-up)

title: nightwatch patrol: recurring
description: Recurring seed for the nightwatch cron trigger. The nightwatch agent scans the repo for quality issues and files a plan to fix them. This seed is reused across patrol runs — it is not closed when a patrol completes.

title: Make seed optional on cron triggers
description: CronTriggerSchema in src/warren-config/schema.ts requires a seed field on every cron trigger. This forces workarounds like creating 'recurring' placeholder seeds for agents that don't work on a specific issue (e.g. nightwatch patrol, which creates seeds rather than working on one). The seed field should be optional — spawnRun already accepts seedId as undefined, so the dispatch path handles it. Change SeedRefSchema from required to optional on CronTriggerSchema, update the dispatch path in src/triggers/dispatch.ts to pass undefined when omitted, and update the triggers.yaml header comment to reflect the new optionality.
