title: auto_plan_run inherits parent agent name instead of defaultRole — bugwatch/nightwatch children get wrong system prompt
description:
## Summary

When bugwatch or nightwatch agents create plans (via `auto_plan_run: true` frontmatter), the auto-dispatched plan-run children inherit the parent agent's name (bugwatch/nightwatch) instead of using a generic coding agent (e.g. `claude-code` / the project's `defaultRole`). This means child runs — which are supposed to implement fixes — get the bugwatch/nightwatch system prompt injected, telling them they are triage/patrol agents that must NOT write source code.

## Root Cause

Two lines in the dispatch chain propagate the wrong agent name:

1. **`src/runs/reap.ts:616`** — when the reap flow auto-creates a PlanRun for a newly-created plan, it passes `agentName: run.agentName`. Since the parent run IS bugwatch (or nightwatch), the PlanRun gets `agentName: "bugwatch"` (or `"nightwatch"`).

2. **`src/plan-runs/dispatch.ts:56`** — when the coordinator dispatches each child run, it passes `agentName: planRun.agentName` to `spawnRun`. Each child run therefore boots with the bugwatch/nightwatch agent definition (system prompt, burrow_config, frontmatter).

## Effect

- Child runs receive a system prompt that says "Do NOT write source files" and "your only writes are to .seeds/ via the sd CLI"
- The agent tries to plan fixes instead of implementing them, or gets confused about its role
- Both bugwatch and nightwatch declare `runtime: "pi"` in frontmatter — the runtime is correct, but the agent name (and therefore the system prompt, rendered agent JSON, and behavioral constraints) is wrong

## Evidence from Fly Logs (warren-deployed, 2026-05-26)

Three plan-runs are stuck in an infinite noop loop on the deployed instance:

```
planRunId: plnr_80yb9yeqh36e — sd show warren-8d78 → "Issue not found"
planRunId: plnr_02qx5me15zxw — sd show warren-3160 → "Issue not found"
planRunId: plnr_8s22dkm84yj1 — sd show warren-383b → "Issue not found"
```

These log every ~10s as `plan_run.noop` (warn level). The child seeds don't exist on the main branch because the bugwatch/nightwatch runs created them on workspace branches that were never merged — a secondary issue, but the agent name bug is the primary problem that prevents the children from doing useful work even if the seeds were found.

## Code Trace

```
triggers.yaml (role: nightwatch/bugwatch)
  → cron tick dispatches run with agent_name = nightwatch/bugwatch
    → run completes, reap detects new plans in .seeds/plans.jsonl
      → reap.ts:613-621 creates PlanRun with agentName: run.agentName  ← BUG
        → coordinator tick picks up the PlanRun
          → dispatch.ts:56 passes agentName: planRun.agentName to spawnRun  ← PROPAGATED
            → child run boots with nightwatch/bugwatch system prompt  ← WRONG
```

## Fix

In `src/runs/reap.ts:616`, the `agentName` for auto_plan_run PlanRuns should NOT inherit from the parent run. Instead:

**Option A (recommended):** Add an `auto_plan_run_agent` field to agent frontmatter that overrides the inherited agent name. Both bugwatch and nightwatch would set `auto_plan_run_agent: "claude-code"`. Falls back to `run.agentName` when unset (backward-compatible).

**Option B:** Resolve `defaultRole` from the project's warren-config (`loadWarrenConfig()`) at reap time. Falls back to `claude-code` when `defaultRole` is not set.

**Option C:** Hardcode `claude-code` as the agent for auto_plan_run children. Simplest but least flexible.

In all cases, `src/plan-runs/dispatch.ts:56` stays unchanged — it correctly reads from `planRun.agentName`, so the fix only needs to land at the PlanRun creation site in reap.

## Files to Change

- `src/runs/reap.ts` (~line 616) — resolve correct agent name for auto_plan_run PlanRuns
- `src/registry/builtins/bugwatch.ts` — add `auto_plan_run_agent` if Option A
- `src/registry/builtins/nightwatch.ts` — add `auto_plan_run_agent` if Option A  
- `src/registry/schema.ts` — add `auto_plan_run_agent` to AgentFrontmatter schema if Option A
- `src/runs/reap.test.ts` — test that auto_plan_run uses the override agent, not the parent

## Secondary Issue

The 3 stuck plan-runs also reveal that plan-run children reference seeds that only exist on the workspace branch (not main). The coordinator's `showSeed` runs against the project's cloned default branch, so `sd show` fails with "Issue not found". This produces an infinite noop loop (every 10s, warn level, indefinitely). The coordinator should either: (a) fail the plan-run after N noop retries, or (b) resolve seeds from the workspace branch the parent run pushed to. Filed separately if desired.

title: Fix auto_plan_run children dispatching before parent PR merges + mirror plans.jsonl
description:
## Problem

Auto_plan_run children (e.g., from bugwatcher/nightwatch) fail because they dispatch against main before the parent agent's PR has merged. The child agent gets `work on sd <child-seed-id>`, but that seed doesn't exist on main — it only exists on the parent's run branch. No commits, empty push, plan-run fails.

This is the second time this has happened in production. v0.6.3 (ba5c652) fixed a related symptom (child runs inheriting the parent's agent name instead of the override), but the deeper issue is that seeds state isn't on main when children dispatch.

## Root Cause (two compounding issues)

### 1. No merge gate before first child dispatch

In `src/runs/reap.ts:614-622`, `repos.planRuns.create()` creates the plan-run with `ref: project.defaultBranch`. The coordinator (`src/plan-runs/coordinator.ts:127`) transitions from `queued → running` immediately on the next 10-second tick and dispatches the first child. But the parent run's PR hasn't merged yet — seeds state (child issues + plans) is on the parent's run branch, not main.

When `spawnRun` runs for the child (`src/plan-runs/dispatch.ts:53`), it refreshes the project clone to main (`src/projects/refresh.ts:173-184`: `git checkout --force main`, `git reset --hard origin/main`). This wipes any local state from `mirrorSeeds` and seeds the child's burrow workspace from main — which has neither the child seeds nor the plans.

### 2. plans.jsonl is never mirrored

`mirrorSeeds` (`src/runs/reap.ts:1320`) only reads `.seeds/issues.jsonl` from the burrow workspace. It does not read `.seeds/plans.jsonl`. Then `stageSeedsForCommit` (`src/runs/reap.ts:1779`) copies BOTH `issues.jsonl` and `plans.jsonl` from the project clone back to the workspace for commit — but since plans.jsonl was never mirrored, it copies the OLD plans.jsonl, overwriting the agent's new plans.

The reap code knows this is a problem — lines 565-568 snapshot the workspace `plans.jsonl` in memory BEFORE the overwrite so plan IDs can be detected for auto-dispatch. But the actual plan data (step descriptions, sections) is lost from git. Even after the parent PR merges, `plans.jsonl` on main won't contain the new plans.

## Timeline of a failing bugwatcher run

```
1. Bugwatcher runs in burrow workspace
   - sd plan submit → writes issues.jsonl + plans.jsonl in workspace
   
2. Reap processes the completed run
   a. Snapshot workspace plans.jsonl (memory only — preserves plan IDs)
   b. mirrorSeeds: workspace issues.jsonl → project clone ✓
      (plans.jsonl NOT mirrored ✗)
   c. stageSeedsForCommit: project clone → workspace
      - issues.jsonl: has children (from mirror) ✓
      - plans.jsonl: OLD version (never mirrored) ✗
   d. git commit + push to warren/run-xxx
   e. auto_plan_run created (ref: main, no parentRunId)
   f. PR created targeting main (auto-merge enabled)
   
3. Coordinator tick (within 10 seconds)
   a. Sees queued plan-run, transitions to running
   b. Dispatches first child immediately
   c. spawnRun refreshes project clone: git checkout --force main
      → wipes mirrorSeeds' local writes
   d. Workspace seeded from main state — no child seeds, no plans
   e. Agent prompt: "work on sd <child-id>"
   f. sd show <child-id> → NOT FOUND
   g. No commits → empty push → child fails → plan-run fails
```

## Fix

Two changes, both required:

### Change 1: Add parentRunId gate to plan-run coordinator

Add a `parentRunId` column to the plan-runs table. When set, the coordinator gates on the parent run's PR being merged before dispatching the first child. This reuses the existing `checkPrMerged` function already wired into the coordinator.

**DB migration** — add nullable column to `planRuns` table:
- `src/db/schema/postgres.ts`: add `parentRunId: text("parent_run_id")` to the `planRuns` table definition
- `src/db/schema/sqlite.ts`: same column addition
- Create migration file for the new column

**Reap** — pass parent run ID when creating auto_plan_run:
- `src/runs/reap.ts:614`: add `parentRunId: run.id` to the `repos.planRuns.create()` call
- `src/db/repos/plan-runs.ts` `CreatePlanRunInput`: add optional `parentRunId?: string` field
- `src/db/repos/plan-runs.ts` `create()`: persist `input.parentRunId ?? null` into the row

**Coordinator** — gate on parent PR merge before first child dispatch:
- `src/plan-runs/coordinator.ts`, at the top of `advancePlanRun`, after the `queued → running` transition (line 132-135), add:
  - If `planRun.parentRunId` is set, look up the parent run via `repos.runs.get(planRun.parentRunId)`
  - If the parent run has no `prUrl`, check for an `empty_push` event (trivial merge, same pattern as `handleInFlight` line 344-359) — if trivial, proceed
  - If the parent run has a `prUrl`, call `checkPrMerged(parentRun.prUrl)`
  - If not yet merged, return `{ kind: "waiting_for_merge" }` (or a new `"waiting_for_parent_merge"` kind)
  - If merged, proceed — the gate is passed, and subsequent ticks skip straight to child dispatch
  - If closed-unmerged or fatal HTTP error, fail the plan-run with reason `"parent_pr_not_merged"`
- Consider caching the gate result: once the parent PR is confirmed merged, the check doesn't need to repeat on every tick. Options: (a) null out parentRunId after gate passes, (b) add a `parentMergedAt` timestamp, or (c) just let it re-poll (the HTTP call is cheap and the state is terminal). Option (c) is simplest and consistent with how child PR polling works.

**Tests:**
- `src/plan-runs/coordinator.test.ts`: add cases for parentRunId gate — waiting, merged, closed-unmerged, trivial (no PR + empty_push), no parentRunId (existing behavior unchanged)

### Change 2: Extend mirrorSeeds to handle plans.jsonl

`mirrorSeeds` (`src/runs/reap.ts:1320`) currently only reads `.seeds/issues.jsonl` from the burrow workspace. Extend it to also read and merge `.seeds/plans.jsonl`.

**Implementation in `src/runs/reap.ts`:**
- Add a `mirrorPlans` step (or extend `mirrorSeeds` to accept a list of files). The merge logic for plans.jsonl is simpler than issues: plans are append-only (new rows from workspace that don't exist in project). Use the same pattern as issues — parse JSONL, index by `id`, add rows whose `id` is absent from the project baseline.
- The `parsePlans` helper can mirror the existing `parseSeeds` shape: `{ id, raw }` — plans rows need `id` for dedup and `raw` for lossless round-trip.
- Call this step alongside or inside `mirrorSeeds`, before `stageSeedsForCommit`.
- After this change, `stageSeedsForCommit`'s project→workspace copy for `plans.jsonl` will carry the merged (new) plans, not the old baseline.

**Cleanup:**
- The snapshot-before-overwrite code at lines 565-583 is still useful for detecting new plan IDs, but the overwrite concern documented in the comment ("which copies project→workspace and may overwrite the agent's version") is resolved — the project clone now has the mirrored plans.
- Update the comment at lines 565-568 to reflect the new reality.

**Tests:**
- `src/runs/reap.test.ts`: add a case where the workspace contains a new plan in plans.jsonl that the project baseline doesn't have — verify it appears in the project clone after mirrorSeeds and in the workspace after stageSeedsForCommit.

## Acceptance criteria

1. A bugwatcher run that creates plans and child seeds via `sd plan submit` results in a plan-run whose first child does NOT dispatch until the bugwatcher's own PR is merged to main.
2. After the bugwatcher PR merges, the coordinator's next tick dispatches the first child against main, and the child's workspace contains both the child seeds in `issues.jsonl` AND the parent plan in `plans.jsonl`.
3. The child agent can run `sd show <child-seed-id>` and `sd plan show <plan-id>` successfully.
4. Existing manual plan-runs (no parentRunId) are unaffected — no gate check, immediate dispatch.
5. If the parent PR is closed without merging, the plan-run fails with a clear reason.
6. `bun test && bun run lint && bun run typecheck` pass.

title: Add 'Sync all' button to the /projects page
description:
Add a bulk-refresh button to the projects list page that syncs all registered GitHub repos in one click.

## Context

RefreshProjectsCTA (src/ui/src/components/RefreshProjectsCTA.tsx) already implements the exact behavior needed: it fans out projectsApi.refresh(id) across every registered project in parallel, handles loading/error/success states, and accepts a custom label prop. It is currently only surfaced on /plots empty-state and /plots/:id 404 pages.

## Implementation

In src/ui/src/pages/Projects.tsx, import RefreshProjectsCTA and place it in the CardHeader of the projects table card, next to the project count title:

1. Import RefreshProjectsCTA from @/components/RefreshProjectsCTA.ts
2. Override CardHeader to use flex-row layout so the title and button sit side-by-side:
   - Add className="flex flex-row items-center justify-between" to CardHeader
3. Add <RefreshProjectsCTA label="Sync all" /> after the CardTitle

No new API endpoints, mutations, or server changes needed — the existing component and projectsApi.refresh() cover everything.

title: ux-whisper: UI telemetry agent that proposes QoL tweaks from usage patterns
description:
A custom canopy agent (not a built-in) that reads UI telemetry data and proposes small QoL improvements as seed plans.

## Overview

ux-whisper observes how the operator actually uses the warren UI — click patterns, page dwell times, filter usage, navigation flows — and proposes micro-tweaks as seeds. Approved proposals auto-dispatch via plan-run. The agent runs weekly on a cron trigger.

## Infrastructure required

### UI telemetry collection
- New `useTelemetry()` React hook wired once in Layout.tsx (~50 LOC)
- Captures: clicks on buttons/links, page dwell via useEffect on route changes, filter/sort usage by instrumenting existing state setters
- Events POST to a new `/api/telemetry` endpoint

### Server
- `POST /telemetry` — record UI events (batch-friendly, fire-and-forget from client)
- `GET /telemetry/analytics` — aggregated stats for the agent to consume. Follows the existing `/analytics/cost` handler pattern in src/server/handlers.ts

### Database
- New `ui_telemetry` table (separate from run events): id, ts, event_kind (click|dwell|filter|navigate), page_route, event_data (JSON)
- New `UiTelemetryRepo` with append(), aggregateByKind(), listByPageRoute()
- Add to both sqlite.ts and postgres.ts schemas (drift test auto-validates parity)

### Agent (custom canopy role, NOT a built-in)
- Lives in warren's `.canopy/` as a project-tier role, not in src/registry/builtins/
- auto_plan_run: true so findings auto-dispatch as fixes
- System prompt instructs it to: fetch 7 days of telemetry via warren API, identify friction patterns (high dwell + low interaction, filters always set the same way, clicks on disabled elements, users always expanding the same panel first), and file small seed plans
- Schedule: weekly cron trigger in .warren/triggers.yaml

### What it looks for
- Pages with high dwell but low interaction (friction/confusion)
- Filters used >80% of visits (should be sticky or default)
- Click patterns on disabled/absent elements (impossible UX)
- Navigation flows showing users lost (unclear labeling)
- Panels always manually expanded (should be default-expanded)
- Features never touched (candidates for removal or better discoverability)

### What it does NOT do
- No nightly overhauls — only small, single-component tweaks
- No architecture changes, no new pages, no dependency additions
- Does not implement fixes directly — creates plans for a coding agent to execute

## Estimated effort
~400 LOC (hook + server endpoints + schema + repo + canopy role definition)
closeReason: Operator decision: ux-whisper UI telemetry agent — dormant feature proposal, not being pursued. Closing per human triage.

title: meta-watch: cross-project agent that diagnoses systemic friction from run history
description:
A custom canopy agent (not a built-in) that analyzes run history across all warren projects, identifies recurring patterns of failure or friction, and files targeted fix seeds in the appropriate repo.

## Overview

meta-watch is nightwatch for the agent system itself. Instead of scanning one repo's code, it scans the aggregate run/event/plan-run data across all registered projects and looks for systemic issues — tools that break everywhere, agents that consistently fail at the same step, error patterns that span repos.

## Key design constraint: cross-project data access

Current agents (nightwatch, bugwatch) run in a burrow sandbox with one project's clone. meta-watch needs to see ALL projects' run history. Two paths:

### Path A — warren-internal scheduled routine (recommended)
- Runs as a scheduled tick inside warren's server process (like plan-run coordinator or pause detector)
- Queries the shared DB directly via repos: runs.listAll(), events.listByRunIds(), planRunChildren queries
- When it finds a pattern, dispatches a targeted child run per-project to file seeds
- More powerful, direct DB access, but requires server-side code

### Path B — custom canopy agent with API access
- Runs as a regular sandboxed agent with network: "open"
- Warren injects its own API URL as an env var
- Agent queries GET /runs, GET /analytics/cost, GET /projects over HTTP
- Simpler to build (just a canopy role), but limited to what the HTTP API exposes
- May need new API endpoints for cross-project aggregation

### Recommendation
Start with Path B (canopy agent + HTTP API) for v1. It's simpler, fits the existing agent model, and pressure-tests the API surface. Graduate to Path A if the agent needs deeper DB access (event-level pattern matching, plan-run-child step analysis).

## What it detects

- **Agent-level failures**: "nightwatch fails 80% of the time on seeds repo" → file a seed in seeds to investigate
- **Failure reason clustering**: no_model_response spikes across projects → file a seed about API reliability / retry logic
- **Plan-step patterns**: "step 3 of every nightwatch plan-run fails" → file a seed about the tool/pattern that step uses
- **Tool breakage across repos**: mulch's ml record failing in 4/5 projects → file a seed in mulch's repo with the error patterns as evidence
- **Cost anomalies**: one project's runs suddenly 3x more expensive → file a seed to investigate prompt/context bloat
- **Worker health** (post R-12): failure rate per burrow worker

## Infrastructure required

### API additions (for Path B)
- `GET /analytics/runs` — cross-project run aggregation: failure rates by agent, by project, by time window
- `GET /analytics/patterns` — pre-computed pattern detection: recurring failure reasons, step-failure correlations
- These follow the existing /analytics/cost pattern in handlers.ts

### Agent (custom canopy role, NOT a built-in)
- Lives in warren's .canopy/ as a project-tier role
- auto_plan_run: true with auto_plan_run_agent: "pi"
- Schedule: weekly or twice-weekly cron trigger in .warren/triggers.yaml
- System prompt instructs it to query the analytics endpoints, identify patterns exceeding thresholds, and file seeds in the appropriate project repo

### Cross-repo seed filing
- The agent runs against warren's repo but needs to file seeds in OTHER repos
- v1: agent files seeds in warren with labels indicating the target repo; human routes them
- v2: warren API endpoint `POST /projects/:id/seeds` that proxies sd create into any registered project's clone

## Data already available (no new tables needed)
- runs table: state, failureReason, agentName, projectId, costUsd, startedAt, endedAt
- events table: kind, stream, payloadJson per run (fan out via listByRunIds)
- plan_run_children table: seedId, seq, state, failureReason per plan-run step
- All in a single shared DB with projectId as a column — cross-project queries are trivial

## Estimated effort
Path B (canopy agent): ~150 LOC for the role + ~200 LOC for analytics API endpoints = ~350 LOC
Path A (server-side): ~400 LOC for the scheduled routine + analysis + child dispatch

title: taste-gate: proposal queue where agents suggest and humans curate with learnable feedback
description:
A proposal system + custom canopy agent (not a built-in) that creates a human-in-the-loop curation layer for agent-suggested changes. Agents propose instead of shipping directly; the human approves or rejects; decisions are recorded in mulch so proposing agents learn taste over time.

## Overview

taste-gate solves the "agents lack taste" problem. Instead of agents shipping features/changes directly (risking vision drift), they submit proposals to a queue. The human operator reviews proposals in the warren UI — approve dispatches a run, reject records feedback. Over time, mulch accumulates approval/rejection patterns, and proposing agents adjust what they suggest.

## Infrastructure required

### Seeds as the proposal store (zero schema migration)
- Seeds extensions already use .passthrough() (SeedExtensionsSchema in src/seeds-cli/schema.ts)
- Proposing agents file seeds with extensions: { proposal: "pending", proposed_by: "<agent-name>", proposed_at: "<iso>" }
- Approval updates to: { proposal: "approved", approved_at: "<iso>", approval_feedback: "..." }
- Rejection updates to: { proposal: "rejected", rejected_at: "<iso>", rejection_reason: "..." }
- No new tables, no migrations — works today with existing seeds infrastructure

### Server endpoints (two new handlers)
- `POST /proposals/:seed_id/approve` — reads seed via showSeed(), extracts prompt/agent from seed body, dispatches run via spawnRun() with seedId linked, updates seed extensions. Returns { run, seed }.
- `POST /proposals/:seed_id/reject` — updates seed extensions with rejection reason. Returns { seed }.
- Both added to ROUTE_TABLE in src/server/handlers.ts alongside existing routes

### UI (new Proposals page)
- New `src/ui/src/pages/Proposals.tsx` — table of seeds where extensions.proposal === "pending"
  - Columns: title, proposing agent, proposed-at, project, status
  - Per-row actions: Approve button, Reject button, View seed link
- Approve modal: shows proposal details, optional feedback textarea ("What convinced you?"), "Approve & Dispatch" button
- Reject modal: optional rejection reason, "Reject" button
- New nav item in Layout.tsx with pending-count badge (follows the existing Plots needs-attention pattern)
- New route in App.tsx: /proposals
- New API client wrapper: src/ui/src/api/proposalsApi.ts

### Mulch feedback loop (the learning mechanism)
- On reap (run completion), if the run was dispatched from a proposal seed:
  - Write a proposal_decision mulch record with type "decision"
  - Include: proposal title, proposing agent, decision (approved+succeeded, approved+failed), human feedback
  - Record lives in .mulch/expertise/ alongside other project expertise
- Proposing agents read mulch on next run (via ml prime) and see the pattern:
  - "Small UI tweaks get approved, architecture rewrites get rejected"
  - "Changes with test plans get approved more often"
  - "Agent X's proposals have a 70% approval rate, agent Y's have 20%"
- This is the taste signal — the human's curation patterns become learnable context

### Custom canopy agents that propose (NOT built-ins)
- Any custom agent can become a proposer by filing seeds with the proposal extension
- Example: a "brainstorm" role that reads the codebase + mulch expertise and proposes improvements
- Example: a "feature-scout" role that reads GitHub issues/discussions and proposes implementations
- The taste-gate infrastructure is agent-agnostic — any role can propose, the approval flow is the same
- Proposing roles live in .canopy/ as project-tier custom roles

## What taste-gate does NOT include (v1)
- No auto-merge — approval means "dispatch the run," human still reviews the PR
- No interactive run pauses for proposals — simpler than the existing question_posed/answered system
- No sophisticated ML on approval patterns — agents just read mulch records naturally
- No cross-project proposals — each proposal targets one project

## Future extensions
- Auto-merge for proposals with high historical approval rate from the same agent
- Proposal templates: structured formats that different agent types fill in
- Approval delegation: "auto-approve proposals from agent X if they touch only test files"
- Cross-project proposals via colonies (R-20)

## Estimated effort
~750 LOC total:
- Server: 2 handlers (~200 LOC)
- UI: 1 page + modals (~400 LOC)
- API client + types (~100 LOC)
- Mulch recording in reap (~50 LOC)

Eight files were grandfathered via biome.json overrides for the function rule, and 48 files are pinned in scripts/file-size-budgets.json above the threshold. title: Add post-reap burrow workspace cleanup to prevent unbounded disk growth
description:
## Problem

Warren provisions a fresh burrow per run (POST /burrows) but never destroys it after reap completes. Burrow workspaces under /data/burrow/projects/*/workspaces/bur_* accumulate indefinitely. On the deployed instance (Fly, 50GB volume), 122+ stale workspaces consumed 47GB (100% disk), producing:

- SQLITE_FULL (errno 13) on burrow's db.sqlite — blocked run cancellation
- SQLITE_IOERR_SHMSIZE (errno 4874) on project .plot/ SQLite files — broke the plots aggregation endpoint
- Stuck run with burrow socket timeouts on every stream bridge reconnect

Root cause: reapRun (src/runs/reap.ts) copies results out of the workspace (mulch merge, seeds close, branch push, PR open, plot merge) but never calls DELETE /burrows/:id to destroy the workspace afterward. Burrow's destroy flow (burrow src/lib/destroy.ts → destroyBurrowFully) handles everything needed: stop → remove workspace (worktree + branch) → archive events → mark destroyed. It's just never called.

## Proposed Fix

Add a new reap sub-step "workspace_destroy" as the final step in reapRun, after all data has been extracted and the branch pushed:

1. Call burrowClient.http.burrows.destroy(run.burrowId, { archive: true }) via withTransportMapping
2. Wrap in the same best-effort fail() pattern as other reap sub-steps — a destroy failure should not block the run state transition
3. Emit a reap.workspace_destroyed event on success with { burrowId, workspaceRemoved, archived }
4. Log the outcome alongside the other reap telemetry fields in the "reap completed" message
5. Surface workspaceDestroyed: boolean in the ReapResult return type

### Considerations

- Preview runs: when previewState is "starting" or "live", the workspace is still needed by the preview sidecar. Skip destroy when preview is active; the preview eviction worker should destroy on teardown instead.
- Interactive runs: mode="interactive" runs may respawn — skip destroy for those too. Only destroy batch runs in terminal state.
- The burrow HTTP client already exposes destroy(): burrow src/lib/http-client.ts:413 (DELETE /burrows/:id with optional archive param). Warren's facade (src/burrow-client/client.ts) can call it via burrowClient.http.burrows.destroy(id).
- Warren's burrows table (src/db/schema/sqlite.ts) should be updated after destroy — either delete the row or mark it destroyed so clientFor() routing doesn't try to contact a dead workspace.

### Fallback GC

Even with per-reap cleanup, edge cases (warren crash mid-reap, stuck runs force-killed) can strand workspaces. A periodic background GC is worth considering as a follow-up:

- Walk the burrows table for rows whose runs are terminal and older than a configurable TTL
- Call destroy on each
- Or: a new warren doctor check that reports stale burrow workspaces with a recovery hint

## Key Files

- src/runs/reap.ts — reapRun function, add workspace_destroy after line ~935 (after reap.completed emit, before broker.close)
- src/burrow-client/client.ts — facade for burrow HTTP calls; destroy available via .http.burrows.destroy(id)
- burrow src/lib/destroy.ts — destroyBurrowFully (stop → workspace remove → archive → mark destroyed)
- burrow src/lib/http-client.ts:413 — DELETE /burrows/:id handler
- src/server/bridges.ts — where reapRun is called after terminal state detection

## Incident Context

Production incident 2026-05-27: 50GB Fly volume at 100% from 122 stale workspaces. Manually deleted all workspaces and restarted. See commit history for timeline.

title: PR-fixer agent: auto-dispatch on CI failure (polling, V1)
description:
<!-- seeds:plan-backref:start -->
Adopted into plan pl-df2f.

Parent seed: warren-4b01 — Batch: clear medium/low open backlog
Plan template: feature

Run `sd plan show pl-df2f` for the full plan (context, alternatives, sibling steps, acceptance criteria).
<!-- seeds:plan-backref:end -->

## Summary

Add a built-in `pr-fixer` agent that automatically dispatches when a
warren-opened PR's CI fails. Uses polling (not webhooks) so it ships on
V1 infrastructure — no webhook receiver, no signature verification, no
GitHub App auth (R-18).

## Motivation

Warren already opens PRs after runs complete, but if CI fails the human
must intervene. A polling-based fixer closes the loop: warren watches its
own PRs, detects check-run failures, fetches the failure logs, and
dispatches a fixer agent that pushes to the existing PR branch.

## Design

### Trigger: CI-status poller

Model after plan-run's PR-merge poller (`src/plan-runs/pr-merge.ts`).
A new tick function runs on the scheduler's existing 60s cadence (or a
dedicated tick configurable via `WARREN_CI_POLLER_TICK_MS`).

Each tick:
1. Query `runs` table for terminal runs (`succeeded`) that have a
   non-null `prUrl` and whose PR is still open.
2. For each, call `GET /repos/:owner/:repo/commits/:ref/check-runs`
   (GitHub REST API, same `GITHUB_TOKEN` auth pattern as `src/runs/pr.ts`).
3. If all check-runs are `completed` and any has `conclusion: failure`
   (or `timed_out`, `action_required`), mark the run as fix-eligible.
4. Dispatch a `pr-fixer` run targeting the same project, with the
   existing PR branch (not a fresh `prefix/run_xxx` branch).

Key files:
- New: `src/ci-fixer/poller.ts` — tick function, GitHub check-run fetch
- New: `src/ci-fixer/dispatch.ts` — decides whether to dispatch, builds prompt
- Extend: `src/triggers/tick.ts` — wire ci-fixer tick into scheduler loop

### Agent: `pr-fixer` built-in

New built-in agent in `src/registry/builtins/pr-fixer.ts`. System prompt
instructs the agent to:
1. Read the CI failure logs (injected into the dispatch prompt).
2. Identify the root cause (type errors, lint violations, test failures).
3. Apply minimal, targeted fixes.
4. Run the project's quality gates locally before committing.
5. Commit and let warren push to the existing branch.

The agent does NOT open a new PR — it pushes to the branch that already
has an open PR, so the CI re-runs automatically.

### Branch handling: push to existing PR branch

Today `composeRunBranch()` always creates `${prefix}/${runId}`. The fixer
needs to target the original run's branch. Options:
- Add an optional `targetBranch` field to the spawn payload; when set,
  skip branch creation and check out that branch instead.
- The reap flow already pushes; just ensure it pushes to `targetBranch`
  rather than composing a new name.

Key files:
- Modify: `src/runs/branch.ts` — support `targetBranch` override
- Modify: `src/runs/spawn.ts` — pass `targetBranch` when dispatching a fixer
- Modify: `src/runs/reap.ts` — push to existing branch, skip PR open step

### CI log extraction

Before dispatching, the poller fetches the failing check-run's log:
- `GET /repos/:owner/:repo/actions/jobs/:job_id/logs` (GitHub Actions)
- Truncate to last N lines (configurable, default 200) to stay within
  prompt budget.
- Inject as a fenced code block in the dispatch prompt.

Fallback: if the check-run isn't a GitHub Actions job (third-party CI),
include just the check-run name + conclusion + `details_url` and instruct
the agent to diagnose from the codebase.

### Guard rails

- **Max retries per PR:** configurable via `.warren/config.yaml`
  (`ciFixer.maxRetries`, default 2). Track fix attempts in a new
  `ci_fix_attempts` column on the `runs` table (or a lightweight
  `ci_fixes` join table keyed by `prUrl`).
- **Cooldown:** don't re-poll a PR for fixes until N minutes after the
  last fixer run completed (default 10min). Prevents rapid-fire loops.
- **Opt-in per project:** gated on `.warren/config.yaml` having a
  `ciFixer.enabled: true` block. Off by default.
- **Skip manual PRs:** only fix PRs that warren itself opened (matched
  via `runs.prUrl`).

### Config schema addition

```yaml
# .warren/config.yaml
ciFixer:
  enabled: true          # opt-in, default false
  maxRetries: 2          # per PR, default 2
  cooldownMinutes: 10    # between fix attempts, default 10
  logTailLines: 200      # lines of CI log to inject, default 200
  role: pr-fixer         # agent to dispatch, default pr-fixer
```

Schema in `src/warren-config/schema.ts`, validated by `loadWarrenConfig()`.

### DB changes

- `runs` table: add `parentRunId TEXT` (nullable FK to `runs.id`) — links
  a fixer run back to the original run whose PR it's fixing. Also useful
  for future retry/chain patterns.
- New index: `idx_runs_pr_url` on `runs.prUrl` for fast lookups.

### Event emission

System events on the existing `events` table:
- `ci_fixer.check_failed` — CI failure detected, dispatch eligible
- `ci_fixer.dispatched` — fixer run spawned
- `ci_fixer.skipped_cooldown` — skipped due to cooldown
- `ci_fixer.skipped_max_retries` — skipped, retry limit reached
- `ci_fixer.succeeded` — fixer run completed, CI re-running

## Out of scope (V2)

- Webhook-based trigger (`kind: webhook` on `check_run.completed`)
- GitHub App auth (R-18) for higher rate limits
- Check-run status annotations (creating warren check-runs on the PR)
- Configurable failure filters (e.g., only fix lint, not test failures)
- UI page for ci-fixer activity

## Test plan

- Unit: poller correctly identifies failing check-runs from mock API responses
- Unit: dispatch skips when cooldown active or max retries exceeded
- Unit: `composeRunBranch` returns `targetBranch` when provided
- Integration: spawn a fixer run against a mock project with a known CI failure
- Acceptance: end-to-end scenario in `scripts/acceptance/scenarios/`
closeReason: Shipped via PR #235 (merged 2026-05-29): src/ci-fixer/ (check-runs.ts, dispatch.ts) + pr-fixer builtin agent + ciFixer config schema

title: warren status returns raw HTML on unknown run_id instead of structured JSON error
description:
## Problem

When a client requests the status of a run with an unknown `run_id`, the server may return raw HTML (the SPA `index.html` shell) instead of a structured JSON error envelope. This breaks API consumers that expect `application/json` on every code path.

## Root cause

The dispatch logic in `src/server/server.ts:191-240` has a SPA fallback that fires for any unmatched `GET` request. The API route `GET /runs/:id` (pattern `^/runs/([^/]+)$`) *does* match arbitrary IDs and correctly throws `NotFoundError` → JSON 404. However, closely-related paths that fall outside the route table — or edge cases where the route match silently fails — bypass the API error handler and land in the SPA fallback (`createUiHandler`), which serves `index.html`.

The `isApiPath` guard in `src/server/ui.ts:63` catches known API prefixes and returns a JSON 404, but any gap between what `isApiPath` recognises and what `matchRoute` covers opens a window where HTML leaks to an API caller.

### Likely trigger paths

- Sub-paths like `GET /runs/<id>/status` that don't match any registered pattern fall to the SPA fallback; `isApiPath` catches `/runs/` so these *should* get JSON 404, but the envelope differs from the handler-thrown `NotFoundError` shape (`no route matches ...` vs `run not found: ...`).
- If a reverse proxy or load balancer rewrites the path before it reaches warren, the pattern match may fail while `isApiPath` still returns false.
- Direct browser navigation to `/runs/<unknown-id>` returns the SPA shell (HTML) because the route *matches* `GET /runs/:id`, the handler throws `NotFoundError`, and the catch block returns JSON — but if the browser `Accept` header isn't honoured, the JSON body renders as a raw page.

## Expected behaviour

Every response on an API prefix (`/runs`, `/agents`, `/projects`, etc.) should return `Content-Type: application/json` regardless of whether the resource exists. Unknown `run_id` should produce:

```json
{ "error": { "code": "not_found", "message": "run not found: <id>" } }
```

with HTTP 404 and `content-type: application/json`.

## Suggested fix

1. Move the `isApiPath` guard from the UI handler into the main dispatch fallback in `server.ts` (before the SPA fallback fires), so API-prefix paths *never* reach `createUiHandler`.
2. Ensure the guard returns the same `renderError(NotFoundError)` envelope the handler-level catch uses, for consistency.
3. Add a test in `src/server/server.test.ts` that asserts `GET /runs/<unknown-id>` returns JSON 404 with `content-type: application/json` (not `text/html`).

## Files involved

- `src/server/server.ts` — dispatch + SPA fallback (lines 191-246)
- `src/server/ui.ts` — `createUiHandler` + `isApiPath` guard (lines 56-82)
- `src/server/handlers.ts` — `isApiPath` / `API_PREFIXES` (lines 4173-4198)
- `src/server/errors.ts` — `renderError` / `notFound` (lines 71-97)

title: CI-postgres: schema migration ordering fails — relation "public.projects" does not exist
description:
The CI (Postgres) workflow fails on every PR branch with:

```
error: Failed query:
ALTER TABLE "agents" ADD CONSTRAINT "agents_project_id_projects_id_fk"
  FOREIGN KEY ("project_id") REFERENCES "public"."projects"("id")
  ON DELETE cascade ON UPDATE no action;

error: relation "public.projects" does not exist
```

138 postgres-dialect tests fail. The drizzle push/migrate step tries to add a foreign key on `agents.project_id` referencing `projects`, but the `projects` table hasn't been created yet. Confirmed identical failure on two independent PR branches (run_9vc128s4zhfx, run_587z0gsqbnpk). CI-postgres has never run on main (no recorded runs), so this may have been broken since the workflow was introduced.

Likely cause: migration ordering or drizzle push-based schema setup doesn't guarantee table creation order in the postgres test harness.

title: Pre-commit hooks don't fire + coding agents skip quality gates before committing
description:
Warren plan-runs stall when agent PRs fail CI, because auto-merge can't proceed. This has happened twice in one night (PR #132: AGENTS.md path validation + bundle-size budget mismatch).

Root cause: two missing guardrails.

**1. Pre-commit hooks are not firing.**
`scripts/hooks/pre-commit` exists and runs lint, typecheck, validate:agents-md, check:file-sizes, check:debt-markers. The `bun install` postinstall copies it to `.git/hooks/pre-commit`. But the hook never fires — neither locally for the operator nor in deployed warren burrow sandboxes. Investigate why (permissions? hook path? bun's git integration?) and fix.

**2. Coding agent prompts don't instruct running quality gates.**
The built-in agent definitions (claude-code, pi) tell agents 'warren handles the push' but never instruct them to run `bun run check:all` (or the project's equivalent) before committing. Agents should be told to run the project's quality gates before every commit and fix any failures before finishing. CLAUDE.md says 'run check:all before committing' but agent system prompts don't reinforce this.

**Fix plan:**
- Debug and fix pre-commit hook installation so it actually fires in both local dev and burrow sandboxes
- Update claude-code and pi agent prompts to mandate running quality gates before committing
- Consider adding a `qualityGate` field to `.warren/config.yaml` so the agent prompt can reference the project-specific command

title: Pi agent_end with provider error (overloaded/529) incorrectly maps to succeeded
description:
## Problem

When the Anthropic API returns an overloaded_error (529), the pi runtime emits an `agent_end` event with clear error signals — `stopReason: "error"`, `errorMessage` containing `overloaded_error`, 0 tokens, empty content array — but warren finalizes the run as `succeeded`.

Observed on run `run_s7vhgvkvc1v6` (2026-05-27, plan-run trigger). The run lasted 7 seconds, produced zero model output, opened a PR with only stale branch state, and reported success. The plan-run machinery then waited for merge and advanced.

## Root cause

`detectRuntimeTerminal()` in `src/runs/stream.ts:508` maps `agent_end` → `"succeeded"` unconditionally:

```ts
if (env.type === "agent_end") return "succeeded";
```

It never inspects the payload for error indicators. The claude-code path (line 507) at least checks `is_error`, but the pi path has no equivalent guard.

Because the outcome reaches reap as `"succeeded"`, reap never calls `inferFailureReason()` (line 902-905 of reap.ts only infers when `outcome === "failed"`), so no safety net catches it.

## Evidence from the run

The `agent_end` payload contained:
- `stopReason: "error"`
- `errorMessage: '{"type":"error","error":{"type":"overloaded_error","message":"Overloaded"}}'`
- `usage: { input: 0, output: 0, totalTokens: 0 }`
- `content: []`
- `willRetry: true` (pi intended to retry but exited anyway)

## Fix direction

`detectRuntimeTerminal()` should inspect pi's `agent_end` payload for error signals and return `"failed"` when the agent exited due to a provider error. Signals to check:

1. Last message in `messages` array has `stopReason: "error"`
2. `errorMessage` is present and non-empty
3. Total tokens are 0 / content array is empty (no work was done)

Additionally, reap could add a safety-net check: if outcome is `"succeeded"` but the event log contains zero stdout text/thinking/tool_use events, downgrade to `"failed"` with `failure_reason: "no_model_response"`. This would catch similar bugs for any runtime.

## Files

- `src/runs/stream.ts:501-510` — `detectRuntimeTerminal()`
- `src/runs/reap.ts:902-914` — failure reason inference (only runs on failed outcome)
- `src/runs/reap.ts:2020-2033` — `inferFailureReason()`
closeReason: Fixed: detectRuntimeTerminal (src/runs/stream/terminal-detect.ts) maps agent_end with stopReason=error / errorMessage to failed; PR #167 (fbe60a2) merged, test PR #168 asserts overloaded_error 529 -> failed

title: Make agent quality-gate parity with CI structural (not prompt-dependent)
description: Recent PR first-pass CI failures (10 in the last 30 PRs) all stem from agents committing work that the local quality gate accepted but CI rejected. Two failure classes: (1) ci-postgres migration FK qualifier (public.X) -- never executed by check:all because no local postgres matrix exists; (2) validate:agents-md path references to gitignored build artifacts -- covered by check:all but bypassed when agent skips the gate. Root cause is parity drift: check:all, scripts/hooks/pre-commit, and .github/workflows/ci.yml have diverged, .warren/config.yaml does not set qualityGate, and the built-in agent contract treats green as advisory rather than terminal. Goal: bun run check:all == CI green, enforced by a drift detector, set as this repo's WARREN_QUALITY_GATE, and reframed in agent prompts so a red gate blocks finishing the run. See plan for sequenced steps.

bun run check:coverage fails at branch tip 964bfb3e (chore: close warren-a2b4) with: 'functions 82.72% (floor 85.00%), lines 90.06% (floor 90.00%)'. Reproduced on a clean checkout (git stash; bun run check:coverage; git stash pop). The drop is pre-existing relative to the warren-599c close work and predates this branch's untracked changes. Likely cause: recent reap/stream/spawn/handlers subdirectory splits (warren-ea0c, warren-041e, warren-f71c, warren-599c) moved code into modules whose function coverage is not exercised the same way as before. Need to either restore the missing function coverage (preferred — add tests to lift back above 85%) or document the legitimate drop and lower the floor in scripts/coverage-budgets.json with a tracker-referenced rationale (per warren-e4b1 ratchet protocol — only goes up). Investigate which functions are uncovered via 'bun run test:coverage' lcov output and target the gap.
closeReason: Moot: cited ephemeral run branch warren/run_wr25e029s0f3 is gone; current coverage floors (functions 87.09 / lines 90.32) sit well above the 85% the bug flagged. Per maintainer.

title: Release workflow should auto-start Fly machine after crash-loop stop
description:
<!-- seeds:plan-backref:start -->
Adopted into plan pl-df2f.

Parent seed: warren-4b01 — Batch: clear medium/low open backlog
Plan template: feature

Run `sd plan show pl-df2f` for the full plan (context, alternatives, sibling steps, acceptance criteria).
<!-- seeds:plan-backref:end -->

When v0.7.0 deployed with a broken server entry path, the Fly machine crash-looped 10 times and entered 'stopped' state. The v0.7.1 hotfix deployed successfully (Fly release v60, status 'complete') but the machine stayed stopped — required a manual 'fly machine start' to recover. The release workflow (.github/workflows/release.yml) should detect when the machine is stopped after deploy and either auto-start it or fail loudly so the operator knows the deploy didn't actually bring the app back up.

title: reap seeds_commit blocked by repo pre-commit hook running full check:all gauntlet
description:
<!-- seeds:plan-backref:start -->
Adopted into plan pl-369d.

Parent seed: warren-8104 — Release-train batch: notable Medium + small quality wins
Plan template: feature
Plan approach: Submit minimal placeholders, release+close them, adopt the work seeds in execution order, adopt a release seed last so plan-run gates each on the previous PR merge and ends with a patch release.

Run `sd plan show pl-369d` for the full plan (context, alternatives, sibling steps, acceptance criteria).
<!-- seeds:plan-backref:end -->

Observed in run_cveqctvdjvfd (warren-dogfood / plan-run on warren-332b). At reap, warren's own bookkeeping commit failed:

  git -c user.name=warren -c user.email=warren@os-eco.dev commit -m 'chore(warren): seeds state'
  -> pre-commit hook fired: bun run check:coverage && lint && typecheck && ... && check:ci-parity
  -> failed at check:coverage, aborting the commit (reap_failed, step=seeds_commit)

Root cause: this repo has a git pre-commit hook that runs the entire check:all suite on EVERY commit, including warren's internal reap-time commits (seeds state, mulch, plot mirroring). Warren's bookkeeping commits should not be gated by the project's quality hooks.

Impact: warren's reap-time commits (seeds_commit, and likely mulch/plot commits) silently fail on any repo with a strict pre-commit hook. Also means agent-authored commits on such repos are hostile-by-default regardless of model.

Proposed fix: warren should pass --no-verify (and likely -c core.hooksPath=/dev/null) on its host-side reap commits so project hooks never block internal bookkeeping. Audit all reap-time git commit invocations. Decide separately whether agent-authored commits inside the sandbox should also bypass hooks or surface the hook failure to the agent.

title: harden against empty pushes: detect, surface, and reduce silent no-commit runs
description:
<!-- seeds:plan-backref:start -->
Adopted into plan pl-369d.

Parent seed: warren-8104 — Release-train batch: notable Medium + small quality wins
Plan template: feature
Plan approach: Submit minimal placeholders, release+close them, adopt the work seeds in execution order, adopt a release seed last so plan-run gates each on the previous PR merge and ends with a patch release.

Run `sd plan show pl-369d` for the full plan (context, alternatives, sibling steps, acceptance criteria).
<!-- seeds:plan-backref:end -->

Observed in run_cveqctvdjvfd: gemini-3.5-flash (pi agent) did the work, got check:all green, ran git add -A, narrated 'before committing' — then the session ended WITHOUT ever running git commit. Reap emitted reap.empty_push ('git push exited zero but the branch landed no new commits — agent did not commit', commitsAhead=0), yet the run is recorded as state=succeeded with pr_url=null.

Problem: an empty push currently looks like success. There's no failure_reason, no signal in the run state, and on plan-runs it auto-merged (plan_run.merged trivial=true) and advanced — so a no-op run silently closes out a plan child. Empty pushes ARE occasionally legitimate (agent correctly determines no change needed), but that's rare; the common case is the agent dropping the final commit step (esp. weaker models like Gemini Flash).

Investigate / consider:
- Distinguish 'legitimately nothing to do' from 'agent failed to commit'. Heuristic: did the agent make file edits / git add staged changes but never commit? (events show write/edit/git add but 0 git commit -> almost certainly a dropped commit, not a deliberate no-op.)
- When commitsAhead=0 but the workspace had staged/dirty changes at reap, mark the run failed (or a distinct 'empty' / 'no_commit' state) with failure_reason, instead of succeeded.
- For plan-runs: do NOT auto-merge/advance on an empty push that looks like a dropped commit — surface it so the plan doesn't silently skip a child.
- Possible mitigation: reap-time fallback that auto-commits staged-but-uncommitted changes (with --no-verify, see warren-27d3) when the agent clearly intended to commit. Decide if this is desirable or too magic.
- Strengthen the pi/Gemini agent prompt's commit step, and/or add a post-run check that nags/retries the commit.

Goal: stop silent no-commit runs from masquerading as successful, while not crying wolf on the rare genuine no-op.
closeReason: PR #205 (commit 22dae5e) implements empty-push detection (reap.empty_push) + surfacing in src/runs/reap/run.ts; merged on main.

title: standardize agent git identity: inject fixed git credentials instead of letting agents self-name
description:
STATUS (2026-05-28): OPS FIX APPLIED — set WARREN_GIT_AUTHOR_NAME=Warren + WARREN_GIT_AUTHOR_EMAIL=28354219+jayminwest@users.noreply.github.com as fly secrets on warren-deployed; verified on the running machine (`git config --global --get user.name/email` returns them, so installGitAuthor() applied on boot). The REMAINING work below (make identity non-bypassable, inject into sandbox env, stop self-naming) is still open.

---

Agents that DO commit set their own git user.name/user.email inside the sandbox, so the committer identity is whatever the model invents. A Gemini agent named itself 'Earendil Agent' — now permanently in git history. Untraceable, inconsistent, pollutes blame/log.

== Current state of the code (already half-built: warren-fe67) ==

A per-deployment identity mechanism already exists but is OPT-IN and was UNSET on the live deployment (now set, see STATUS above):

- src/supervisor/git-identity.ts :: installGitAuthor() — when WARREN_GIT_AUTHOR_NAME + WARREN_GIT_AUTHOR_EMAIL are both set, runs `git config --global user.name/user.email` and exports GIT_AUTHOR_*/GIT_COMMITTER_* into the supervisor env (burrow's default 'user' identity mode mirrors the global gitconfig into <workspace>/.gitconfig.burrow, so the agent inherits it). When EITHER is empty it logs and returns {installed:false} — fallback is 'host git identity if available, otherwise unconfigured' (git-identity.ts:75-81).
- Wired at src/supervisor/main.ts:448-463 (authorName/Email from process.env.WARREN_GIT_AUTHOR_NAME/EMAIL).
- Documented in .env.example:148-162.

ROOT CAUSE of 'Earendil Agent': the deployed fly app had NEITHER secret set, so installGitAuthor no-op'd, the sandbox had no configured identity, and the agent was free to `git config` whatever name it liked.

Note the reap-side path is already pinned: warren's own commit-through-reap hardcodes `-c user.name=warren -c user.email=warren@os-eco.dev` (src/runs/reap/stage.ts:90-102 plot, :189-201 seeds; src/plots/sync.ts:157). So only the AGENT-AUTHORED commit identity was uncontrolled.

== Remaining work ==

1. Make identity non-bypassable, not just opt-in. An unset deployment silently lets agents self-name. Options: (a) default to the warren identity when env vars unset instead of leaving it unconfigured; (b) operator identity via config; (c) dedicated bot GH account.
2. Belt-and-suspenders: export GIT_AUTHOR_*/GIT_COMMITTER_* into the burrow sandbox env at dispatch (src/runs/spawn/dispatch.ts:331) and/or set core.hooksPath. Confirm whether an agent running `git config --local user.name ...` inside the workspace overrides the inherited global — if so, global config alone is insufficient.
3. Stop instructing/allowing agents to self-identify in prompts (builtins have no git-identity instruction today; they should be told identity is preset and not to touch it).

Relates to warren-27d3 (reap commit / pre-commit hook handling) and warren-72b9 (empty-push hardening).
closeReason: Fixed identity injection shipped in src/supervisor/git-identity.ts (WARREN_GIT_AUTHOR_NAME/EMAIL) wired in supervisor/main.ts, covered by git-identity.test.ts; commit 9404989 (warren-fe67).

title: Ratchet bundle-size budgets back down after pl-55a3 closes
description:
<!-- seeds:plan-backref:start -->
Adopted into plan pl-369d.

Parent seed: warren-8104 — Release-train batch: notable Medium + small quality wins
Plan template: feature
Plan approach: Submit minimal placeholders, release+close them, adopt the work seeds in execution order, adopt a release seed last so plan-run gates each on the previous PR merge and ends with a patch release.

Run `sd plan show pl-369d` for the full plan (context, alternatives, sibling steps, acceptance criteria).
<!-- seeds:plan-backref:end -->

PR #197 (Phase 2a) temporarily raised the bundle-size ceilings in scripts/bundle-size-budgets.json to unblock the in-flight pl-55a3 frontend overhaul, knowingly overriding the 'ratchet only goes down' rule:

- raw  js  475810 → 500000
- raw  css  35550 →  40000
- gzip js  136500 → 145000
- gzip css   7200 →   8500

These are GENEROUS temporary ceilings, not real floors. When pl-55a3 (warren-6358) closes:
1. Rebuild the UI fresh: bun run build:ui
2. Run bun run check:bundle-size to read the final actual output sizes.
3. Edit scripts/bundle-size-budgets.json: set all four budgets (totals.raw.js/css, totals.gzip.js/css, largest.gzip.js/css) back down to the measured sizes plus the small ~150-200B headroom the file historically used.
4. Collapse the stacked pl-55a3 $comment_* entries into one final note recording the new frozen floor.
5. bun run check:all and land it as the pl-55a3 close-out / patch-release step.

Context: the Phase 2a comment under-measured (claimed gzip css 7175 / js 135403); the real fresh CI build gzips to css 7227 / js 136561, which is what tripped CI on #197. Re-measure from a clean build, do not trust prior comments.
closeReason: PR #209 (commit c3bcb3a) ratcheted bundle-size budgets down after pl-55a3 (done); scripts/bundle-size-budgets.json closeout comment names warren-bfc6; merged on main.

title: Ratchet coverage floors up to current levels (unblock pre-commit hook)
description:
<!-- seeds:plan-backref:start -->
Adopted into plan pl-369d.

Parent seed: warren-8104 — Release-train batch: notable Medium + small quality wins
Plan template: feature
Plan approach: Submit minimal placeholders, release+close them, adopt the work seeds in execution order, adopt a release seed last so plan-run gates each on the previous PR merge and ends with a patch release.

Run `sd plan show pl-369d` for the full plan (context, alternatives, sibling steps, acceptance criteria).
<!-- seeds:plan-backref:end -->

The pre-commit hook runs check:all, which includes check:coverage. Coverage now exceeds the floors in scripts/coverage-budgets.json by ≥2pt, and the ratchet treats that as a failure (exit 1) to force the floors upward — so EVERY commit currently fails the hook until the floors are bumped. This is the same 'ratchet blocks unrelated work' friction that the bundle-size guard caused on PR #197, and it will bite the pl-55a3 agents on every commit.

Current vs floor (as of 2026-05-28):
- functions 87.14% (floor 85.00%)
- lines     90.70% (floor 90.00%)

check:coverage's own suggestion: ratchet to {functions: 86.64, lines: 90.20} (current minus a small margin).

Fix:
1. Edit scripts/coverage-budgets.json to raise the functions and lines floors to ~86.64 / ~90.20 (or current-minus-small-margin).
2. bun run check:coverage to confirm it passes.
3. Land as a standalone chore so the pre-commit hook stops failing for everyone.

Note: this is independent of pl-55a3 — it's general repo hygiene — but it's urgent because it blocks the hook on every commit.
closeReason: PR #208 (commit 3de3b60) ratcheted coverage floors up (functions 87.09 / lines 90.32 in scripts/coverage-budgets.json, cites warren-f65e); merged on main.

title: Pin bun version in CI (setup-bun) for build reproducibility
description:
<!-- seeds:plan-backref:start -->
Adopted into plan pl-df2f.

Parent seed: warren-4b01 — Batch: clear medium/low open backlog
Plan template: feature

Run `sd plan show pl-df2f` for the full plan (context, alternatives, sibling steps, acceptance criteria).
<!-- seeds:plan-backref:end -->

Three workflows use `oven-sh/setup-bun@v2` with no pinned version (.github/workflows/ci.yml:15, ci-postgres.yml:47, release.yml:24), so each run installs whatever bun is latest. The --frozen-lockfile change in build:ui (warren-bfc6, PR#209) makes DEPENDENCY resolution deterministic, but a future bun/esbuild bump can still shift Vite's minified output and silently move the measured bundle size — re-introducing the 'passes locally, fails in CI' bundle-size churn that warren-bfc6 fixed.

Fix: pin a concrete bun version across all setup-bun steps (add `with: bun-version: <X.Y.Z>`, or commit a .bun-version / .tool-versions that setup-bun honors), matching what local devs run (currently 1.3.14). Bump it deliberately as a tracked change, re-measuring bundle-size budgets via `bun run check:bundle-size:build --update` at the same time.

Files: .github/workflows/ci.yml, ci-postgres.yml, release.yml. Related: warren-bfc6 (bundle-size parity), scripts/check-bundle-size.ts.

title: auto_plan_run skips child-seed validation that manual POST /plan-runs enforces
description:
The auto_plan_run reap path (src/runs/reap/auto-plan-run.ts -> dispatchAutoPlanRuns) creates plan-runs by reading child seed IDs verbatim from the agent's in-sandbox .seeds/plans.jsonl and calling repos.planRuns.create directly. The only guard is children.length === 0 — it never validates that the child seeds actually exist in the project's persisted seed store.

By contrast, the manual path (src/server/handlers/plan-runs.ts createPlanRunHandler) probes every child via showSeed in a Promise.all and fails creation if any seed is missing.

Result: an agent can author a plan referencing child seed IDs that were never persisted to .seeds/issues.jsonl on the default branch (planned-but-never-created, or created only on an unmerged branch). The auto path happily writes plan_run_children rows and dispatches; the coordinator then wedges on the first unresolvable child.

Observed on the deployed instance (warren-deployed / supabase warren-dogfood): plnr_c743hq9efm61 (next child warren-af9c) and plnr_rj2x7bcmt7dv (next child plot-fb26), both trigger=auto_plan_run, stuck in 'running'. warren-af9c/warren-48e7/warren-7073 are absent from .seeds/issues.jsonl entirely (0 raw matches). The manual plan-run plnr_363pvm228nyr is clean because its seeds all resolve.

Fix direction: have the auto_plan_run path run the same showSeed/showPlan validation the manual handler does before persisting the plan-run (skip/closed children handled the same way), so a plan referencing non-existent seeds is rejected at creation with a clear reason instead of wedging later.

---

## Implementation plan (investigated 2026-05-29)

Depends on the SeedNotFoundError discriminator added in warren-0fed step 1 (src/seeds-cli/errors.ts + show.ts: non-zero exit matching /not found/i throws SeedNotFoundError).

1. src/runs/reap/auto-plan-run.ts — add optional validation to DispatchAutoPlanRunsInput:
   - Add `seedsCli?: SeedsCliDeps` and extend the `project` shape with `localPath`.
   - Before calling input.planRuns.create for each new planId: when seedsCli is wired, probe every child via showSeed(seedsCli, project.localPath, seedId). A SeedNotFoundError on any child -> skip that plan's dispatch (do NOT create the plan-run), emit a new `auto_plan_run_skipped` event { planId, reason, missing: string[] }, and continue to the next plan. Require >=1 non-closed child (mirror the manual handler's hasOpenChild check).
   - When seedsCli is absent (existing unit tests), behavior is unchanged (no validation) — same optional-seam posture as warrenConfigs/portAllocator.

2. Thread seedsCli into reap (optional seam, same pattern as warrenConfigs/portAllocator):
   - ReapRunInput (src/runs/reap/types.ts) gains `seedsCli?: SeedsCliDeps`; src/runs/reap/run.ts forwards it + project.localPath into dispatchAutoPlanRuns.
   - CreateBridgeRegistryInput (src/server/bridges.ts) -> RunWithReconnectInput (src/server/bridge-reconnect.ts) -> the inline reap call.
   - Wire it in src/server/main/index.ts bootBridges call (reuse the { sdBinary, spawn: defaultSpawn } shape already built for the plan-run coordinator, planRunSeedsCli) and in src/cli/commands/run.ts. cancel.ts is left alone — auto_plan_run only dispatches on outcome === 'succeeded'.

3. Tests: auto-plan-run skips dispatch + emits auto_plan_run_skipped when a child seed is missing; still dispatches when all children resolve; at-least-one-open-child guard.

Pairs with warren-0fed (coordinator fail-fast on the wedged plan-runs that already exist).

title: plan-run coordinator loops forever on a missing seed (showSeed throw -> noop) instead of failing
description:
In src/plan-runs/coordinator.ts, when showSeed(projectId, next.seedId) throws (e.g. 'sd show <id>' exits 1 with 'Issue not found'), the coordinator returns { kind: 'noop', reason: 'show_seed_failed:...' } and retries on every tick (~10s) indefinitely. It never fails the child or the plan-run, so a plan-run referencing a now-unresolvable seed sits in 'running' with the next child stuck 'pending' forever.

On the deployed instance this manifested as continuous plan_run.noop log spam for plnr_c743hq9efm61 (show_seed_failed: sd show warren-af9c exited 1: Issue not found) and plnr_rj2x7bcmt7dv (sd show plot-fb26 ... Issue not found). From the UI these look permanently 'pending'.

Relevant code (coordinator.ts, dispatch branch):
  try { seedShow = await input.showSeed(planRun.projectId, next.seedId); }
  catch (err) { return { kind: 'noop', reason: `show_seed_failed:${formatError(err)}` }; }

Note the distinction this should preserve: a transient sd failure (timeout, lock) is legitimately retryable, but a definitive 'Issue not found' should be terminal.

Fix direction: treat a definitive not-found result as a child failure -> fail (or skip) the child and transition the plan-run to failed with failure_reason, rather than an infinite retryable noop. Consider a bounded retry/backoff for genuinely transient sd errors so a hung seed store doesn't instantly fail healthy plan-runs. Pairs with warren-41d5 (add create-time validation on the auto_plan_run path).

---

## Implementation plan (investigated 2026-05-29)

Root cause: showSeed (src/seeds-cli/show.ts) throws a generic SeedsCliError on ANY non-zero sd exit, so it cannot distinguish a definitive "Issue not found" (exit 1) from a transient failure (timeout/lock). The coordinator's catch treats every case as a retryable noop.

1. Add a not-found discriminator in seeds-cli (mirror the existing canopy isPromptNotFoundMessage / structured-not-found pattern in src/registry/canopy.ts):
   - New SeedNotFoundError extends SeedsCliError (code `seed_not_found`) in src/seeds-cli/errors.ts.
   - In showSeed (and showPlan) in src/seeds-cli/show.ts: when exit is non-zero AND stderr/stdout matches /not found/i, throw SeedNotFoundError instead of the generic SeedsCliError. Export it from src/seeds-cli/index.ts.

2. Coordinator fix (src/plan-runs/coordinator.ts, the showSeed catch block ~line 230):
   - err instanceof SeedNotFoundError -> TERMINAL: updateChild to state 'failed' with failureReason `child_seed_not_found:<seedId>`, transitionTo plan-run 'failed', emit plan_run.failed on the anchor run, return { kind: 'plan_failed', failedSeq, reason }.
   - Any other error (transient) -> keep the existing retryable { kind: 'noop' } so a hung/locked seed store does not instantly kill healthy plan-runs (per the caution above).

3. Tests: coordinator not-found -> plan_failed; transient sd error -> noop (still retryable); showSeed unit test asserts SeedNotFoundError on /not found/ vs SeedsCliError on other non-zero exits.

Note: warren-41d5 (create-time validation on the auto_plan_run path) reuses the same SeedNotFoundError discriminator from step 1.

title: plan-run hangs forever in waiting_for_parent_merge when parent PR can never merge (CI failure / blocked auto-merge)
description:
<!-- seeds:plan-backref:start -->
Adopted into plan pl-df2f.

Parent seed: warren-4b01 — Batch: clear medium/low open backlog
Plan template: feature

Run `sd plan show pl-df2f` for the full plan (context, alternatives, sibling steps, acceptance criteria).
<!-- seeds:plan-backref:end -->

Sibling of warren-0fed (coordinator never terminates a doomed wait), different root cause.

In src/plan-runs/coordinator.ts -> checkParentRunMerged (added by warren-d9a2), an auto_plan_run gates dispatch of its FIRST child on the parent run's PR being merged. checkPrMerged returns 'open' as long as the PR is open, and the coordinator returns { kind: 'waiting_for_parent_merge' } on every tick with no timeout and no terminal path for a PR that is open-but-unmergeable. If the parent PR can never merge (failing required check, branch protection BLOCKED, auto-merge enabled but stuck), the plan-run sits in 'running' forever: all children stay 'pending', run_id=null, no agent is ever spawned.

Observed on deployed instance (warren-dogfood / warren-deployed):
- plnr_pq4f4nbrm4qv (plan pl-a639, agent pi), created 2026-05-29T10:06:01Z, state=running, 6 children all pending/run_id=null.
- parent_run_id run_a1y23s60geky: cron nightwatch batch, succeeded, opened PR jayminwest/mulch#44 ('chore(warren): seeds state').
- PR #44: state OPEN, mergeStateStatus BLOCKED, mergedAt null. auto-merge (squash) enabled 10:06:15, but the 'ci' check concluded FAILURE at 10:07:51, so auto-merge never lands. Gate polls #44 -> 'open' indefinitely.
- Same hour-cron pattern stranded plnr_c743hq9efm61 / plnr_rj2x7bcmt7dv (those are warren-0fed's showSeed variant); 07:06 and 11:04 siblings succeeded because their parent PRs merged.

Distinction to preserve: a transient 'open' (PR genuinely in flight / mid-merge) is legitimately retryable; an open-but-cannot-merge PR (failing required checks, BLOCKED mergeStateStatus) should not block a plan-run indefinitely.

Fix direction:
- Treat the parent-merge gate the same way warren-0fed proposes for showSeed: detect a definitively-unmergeable parent PR (e.g. failing required checks / BLOCKED mergeStateStatus, or a bounded wall-clock timeout for waiting_for_parent_merge) and fail the plan-run with a failure_reason (e.g. parent_pr_unmergeable / parent_pr_merge_timeout) instead of an unbounded wait.
- Surface the parent CI failure in the plan_run / event stream so the operator sees why it's stuck rather than a silent 'running'.
- Consider the same bounded-timeout treatment for the child waiting_for_merge branch (handleInFlight), which has the identical unbounded-wait shape.

Relates to warren-0fed (coordinator never-terminates meta-bug) and warren-d9a2 (introduced this gate).

description:

Final step of the medium/low batch plan. Have the agent perform a patch version bump and release prep following .claude/commands/release.md exactly:
1. Find last tag: git describe --tags --abbrev=0
2. Review changes since tag: git log <tag>..HEAD --oneline and git diff
3. Patch bump (unless user explicitly says minor/major).
4. Bump version in BOTH package.json ("version") and src/index.ts (export const VERSION) so .github/workflows/release.yml does not fail on mismatch.
5. Move CHANGELOG.md [Unreleased] items into the new version section dated today.
6. Update CLAUDE.md if command counts/structure changed.
7. Update README.md if CLI reference/stats changed.
8. Run bun run check:all, then commit.
Runs last so it captures all prior children merged in this batch.

title: Expose per-group cancelled count on RunGroupBucket so insights stop reverse-engineering the terminal denominator
description:
<!-- seeds:plan-backref:start -->
Adopted into plan pl-247b.

Parent seed: warren-6daa — Batch: clear orphan techdebt backlog
Plan template: feature

Run `sd plan show pl-247b` for the full plan (context, alternatives, sibling steps, acceptance criteria).
<!-- seeds:plan-backref:end -->

Found by nightwatch patrol 2026-06-02. src/runs/analytics/run-metrics.ts computes terminal = succeeded + failed + cancelled in finalizeGroup() but RunGroupBucket only exposes { succeeded, failed, successRate } — not cancelled. As a result src/runs/analytics/insights.ts:terminalRuns() must reverse-engineer the denominator from successRate (Math.round(succeeded/successRate)), with a documented blind spot: when successRate === 0 it falls back to succeeded + failed and DROPS cancelled. Consequence (real bug): worstSuccessAgent() gates on terminalRuns(g) < MIN_AGENT_TERMINAL_RUNS (3); an agent with 0 succeeded, 1 failed, 5 cancelled has true terminal=6 / 0% success but terminalRuns()=1, so the genuinely worst agent is suppressed from the callout. Fix: add readonly cancelled: number to RunGroupBucket (run-metrics.ts), populate it in finalizeGroup, then change insights.ts terminalRuns() to g.succeeded + g.failed + g.cancelled and drop the successRate reverse-engineering. This changes a public exported type signature (RunGroupBucket is re-exported via src/runs/index.ts and shaped into the GET /analytics/runs response), so it is filed as a standalone task per the patrol scope rather than folded into the patrol plan. Update run-metrics.test.ts + insights.test.ts to cover the rate===0-with-cancellations case.

title: POST /runs ignores Idempotency-Key — no dedupe window, duplicate dispatches double-spawn
description:
createRunHandler (src/server/handlers/runs/dispatch.ts:185) reads the request body but never inspects the `Idempotency-Key` header — it calls spawnRun() unconditionally. So any duplicate delivery of a single logical dispatch spawns a SECOND run (new burrow + agent), silently ~2x'ing agent spend.

## Context
Counterpart to kota-mind-9376. kota-mind's WarrenClient was retrying the non-idempotent POST /runs on timeout/5xx, creating a duplicate run the client never saw (run_ydg0jne7fkkt + run_st0c7q7vfkff, identical started_at, same seed/project/prompt, ~$0.71 burned on a trivial test). That client-side retry is now fixed (maxRetries:0), and kota-mind now SENDS a stable `Idempotency-Key` header on every dispatch (kota-mind commit cf717ae). But the header is currently a no-op on the Warren side — this seed is the server half.

## Fix
In createRunHandler, before spawnRun():
- Read the `Idempotency-Key` header.
- Look it up in a dedupe window keyed by (projectId, key) — short TTL (e.g. minutes) is enough to cover retries/replays.
- On hit within the window: return the existing run's 201 response (same {run, burrow} shape) instead of calling spawnRun() again.
- On miss: spawn as normal and record (projectId, key) -> runId.
- Header absent: preserve current behavior (always spawn) for backward compat.

This is the durable backstop: it closes duplicates from ANY source — proxy/LB replays, scheduler double-fire, future clients that re-retry — not just kota-mind's now-fixed retry path.

## Acceptance
- Two POST /runs with the same Idempotency-Key (same project) within the window => one run spawned, both responses reference it.
- Same key after the window expires, or a different key => new run.
- No header => unchanged behavior.
- Test covering the dedupe path.

title: pi: per-agent tool allowlist/denylist configurable in .warren agent definitions
description:
<!-- seeds:plan-backref:start -->
Adopted into plan pl-a141.

Parent seed: warren-763e — Polish pass: observability, bug fixes, hygiene, config
Plan template: feature

Run `sd plan show pl-a141` for the full plan (context, alternatives, sibling steps, acceptance criteria).
<!-- seeds:plan-backref:end -->

Plumb pi's --tools / --exclude-tools through warren agent frontmatter so an agent definition can declare which built-in/extension tools the pi runtime exposes to the LLM. This makes real read-only modes (reviewer / bugwatch / patrol agents) enforceable at the harness level, not just via bwrap.

Background: pi CLI already supports `--tools <allowlist>`, `--exclude-tools <denylist>`, `--no-tools`, and `--no-builtin-tools` (confirmed against pi v0.77.0; burrow pins v0.74.0). burrow's buildPiArgv (../burrow src/runtime/pi.ts) currently forces a fixed argv and only honors frontmatter.{provider,model}. warren reads provider/model frontmatter in src/registry/schema.ts (readProviderFrontmatter) and folds it onto burrow run metadata via composeBurrowMetadata in src/runs/spawn/dispatch.ts.

Scope:
- Add a tools field to the agent frontmatter schema (src/registry/schema.ts), e.g. frontmatter.tools = { allow?: string[], deny?: string[], builtinsOnly?/noBuiltins? }. Keep it generic but only the pi runtime consumes it in V1.
- Surface it from .warren agent definitions / DefaultsConfigSchema so operators configure it per agent without code changes.
- Pass the resolved allow/deny set to burrow on run metadata (same seam as provider/model), and extend burrow's buildPiArgv to append --tools / --exclude-tools accordingly. This is a paired warren<->burrow change (must stay in sync per SPEC §11.K).
- Tests: src/registry/schema.test.ts (frontmatter parse), src/runs/spawn/dispatch.test.ts (metadata fold), and a burrow buildPiArgv unit test.
- Docs: SPEC §11.K, CLAUDE.md per-project config knobs.

Payoff: cheapest high-value pi extension; gives patrol/reviewer agents a hard read-only guarantee. Cross-references warren CLAUDE.md 'Relationship to burrow' (paired pin).

title: Investigate pi per-run system-prompt injection (before_agent_start / --append-system-prompt) for plan-runs
description:
Spike (NOT urgent — current system works fine as-is). Investigate dynamically injecting per-run context into pi's system prompt instead of relying on the static SYSTEM_BODY in src/registry/builtins/pi.ts.

Two mechanisms pi exposes (confirmed via pi v0.77.0):
1. CLI: --append-system-prompt <text|file> (repeatable) and auto-loaded SYSTEM.md / AGENTS.md — no extension needed, plumbable through frontmatter into burrow buildPiArgv like provider/model.
2. Extension hook: before_agent_start receives event.systemPrompt (chained) + structured systemPromptOptions and can return { systemPrompt } to rewrite per turn — requires re-enabling extensions (currently --no-extensions in burrow; see separate keystone seed).

Why investigate: this looks especially useful for plan-runs (SPEC §11.P) and Plot-composed runs — inject the active seed/plan step, mulch expertise, plot actor, and prior-child outcomes into each child run's system prompt dynamically, rather than baking context into prompts or re-seeding agent definitions.

Questions to answer:
- Is per-run --append-system-prompt sufficient for plan-run context, or do we need the before_agent_start hook (and thus the extensions unlock)?
- Where would the injected context come from in warren (plan-run walker in src/plan-runs/, dispatch.ts), and how does it compose with canopy .pi/prompts and .pi/skills seeding?
- Token-budget implications of injecting per-child context.
- Interaction with the frozen rendered_agent_json (does injected context need to be recorded for reproducibility?).

Deliverable: a short design note / recommendation + follow-up implementation seed(s). No code required for this seed.

Refs: warren-8dee (tool allowlist, sibling pi-frontmatter work), SPEC §11.K (pi runtime), §11.P (plan-runs).
