title: Default new agents to pi runtime, not claude-code runtime
description:
<!-- seeds:plan-backref:start -->
Adopted into plan pl-a141.

Parent seed: warren-763e — Polish pass: observability, bug fixes, hygiene, config
Plan template: feature

Run `sd plan show pl-a141` for the full plan (context, alternatives, sibling steps, acceptance criteria).
<!-- seeds:plan-backref:end -->

When Warren resolves a new/builtin agent with no explicit runtime override, it currently defaults to the claude-code runtime. Pi (the multi-provider runtime, see pl-4374 / warren-39c1) is now the preferred default: cost streams in-band (warren-17a4), supports the unified provider matrix, and is what most dogfood runs actually use. claude-code runtime should be opt-in.

Scope:
- Flip default runtime resolution in src/warren-config (readRuntimeId / DefaultsConfigSchema, plus the non-interactive path) so 'pi' is the fallback when no agent frontmatter or config override pins a runtime.
- Update src/registry/builtins/ entries that implicitly inherit the old default. claude-code stays available — only the *default* changes.
- Update warren-config schema docs, .env.example, and acceptance scenarios that assert the old default.
- KNOWN_RUNTIME_IDS already includes 'pi'; no new runtime plumbing needed.

Out of scope: removing claude-code runtime, changing user agents that pin runtime explicitly.

Acceptance: freshly-dispatched builtin agent with no runtime override lands on pi; configs that pin claude-code unchanged; bun run check:all green.

title: Dispatch idempotency: client timeout creates duplicate runs server-side
description:
Repro (2026-06-05): a remote dispatch call from kota's warren-mcp tool timed out client-side, the caller retried, and Warren queued two runs for the same seed within 1ms of each other (run_e7q2a63jrc4z full-prompt original, run_3hrzth5mzfn3 retry — same projectId prj_ehzfkqxjfsah, same seedId kota-voice-0abf, trigger=manual). The first request succeeded server-side despite the client-perceived timeout; the retry had no way to detect that and queued a duplicate.

Root cause: POST /runs (or whichever dispatch endpoint Warren exposes to the MCP) is not idempotent. There is no client-supplied request id, no recent-duplicate dedupe window, and no way for a retry to discover the first request's run_id.

Proposed fix (server-side, definitive):
- Accept an optional 'Idempotency-Key' header (and/or body field idempotencyKey). When set, dedupe within a short window (e.g. 5 min): if a run with the same key already exists, return its run_id with 200 instead of queuing a new one. Match Stripe-style semantics.
- Optionally: also dedupe on (projectId, seedId, prompt-hash, trigger) inside a small window as a belt-and-suspenders fallback for clients that don't supply a key.

Client coordination:
- Update warren-mcp tool to generate an Idempotency-Key per logical dispatch attempt and reuse it on retry. (Filed separately if needed — primary fix lives here.)

Acceptance:
- Two POSTs to dispatch with the same Idempotency-Key return the same run_id and only one run exists in the DB.
- Without the header, behavior unchanged.
- Acceptance scenario covers the timeout-then-retry case.
closeReason: Shipped: Idempotency-Key dedupe in POST /runs (commit a29513ab, warren-d525) + IdempotencyStore; acceptance scenario merged via PR #278 (2026-06-06)

title: Investigate: push notifications to KOTA when Warren runs finish
description:
Today an operator (KOTA or human) who dispatches a remote Warren run has to manually poll warren_status to find out when it's done. The result: KOTA either spam-polls, forgets to check back, or the user has to nudge it. We want the inverse: Warren wakes KOTA when the run terminates (success, failure, paused-for-question, cancelled).

This is an exploration seed — design space first, implementation second.

Open questions to answer in the plan:
1. Transport. Options: outbound webhook (Warren POSTs to a configured URL on terminal state), kota-sense pull adapter (Warren exposes /runs/events SSE that kota-sense subscribes to and turns into KOTA inbox items), or a direct kota-memory write (Warren calls kota-memory with an event node). Webhook is simplest; SSE is cheaper at scale; memory-write is tightest integration but couples Warren to KOTA. Recommend webhook + a thin kota-sense 'warren' adapter that turns webhook events into kota-sense events / kota inbox messages.
2. Trigger events. Minimum: run terminal state (succeeded, failed, cancelled, timed_out). Probably also: paused (agent asked a question), preview ready, PR opened. Skip per-event noise.
3. Payload shape. run_id, projectId, seedId, plotId, state, prUrl, costUsd, durationMs, failureReason, link to UI. Keep small; KOTA can fetch details if needed.
4. Configuration. Per-project? Per-run (opt-in flag on dispatch)? Global? Likely global webhook list in warren-config with optional per-run override.
5. Security. Shared secret / HMAC signing of webhook bodies so KOTA can verify origin.
6. KOTA side. Where does the event land? kota-sense 'warren' adapter → triage → kota inbox surface to next KOTA session? Or direct memory write that the next session sees on prime?
7. Retry / dead-letter behavior on webhook failures.

Acceptance for the *exploration*:
- A short design doc (docs/run-notifications.md) covering the above, with a recommended path.
- One follow-up seed for the implementation slice.

Out of scope here: building the kota-sense adapter, building the UI — that's the follow-up.

title: Agents don't reliably close their seed when prompted to
description:
<!-- seeds:plan-backref:start -->
Adopted into plan pl-247b.

Parent seed: warren-6daa — Batch: clear orphan techdebt backlog
Plan template: feature

Run `sd plan show pl-247b` for the full plan (context, alternatives, sibling steps, acceptance criteria).
<!-- seeds:plan-backref:end -->

Repro (run_e7q2a63jrc4z, 2026-06-05): dispatch prompt explicitly said 'Open a PR and close the seed when done'. The agent (claude-code, builtin) shipped the fix, opened PR #1 (jayminwest/kota-voice), committed, but left seed kota-voice-0abf in 'open' state. The operator (KOTA) had to pull and 'sd close' by hand after the merge.

Hypotheses to confirm:
1. The 'sd' binary isn't installed in the burrow image for the claude-code runtime, so the close step silently no-ops or the agent skips it.
2. 'sd' is present but auth/git config in the burrow means 'sd close' + 'sd sync' don't round-trip back through the PR — the close happens on the run branch and is reaped, but the merge of the PR doesn't carry the .seeds/ change because the close came after the commit/push step in the agent's flow.
3. The agent's system prompt biases it toward 'finish at the PR', treating seed bookkeeping as out of scope.

Fix direction (pick one or stack):
- Make seed-closing a *runtime* concern, not an agent concern: on successful reap with a non-null seedId, Warren itself runs 'sd close <seed>' against the project (host-side, after merge) and commits a 'seeds: sync' on main. Removes the burden from every agent and every prompt.
- Or: ensure 'sd' is on PATH in every builtin runtime image and add it to the operating contract in the rendered system prompt ('If the prompt names a seed_id, you MUST sd close it before reporting completion').
- Or both — runtime as the safety net, agent as the happy path.

Acceptance:
- A successful run with a seedId leaves that seed closed in main once the PR is merged, without operator intervention.
- The close shows up in git history as either an agent commit on the PR branch or a Warren-host commit on main, never silently dropped.
closeReason: Implemented host-side seed-id close: reap now calls sd close <seedId> after a successful run when seedsCli is configured, project.hasSeeds=true, and run.seedId is set. Best-effort, idempotent, lands in the chore(warren): seeds state commit. 5 tests added (seeds.test.ts), commit c6c29be4.

title: Runaway/hung quality-gate commands have no timeout — single run can wedge indefinitely
description:
run_wawgn5tbejkx (agent pi, project kota-mind) sat in state=running for 13+ min with zero new events. Root cause: the agent launched 'bun run lint:fix' (biome check --write .) as a bash tool call (event seq 842, 17:22:14Z) and biome went runaway (~4 cores pinned). Nothing reaped it: burrow run stayed 'running', exitCode null, and warren happily showed it live. There is no per-tool/per-bash or wall-clock budget that force-terminates a stuck command or fails the run. agent.pauseTimeoutMs only covers paused/question turns, not a busy-but-silent tool. Add a configurable command/heartbeat timeout (or surface burrow's run timeout) so a hung gate command reaps the run to failed instead of hanging forever. Repro evidence: process tree on warren-deployed showed bash->bun->node biome->biome cli accumulating ~57 CPU-min; killing the biome pid let the agent resume (and it immediately re-ran the same gate and re-hung — deterministic).

UPDATE (cancel does not reap the sandbox process tree): POST /runs/run_wawgn5tbejkx/cancel returned {state:cancelled, alreadyTerminal:false} and the run row went state=cancelled/ended_at set, but the runaway in-sandbox process tree (bash -> bun run lint:fix -> node biome -> biome cli) kept running and kept pinning ~4 cores until I manually 'kill -9'd the pids on the machine. So cancel marks the warren run terminal without terminating the burrow run's child process tree — same class of leak as the missing gate timeout. Fix should ensure cancel (and timeout-driven reaping) actually stops the burrow run / tears down the bwrap sandbox so orphaned children don't keep burning CPU after the run is terminal.
closeReason: Heartbeat watchdog landed (src/runs/watchdog.ts, commit 180488ca): WARREN_RUN_HEARTBEAT_TIMEOUT_MS arms a per-tick sweep that force-fails 'running' runs whose newest event (or startedAt) is older than the budget — emits watchdog.timed_out, cancels the burrow run, then reaps it failed (failure_reason=timed_out). Routing through reap's workspace_destroy tears down the burrow workspace so a hung gate command no longer wedges the run forever. Note: the residual 'cancel/destroy must actually kill the in-sandbox bwrap process tree' is a burrow-side guarantee (DELETE /burrows/:id semantics) — warren now reliably issues the destroy; if orphaned children survive it, file against burrow.

title: workspace_gc retries the same stranded burrow rows every cycle (58/59 fail with 404), flooding logs and hammering the burrow socket
description:
<!-- seeds:plan-backref:start -->
Adopted into plan pl-247b.

Parent seed: warren-6daa — Batch: clear orphan techdebt backlog
Plan template: feature

Run `sd plan show pl-247b` for the full plan (context, alternatives, sibling steps, acceptance criteria).
<!-- seeds:plan-backref:end -->

On warren-deployed, workspace_gc.swept logs 'scanned:59 stranded:58 destroyed:0 failed:58' every ~5 min. Each stranded entry fires DELETE /burrows/:id which 404s ('burrow not found') because burrow no longer knows the id — warren keeps stale burrow DB rows and never reconciles them, so it retries the same 58 forever. Two harms: (1) log spam (hundreds of NotFoundError stacks per sweep), (2) the DELETE storm hammered the unix socket and coincided with a transient 'burrow unreachable at unix:/var/run/burrow.sock: The operation timed out' that broke the run stream bridge for run_wawgn5tbejkx (bridge went into reconnect backoff). Fix: on a 404 from burrow during GC, treat the burrow as already-gone and forget/prune the warren-side row (or mark reconciled) instead of re-attempting destroy every cycle. Consider rate-limiting the sweep's per-burrow calls.

title: PR-open: retry transient GitHub 422s instead of treating every non-'already exists' 422 as terminal
description:
<!-- seeds:plan-backref:start -->
Adopted into plan pl-247b.

Parent seed: warren-6daa — Batch: clear orphan techdebt backlog
Plan template: feature

Run `sd plan show pl-247b` for the full plan (context, alternatives, sibling steps, acceptance criteria).
<!-- seeds:plan-backref:end -->

In src/runs/pr.ts, openPullRequest only treats a 422 as recoverable when the errors blob matches /already exists/; every other 422 (e.g. transient post-push 'head invalid' while GitHub indexes a just-pushed ref) falls through to a non-retryable http_error. src/runs/reap/pr-open.ts runPrOpen calls tryOpenPr exactly once with no retry. Observed in run_fcg4wje2s5ex: branch pushed OK (branchPushed:true, commitsAhead:3) but POST /pulls returned 422 'Validation Failed'; retrying the identical create later succeeded (PR #32), confirming it was transient. Add bounded retry/backoff for non-idempotent 422/5xx on PR open, and persist the GitHub errors[] payload (not just message) so future failures are diagnosable.

title: Sandbox: relocate Bun install cache outside the workspace so agent git add can't commit it
description:
<!-- seeds:plan-backref:start -->
Adopted into plan pl-247b.

Parent seed: warren-6daa — Batch: clear orphan techdebt backlog
Plan template: feature

Run `sd plan show pl-247b` for the full plan (context, alternatives, sibling steps, acceptance criteria).
<!-- seeds:plan-backref:end -->

Root cause of run_fcg4wje2s5ex / plnr_3k4np39mh199 failure. The Bun install cache lands at the workspace root (.bun/install/cache), and an agent doing a broad 'git add' (prompt said 'commit when done') swept ~5074 files / ~1.2M additions into its commit. That huge push then hit a GitHub 422 'Validation Failed' on PR open. Fix at the durable layer: set BUN_INSTALL_CACHE_DIR (or XDG_CACHE_HOME) to a path OUTSIDE the mounted workspace in the burrow sandbox env, so no agent git add can pick it up for any project. Sandbox env setup lives around src/supervisor + burrow serve / src/runs/burrow-config.ts. Gitignoring .bun/ in the burrow repo is the per-repo quick patch already applied; this seed is the all-projects fix.

title: plan-run: don't silently halt the whole plan when a child's best-effort PR-open fails (child_succeeded_without_pr)
description:
<!-- seeds:plan-backref:start -->
Adopted into plan pl-247b.

Parent seed: warren-6daa — Batch: clear orphan techdebt backlog
Plan template: feature

Run `sd plan show pl-247b` for the full plan (context, alternatives, sibling steps, acceptance criteria).
<!-- seeds:plan-backref:end -->

A best-effort, never-fails-the-run PR-open failure currently translates into a hard plan-run failure: run_fcg4wje2s5ex finished state=succeeded with pr_url=null, and the serial gate marked plnr_3k4np39mh199 failed with reason child_succeeded_without_pr, leaving children seq 2-4 (burrow-5704, burrow-c386, burrow-a57d) never dispatched. The branch WAS pushed (3 commits ahead) — only the PR object was missing. Make the gate recoverable: attempt to (re)open the PR before failing the plan, surface an actionable operator state instead of a terminal fail, and/or allow resuming the plan-run once the PR exists. Pairs with the PR-open retry seed.
closeReason: Implemented coordinator PR-reopen seam: resolveChildPrReopen in merge-gate.ts, reopenPr hook in AdvancePlanRunInput wired from bootPlanRunCoordinator, plan_run.waiting_for_pr_reopen event. commit a7c90450.

title: Leveret v1 — conversational overseer (warren side)
DB: add conversations + messages tables and mode:"conversation" run mode, with repos (ConversationsRepo/MessagesRepo) and drift parity
description:
Spec: LEVERET.md §0.5 / §0.0.F / build-phase 2.

Add two new tables and a new run mode, following the projects + ProjectsRepo template.

conversations: id (pk), project_id (REQUIRED in v1; FK/text to projects), plot_id (text, nullable in schema for forward-compat though v1 always sets it), anchoring_run_id (text, nullable — rotates on re-wake), status ('active'|'closed'), title, created_at, last_activity_at, closed_at. Indexes: (project_id), (plot_id).
messages: id (pk), conversation_id (FK, onDelete: cascade), seq (monotonic per conversation), role ('user'|'assistant'|'system'|'tool'), content (text/json), created_at, optional run_id. Index (conversation_id, seq).
Add 'conversation' to the RUN_MODES tuple in src/db/schema/columns.ts (sibling of RUN_STATES).

Mirror all of this in src/db/schema/{columns,sqlite,postgres}.ts and the drift.test.ts maps (postgres mirror is mandatory or the drift test fails), then run bun run db:generate. Add ConversationsRepo + MessagesRepo repos.

events table stays run-anchored + single-writer (the bridge); conversation turns live ONLY in messages.

Acceptance: drift test green; bun run db:generate produces a committed migration; repos covered by unit tests; bun run check:all passes.

title: DB: add plots projection table (JSON-blob state + promoted scalars id/project_id/status/title/updated_at) and PlotsRepo with drift parity
description:
Spec: LEVERET.md §0.0.A / §0.0.F / build-phase 2.

Add a plots PROJECTION table — a read-cache that mirrors full git-backed Plot state, NOT an authoritative store. Source of truth stays git (.plot/ <id>.json + <id>.events.jsonl). Schema:
- A single JSON-blob column holding full plot state (so the schema is stable as plot section shape drifts — no migration per shape change).
- Promoted scalar columns for list/index queries: id, project_id, status, title, updated_at.
Open (settle in impl, §0.0.G): whether to summarize events (count/last-seq) onto the row.

Add PlotsRepo (ProjectsRepo template). Mirror in src/db/schema/{columns,sqlite,postgres}.ts + drift.test.ts maps; run bun run db:generate.

This is a deliberate partial down-payment on the §8 first-class plots table, scoped to a projection so the 'don't rewrite the spine' deferral still holds.

Acceptance: drift test green; PlotsRepo upsert/get/list covered by tests; bun run check:all passes.
closeReason: Shipped: plots table in sqlite.ts:478 + postgres.ts:284 (stateJson blob + promoted scalars), PlotsRepo in src/db/repos/plots.ts with upsert/get/list/delete, drift.test.ts parity (lines 56/71), repo tests in plots.test.ts.

title: Wire a plots-projection upsert hook into the src/plot-client/ read/write paths
description:
Spec: LEVERET.md §0.0.A / §0.0.F.

Upsert the plots projection (warren-9022) whenever warren reads or writes a Plot through the single src/plot-client/ write path (which already does the locked read-modify-write on <id>.json + appends intent_edited). The projection is a read-cache only.

Hook the upsert on both the read and write paths so list/index queries stay current. Keep git as source of truth.

Acceptance: writing intent via the plot client refreshes the projection row; reading a Plot populates/refreshes it; tests assert the row matches plot state after an intent_edited; bun run check:all passes.

title: Lifetime: exempt mode:"conversation" from watchdog force-fail, crash-recovery finalize, and reap workspace-destroy
description:
Spec: LEVERET.md §0.4 / §0.0.E / build-phase 2.

The pi-chat runtime (burrow side) stops the per-turn agent_end terminal envelope, so the run stays running across turns. Warren-side guards must stop force-killing an idle conversation run. NOTE (§0.0.E scout correction): the pause detector ALREADY exempts conversation for free (src/runs/pause.ts guards on mode !== 'batch'), and workspace GC (src/runs/reap/gc.ts) is state-keyed (safe while non-terminal). So ONLY these need an explicit mode:'conversation' skip:
- Heartbeat watchdog (src/runs/watchdog.ts): opt-in via WARREN_RUN_HEARTBEAT_TIMEOUT_MS, has NO mode exemption today — add one so an armed watchdog can't force-fail an idle conversation run.
- Reap workspace-destroy (src/runs/reap/destroy.ts): extend the existing mode:'interactive' exemption to mode:'conversation'.
- Crash recovery: bootBridges (src/server/bridges.ts) RESUMES bridges and only finalizes (burrow_run_lost) when a burrow pre-probe 404s — exempt or re-anchor mode:'conversation' rows (trigger is burrow-lost, not a blanket restart sweep).

Acceptance: an idle conversation run with the watchdog armed is not force-failed; reap does not destroy its workspace; crash-recovery does not blanket-finalize it; tests cover each guard; bun run check:all passes.
closeReason: Shipped all three exemptions: watchdog skip (watchdog.ts:116-123), crash-recovery conversation_burrow_lost skip (bridges.ts:319-332), reap workspace-destroy skip (destroy.ts:62-68); each with tests. Live-verified: deployed reap emitted workspace_destroy_skipped reason=conversation_run.

title: Lifetime: add conversation.idleTimeoutMs config knob + idle-timeout coordinator boot-worker that finalizes the anchoring run only
description:
Spec: LEVERET.md §0.4 / §0.14 / build-phase 2.

Add a conversation-scoped idle-timeout that finalizes the ANCHORING RUN after inactivity. It does NOT close the conversation (the Plot and transcript persist; only the run finalizes).
- New config knob: conversation.idleTimeoutMs in src/warren-config/schema.ts (DefaultsConfigSchema), default ~20 min (settle exact default + path in impl, §0.14), mirroring the agent.pauseTimeoutMs pattern.
- New boot-worker coordinator mirroring bootPauseDetector that finalizes the anchoring run after last_activity_at + idleTimeoutMs.

Acceptance: an idle conversation's anchoring run finalizes after the configured timeout while the conversation stays status='active'; transcript survives in messages; knob is schema-validated with bounds; bun run check:all passes.

title: Re-wake: spawn a fresh mode:"conversation" run that replays the DB transcript into a new pi session
description:
Spec: LEVERET.md §0.4 / §0.2 (Re-wake) / build-phase 2.

When the operator returns to an active conversation whose anchoring run is terminal, spawn a NEW mode:'conversation' run that REPLAYS the DB transcript (messages rows) into a fresh pi session. Do NOT depend on the prior workspace/.pi/sessions surviving (v1 needs no pi --session resume). Update conversations.anchoring_run_id to the new run.

Token cost grows with transcript length — acceptable for v1.

Depends on the conversation tables (warren-0b91) and lifetime exemptions (warren-c770).

Acceptance: re-waking an active conversation with a terminal anchoring run produces a new running conversation run seeded with the full prior transcript; anchoring_run_id rotates; no dependence on the old burrow; tests cover the replay; bun run check:all passes.
closeReason: Shipped: rewakeConversation() in conversation-rewake.ts:273-307 (transcript replay via buildRewakePrompt, fresh mode:conversation run, anchor rotation, conversation.rewake_replayed event), comprehensive tests. Live-verified: deployed re-wake replayed <conversation_transcript> with trigger=rewake. Blocker warren-c770 now closed (was stale edge).

title: Seeding: add pi_extensions agent section -> .pi/extensions/ drop in src/runs/seed.ts (mirror pi_skills/pi_prompts)
description:
Spec: LEVERET.md §0.10 / build-phase 3.

Mirror the existing pi_skills / pi_prompts seeding path in src/runs/seed.ts (buildPiArtifactFiles):
- Add a third PiArtifactKind = 'extension' -> base dir .pi/extensions, flat path ${name}.ts (extensions are TS modules default-exporting (pi) => {…}). Reuse the JSONL {name, body} shape + the isSafeArtifactName guard.
- Add agent.sections.pi_extensions handling + a piExtensions field on BuildSeedFilesResult.

This is INERT until burrow drops --no-extensions for pi-chat (burrow plan) — both land together. Seeding alone is a no-op until the burrow slice ships.

Acceptance: an agent def carrying a pi_extensions section seeds .pi/extensions/<name>.ts into the workspace; unsafe names rejected; tests mirror the pi_skills/pi_prompts coverage; bun run check:all passes.

title: Agent: add src/registry/builtins/leveret.ts (runtime pi-chat, network open) shipping the propose_intent extension, and append to BUILTIN_AGENTS
description:
Spec: LEVERET.md §0.11 / §0.1 / §0.2 / build-phase 3.

Add src/registry/builtins/leveret.ts AgentDefinition and append to BUILTIN_AGENTS:
- runtime: 'pi-chat' (readRuntimeId reads frontmatter.runtime as a free string — no KNOWN_RUNTIME_IDS change needed, §0.0.E).
- system section (overseer persona that shapes Plot intent via conversation).
- burrow_config network='open'.
- pi_extensions section carrying the single shipped extension propose_intent (depends on warren-e38b seeding).
- Read-leaning tools read/grep/find/ls/bash (bash = operator-trusted overseer escape hatch). NO edit/write tool — this is what makes the send-off PR safe by construction.

Also ship the propose_intent extension module itself (TS, default-exporting (pi) => {…}, registering a propose_intent tool). It patches STRUCTURED intent fields (goal, non-goals, constraints, success criteria), bound exactly to the ../plot intent schema (§0.14) — NOT free-form replace. Its execute() returns details (arbitrary JSON) carried on the tool_execution_end stdout event; correlate via toolCallId (§0.0.E).

Acceptance: leveret appears in BUILTIN_AGENTS with runtime pi-chat + network open; the propose_intent extension module is shipped and seeded; def covered by registry tests; bun run check:all passes.
closeReason: Shipped: src/registry/builtins/leveret.ts (runtime pi-chat, network open, propose_intent pi_extension returning details.intent_patch), appended to BUILTIN_AGENTS (index.ts:65); tests in builtins.test.ts:274-328. Live-verified on warren-deployed (run_x17v275aqkye resolved builtin:leveret).

title: Conversation stream bridge: persist turns to messages, parse propose_intent from tool_execution_end details, write intent_edited(actor=leveret) host-side
description:
Spec: LEVERET.md §0.6 / §0.0.E / §0.5 / build-phase 3.

Add a conversation stream-bridge variant (alongside src/runs/stream/bridge.ts) that:
1. Persists conversation turns to the messages table (assistant text streams to the UI via the existing event broker for live view, then persists as a final messages row on turn end; user turns ride the existing mid-run steering/inbox loop).
2. Parses the propose_intent emission from the tool_execution_end event's details field (NOT the message_end/toolResult form, which drops details — §0.0.E), correlating via toolCallId.
3. Writes the canonical intent_edited HOST-SIDE via the plot client with actor = agent:leveret. (Leveret runs inside the sandbox, so workspace .plot/ writes are invisible until reap — live intent travels out over the event stream.)

DEPENDS on the ../plot ACL widening (EVENT_ACL.intent_edited -> ['user','agent']) shipping in the ../plot repo, and bump the plot-cli pin if a new capability gate is needed. The old formalize marker-parser is NOT reused for intent (superseded by the typed tool); retire or repoint it.

Acceptance: a propose_intent tool call results in an intent_edited(actor=leveret) on the Plot and a persisted messages row; details parsed from tool_execution_end; tests cover the parse + host-side write; bun run check:all passes.

title: API: conversation endpoints (create/list/get/post-message) and hide mode:"conversation" from the Runs API

Spec: LEVERET.md §0.11 / §0.9 / build-phase 4.

Add conversation API endpoints (register in the ROUTE_TABLE; gen:docs + gen:openapi must stay in sync — run bun run gen:docs && bun run gen:openapi after editing routes):
- POST create conversation: project_id REQUIRED; auto-create a fresh Plot OR attach to an existing one (operator choice, §0.2). Dispatches the anchoring mode:'conversation' run using the leveret builtin (warren-fdd9). N conversations per Plot (N:1).
- GET list / GET get.
- POST post-message: delivers an operator turn over the conversation turn-delivery path (the repointed steering channel).
HIDE mode:'conversation' runs from the Runs API/list (filter by mode).

Send-off endpoint lands separately in warren-756d.

Depends on warren-0b91 (tables/repos), warren-fdd9 (builtin), warren-ce65 (bridge).

title: UI: Leveret split-view page — streamed chat (left), live plot intent rendered dynamically from the plot JSON shape (right), top-bar send-off buttons

title: Send-off: open a plotSync PR carrying only the plot-state update, close the conversation, and persist the PR ref + plot_id + planner agent
description:
Spec: LEVERET.md §0.0.B / §0.7 (steps 1-2) / build-phase 5.

Add the 'Send to planner' send-off endpoint:
1. Open a PR against the target project whose ONLY change is the plot-state update — safe by construction (Leveret ships no edit/write tool). REUSE the existing plotSync PR machinery (src/plots/sync.ts).
2. This action CLOSES the conversation: transition status -> 'closed', set closed_at; the anchoring run finalizes. The Plot persists.
3. PERSIST the submitted PR ref + plot_id + planner agent so the merge poller can auto-dispatch later — on the conversation row OR a small plot_submissions record (§0.0.G, settle in impl).

Enabled once intent is non-empty. Re-plan after send-off is a NEW conversation attached to the same Plot (§0.0.C) — the old 're-send supersedes' mechanic is dropped.

Depends on the conversation API (warren-af15).

Acceptance: Send to planner opens a plotSync PR containing only the plot-state diff, closes the conversation (anchoring run finalizes, Plot persists), and persists PR ref + plot_id + planner agent; handler tests; bun run check:all passes.

title: Send-off: PR-merge poller boot-worker (reuse src/plan-runs/pr-merge.ts) that auto-dispatches the planner run keyed on plot_id when the PR merges
description:
Spec: LEVERET.md §0.0.B (steps 2-3) / §0.7 (steps 2-3) / build-phase 5.

No webhooks in v1. Add a PR-merge poller boot-worker (mirror bootPauseDetector) that polls the submitted send-off PR/branch until merged — the operator merges it manually (accepted v1 tradeoff). REUSE the existing plan-runs merge-polling helpers in src/plan-runs/pr-merge.ts / merge-gate.ts rather than re-implementing.

On detected merge: AUTO-DISPATCH a separate planner run (existing src/registry/builtins/planner.ts, its own burrow) keyed on plot_id. Its fresh clone now contains the merged intent. The planner emits an sd plan via sd plan prompt / sd plan submit and DOES NOT self-dispatch (its prompt forbids POST /runs and POST /plan-runs).

Reads the persisted PR ref + plot_id + planner agent from warren-756d.

Open (§0.0.G): poller interval; whether to reuse the plotSync PR identity for the merge probe.

Acceptance: the poller detects a merged send-off PR and auto-dispatches the planner keyed on plot_id; the planner emits a plan and does not self-dispatch; tests cover the poll->dispatch transition with stubbed merge state; bun run check:all passes.

title: UI: operator-gated "Dispatch plan" popup mirroring /plan-runs/new, wired to the existing plan-run dispatch path

title: Acceptance: add a deterministic, idempotent scenario exercising conversation -> live intent -> send-off -> dispatch under scripts/acceptance/scenarios/
description:
Spec: LEVERET.md §0.13 / build-phase (acceptance).

Add a new scenario under scripts/acceptance/scenarios/ (using scripts/acceptance/lib/ helpers) that exercises the full loop: conversation -> live intent (propose_intent -> intent_edited attributed to leveret) -> send-off (plotSync PR + conversation closes) -> merge-detected planner auto-dispatch -> operator-gated plan-run dispatch. Must be deterministic, idempotent, and clean up after itself (runs against a live, possibly long-lived warren+burrow stack).

Also assert: no mode:'conversation' run appears in the Runs list; the conversation survives many turns + an idle finalize + a re-wake (transcript replay); the Plot persists and re-planning is a new conversation on the same Plot.

Depends on the send-off poller (warren-b872), the Dispatch-plan path (warren-6e45), and interactive removal (warren-d622).

Acceptance: the scenario passes deterministically against a real stack and is idempotent on re-run; documented in ACCEPTANCE.md if the runbook lists scenarios; bun run check:all passes.

title: Release: run /release per .claude/commands/release.md with a MINOR version bump (package.json + src/index.ts) and CHANGELOG update
description:
Spec: LEVERET.md §0.13 acceptance + repo release process (.claude/commands/release.md).

FINAL step. Perform a MINOR version bump for the Leveret v1 feature (this is a new capability, so minor — not patch):
1. git describe --tags --abbrev=0 to find the last release; review git log <tag>..HEAD.
2. Bump the MINOR version in BOTH package.json ('version') AND src/index.ts (export const VERSION = 'X.Y.Z') — they must agree or .github/workflows/release.yml fails the release job.
3. Move CHANGELOG.md [Unreleased] items into the new version section dated today, summarizing the Leveret v1 work.
4. Update CLAUDE.md / README.md / SPEC.md if command counts, structure, or the §1/§2 standalone-vs-feature framing changed (fold the §0 resolution into SPEC.md per LEVERET.md §0.11).
5. Commit the changes.

Depends on the acceptance scenario (warren-9f47) passing — this gates the release.

Acceptance: package.json and src/index.ts carry the same bumped MINOR version; CHANGELOG has a dated section; bun run check:all passes; changes committed.

title: Workspace prep: install project git hooks before handing workspace to agent (lint-failing commits reached PR #290)
description:
<!-- seeds:plan-backref:start -->
Adopted into plan pl-247b.

Parent seed: warren-6daa — Batch: clear orphan techdebt backlog
Plan template: feature

Run `sd plan show pl-247b` for the full plan (context, alternatives, sibling steps, acceptance criteria).
<!-- seeds:plan-backref:end -->

PR #290's agent committed Biome lint failures (src/cli/output.test.ts formatting, src/client/types.test.ts import order) because the sandbox workspace is a fresh clone where the pre-commit gate was never armed: the hook only activates via the 'prepare' script ('git config core.hooksPath scripts/hooks'), which runs on 'bun install' — something the agent never did before its first commit. Warren's own reap bookkeeping commits also use --no-verify by design (warren-27d3, src/runs/reap/stage.ts), and there is no pre-push hook, so nothing gated the branch until CI went red post-push.

Fix: warren's workspace preparation should arm the project's git hooks before the agent gets the workspace — e.g. run the project's install/prepare step, or detect and apply a hooksPath convention. IMPORTANT: this must be a generic mechanism in warren's workspace-prep path applied to ALL projects warren dispatches runs against, not a warren-repo one-off — any project relying on prepare-installed hooks currently has ungated agent commits. Keep the warren-27d3 carve-out: warren's internal bookkeeping commits at reap stay --no-verify; only agent-authored commits should face the project's hooks. Consider also surfacing a knob in .warren/config.yaml if some projects want hooks off for agents.

Observed on conv_4v2b2rewmq6r: both run_yyrcrhw4et4f and rewake run_vj66cemfx7q4 failed never_started ('agent pi-chat is not registered' burrow-side) with zero operator-visible signal. title: Stream bridge loops forever on burrow socket timeouts; never finalizes the run
description:
<!-- seeds:plan-backref:start -->
Step 3 of plan pl-f700.

Parent seed: warren-3679 — Logging and traceability hardening
Plan template: feature
Plan approach: Quick wins first (spawn instrumentation + durable spawn_failed trail, access/auth-denial logging, log rotation, the af76 correctness fix), then the structural pass (per-run child loggers, non-optional loggers, naming convention) that…

Run `sd plan show pl-f700` for the full plan (context, alternatives, sibling steps, acceptance criteria).
<!-- seeds:plan-backref:end -->

src/server/bridge-reconnect.ts runWithReconnect only finalizes a stuck run when burrow returns a clean 404 (burrowRunMissing === true -> reconcileLostBurrowRun -> failed/burrow_run_lost) or when a terminal event is observed. When burrow is up but unresponsive (socket probe times out: 'burrow unreachable: operation timed out'), the bridge result is errored:true with burrowRunMissing:false. That path backs off and reconnects INDEFINITELY - it emits a one-shot cosmetic bridge_stalled event but never finalizes the warren run.

Observed: run_xmz3vgw0ehs9 (burrow_run_id run_85bbv8pk6rty) wedged in state 'running' forever after burrow stalled at 16:19 UTC; bridge logged attempts 1..5+ with 'bridge stalled: burrow unreachable across consecutive reconnects' and never gave up. Root burrow cause tracked separately (burrow-05cb: 1.83 GB bloated db.sqlite stalling burrow's event loop).

Fix:
- Add a hard stall ceiling to runWithReconnect: once bridge_stalled fires (or after a wall-clock budget), call reconcileLostBurrowRun / finalize the run as failed (e.g. failure_reason='burrow_unreachable') instead of looping forever. bridge_stalled is currently only a UI signal, not a finalizer.
- Ensure reap actually destroys the workspace - run_eabjxqramzwd reaped with workspaceDestroyed:false and its bwrap/pi sandbox leaked on the host.

title: Leveret repair arc: live intent shaping end-to-end
description: Get Leveret working per LEVERET.md §0. Phases 1,2,4,5,6 shipped; the broken core is Phase 3: no conversation stream bridge (warren-ce65), ../plot ACL still blocks agent intent_edited, assistant turns never persisted to messages, merge poller off by default, propose_intent extension unproven in-sandbox. Operator approved widening EVENT_ACL.intent_edited to [user,agent] per §0.6.

title: Logging and traceability hardening
description: Audit findings (2026-06-10): spawnRun has zero logging and rollback swallows failures (runs die cancelled with no trail); no HTTP access log; auth denials silent; warren-af76 bridge loops forever on burrow socket timeouts; no per-run child loggers; optional-logger pattern allows silent drops; docker-compose has no log rotation. Cost/token data IS already persisted (runs.cost_usd).
closeReason: Anchoring plan pl-f700 done; all 6 children closed (warren-c686/fc6e/af76/26c2/9f06/b2dd) and PRs #448/#449/#451/#452/#453 merged on main.

title: warren plan run <plan-id>: thin HTTP client CLI for cloud plan-runs
description: Replace os-eco/scripts/run-plan.sh with a CLI that dispatches plan-runs against a remote warren (WARREN_BASE_URL + WARREN_API_TOKEN) and live-tails children. Server surface complete (POST /plan-runs, GET /plan-runs/:id, cancel, NDJSON union stream /plan-runs/:id/events?follow=1). WarrenClient SDK exists in src/client/ but lacks cancelPlanRun + streamPlanRunEvents. New CLI command is the first thin-HTTP-client command (no withCliDb).
closeReason: plan pl-55df done; all 4 children closed; CLI shipped on main (src/cli/commands/plan-run.ts, PR #455)

title: Monorepo support: onboard kota-monorepo as a warren project
description: Investigation (2026-06-10): kota-monorepo works today as a single warren project zero-code (burrow worktrees, agents at repo root, one branch/PR per run). Track onboarding + the coarseness follow-ups: root-only feature detection, sparse/partial clone if checkout size bites, R-20 colonies for cross-repo policy later. True multi-repo burrow workspaces deliberately deferred.

title: gatewatch: PR #340 commits stray .pi/sessions agent transcript not named in its title
description: Article I (Merges are truthful): a PR's title describes its diff. Commit d7d0002d (#340) 'Harmonize app-side truthy env parsers on 1/true/yes/on' is titled and scoped as an env-parser change (src/server/config.ts, probe.ts, main/utils.ts + tests). The diff ALSO adds an undisclosed 57-line agent session transcript: .pi/sessions/2026-06-12T09-08-20-735Z_019ebb16-e67f-7a77-ba58-2dfff553a845.jsonl . This file is a leaked pi run log (full prompt + thinking + tool calls), unrelated to the stated work and unmentioned in the subject/body. No gate caught the inclusion and the title hid it. Evidence: SHA d7d0002d (#340); file .pi/sessions/2026-06-12T09-08-20-735Z_019ebb16-e67f-7a77-ba58-2dfff553a845.jsonl (57 lines added). Article I requires the diff to match the title; an unrelated artifact rode in silently. No open seed covers this (sd search '.pi/sessions' / 'session log' returned only unrelated closed seeds).

title: Leveret UI: render the pi-chat event vocabulary in the conversation surface
description:
The conversation split-view (src/ui/src/pages/ConversationDetail.tsx -> Chat.tsx -> chat-messages.ts) renders NOTHING from a Leveret run because it only materializes the legacy brainstorm event kinds user_message/agent_message (chat-messages.ts USER_KIND/AGENT_KIND). A pi-chat run never emits those; its stream is text/thinking/tool_use/tool_result/state_change. Confirmed live: run_f9n6dfapnkz0 (conv_q3r89mnnksd6) produced 1139 events and the operator saw an empty conversation.

Scope (UI only):
- Teach chat-messages.ts + Chat.tsx pi's vocabulary:
  - text -> agent bubble (payload.text)
  - tool_use / tool_result -> COMPACT one-line activity rows (tool name + short arg, e.g. 'bash: cd /workspace && ...', 'propose_intent'). Decision: compact one-liners.
  - thinking -> MUTED / COLLAPSIBLE block. Decision: muted+collapsible.
  - keep user_message/agent_message mapping for back-compat with the still-present brainstorm path.
- Extend the ChatMessage kind union (currently user|agent) + Bubble styling for the new tool/thinking kinds; update src/ui/src/api/types.ts event types as needed.
- Header: surface anchoring-run working/idle/failed state so the surface never looks dead (overlaps warren-203d).
- Mind the transcript<->stream dedupe in buildChatMessages once assistant turns persist (see backend seed).

Note: visible fix but only shows live data for the run lifetime unless the backend keeps the run alive + persists turns (companion backend seed).

--- Investigation evidence (2026-06-13, conv_q3r89mnnksd6 / warren-dogfood DB) ---
- Event-kind breakdown for run_f9n6dfapnkz0: text:6, thinking:6, tool_use:10, tool_result:10, state_change:77, telemetry:176, mulch.record.skipped:850, reap.*:4 (~1139 total). Only the 6 text events are real assistant turns; the 850 mulch.record.skipped + 176 telemetry are noise the surface must NOT turn into bubbles.
- Root cause pinned: chat-messages.ts toMessage() returns null for every kind except user_message/agent_message, so the live stream contributes 0 bubbles. transcriptRowToMessage maps user->user, assistant->agent, and drops system/tool rows.
- ConversationDetail passes runId={anchoringRunId} + transcript={conversation.data?.messages} into Chat; buildChatMessages merges both. Today BOTH sides are empty of agent content: the transcript has only the user row (see warren-df71), so the pane shows just the operator prompt.
- Render allowlist: materialize text (+ tool_use/tool_result/thinking per the decisions above) and explicitly ignore telemetry / mulch.record.skipped / reap.* / state_change carriers so the 850 noise events never reach the list.
- Re-anchoring: warren-df71's keep-alive rotates anchoring_run_id on re-wake; Chat is keyed on runId, so verify the event stream rebinds (and transcript+stream dedupe still holds) after the run rotates.

title: Leveret backend: keep conversation runs alive, persist assistant turns, wire propose_intent
description:
A Leveret conversation run is treated like a one-shot batch run, so it dies on the agent's first turn and its work never reaches the UI. Confirmed live on conv_q3r89mnnksd6 / run_f9n6dfapnkz0: agent ran ~70s, called propose_intent with a full 4-field intent, asked a follow-up, emitted agent_end, then was finalized succeeded + reaped (opened/merged junk PR #354 'chore(warren): plot state'). Plot plot-781dea58 intent is still empty; messages table holds only the user turn.

Three backend gaps (all must be Leveret/conversation scoped only):
1. KEEP-ALIVE (mode==conversation ONLY): src/runs/stream/bridge.ts -> detectRuntimeTerminal (terminal-detect.ts) treats pi agent_end as terminal for every run; bridges.start() isn't even passed run.mode. Thread mode through bridges.start/bridgeRunStream and, when mode==='conversation', treat agent_end as TURN-end (persist usage, keep run 'running', do NOT break/inline-reap). MUST NOT change any non-conversation run's lifecycle. This also stops the spurious PRs (reap currently only skips workspace_destroy for conversation runs).
2. PERSIST ASSISTANT TURNS (warren-b9a2): nothing writes role:assistant to messages -- repos.messages.append is only called for user turns in conversations.ts. Add a conversation-aware path that persists assistant text (and optionally tool turns) on turn_end/agent_end so reload + re-wake replay aren't blank.
3. WIRE propose_intent -> intent_edited(actor=leveret): the extension emits details.intent_patch on tool_execution_end (confirmed in event log) but no host-side code reads it. Parse it off the stream and write through the plot client so the IntentPane (already polling /plots/:id every 4s) fills in.

Pairs with the UI seed warren-4ccc (render pi vocabulary). Existing related: warren-b9a2, warren-87fe, warren-9d99, warren-203d.

--- Investigation evidence (2026-06-13, conv_q3r89mnnksd6 / warren-dogfood DB biaxgkadzaruhrqsjnmi) ---
DB proof:
- runs: run_f9n6dfapnkz0 mode=conversation state=succeeded, started 17:53:16 -> ended 17:54:26 (~70s), pr_url .../pull/354; rendered_agent_json carries runtime pi-chat (so warren DID dispatch pi-chat; the kept-alive is what's missing).
- messages: exactly 1 row for conv_q3r89mnnksd6 (seq 1, role=user); ZERO assistant rows. conversations.last_activity_at == created_at (never bumped after the run ran).
- plots: plot-781dea58 state_json.intent = {goal:'', non_goals:[], constraints:[], success_criteria:[]} -- empty despite propose_intent being called. PR #354 therefore committed an empty intent.

Code anchors:
1. KEEP-ALIVE: src/runs/stream/bridge.ts is the SOLE writer and only touches the events table (no conversation/messages awareness at all). detectRuntimeTerminal (src/runs/stream/terminal-detect.ts) maps pi agent_end -> succeeded/failed for ALL runs. bridges.start(runId, burrowRunId, burrowId) is called from conversations.ts (create + re-wake) WITHOUT run.mode -> thread mode in. reap already skips workspace_destroy for conversation runs (src/runs/reap/destroy.ts) but still branch-pushes + opens PR; also gate pr-open / plot-commit / branch-push on mode!=='conversation' to kill the junk PR.
2. PERSIST ASSISTANT TURNS: grep confirms repos.messages.append fires ONLY in src/server/handlers/conversations.ts (lines 156 create, 235 post-message), both role=user. MISLEADING COMMENT: conversations.ts:209 claims the reply is 'persisted by the conversation bridge, warren-ce65' -- no such code exists. Either implement it or delete the comment.
3. WIRE propose_intent: the extension (src/registry/builtins/leveret.ts) is a pure carrier -- execute() returns details:{intent_patch} and writes nothing. No host-side reader exists (grep: tool_execution_end / intent_patch appear only in leveret.ts comments). Parse details.intent_patch off the tool_execution_end stdout event, correlate by toolCallId, and write via the plot client intent path (src/plots/intent-editor.ts, which already refreshes the plots projection) as intent_edited(actor=leveret).

Architecture note: the burrow did NOT kill this run -- it reaped because pi agent_end was treated as terminal. Running pi at the warren-container level is unnecessary for this fix and would drop the sandbox around leveret's bash tool.

title: Enable RLS on conversations + messages tables (dogfood Supabase exposes them to anon key)
description:
<!-- seeds:plan-backref:start -->
Adopted into plan pl-c34d.

Parent seed: warren-68c9 — Cluster C burndown: self-contained warren bugs + chores (audit 2026-06-21)
Plan template: feature

Run `sd plan show pl-c34d` for the full plan (context, alternatives, sibling steps, acceptance criteria).
<!-- seeds:plan-backref:end -->

The warren-dogfood Supabase project (biaxgkadzaruhrqsjnmi, used by warren-deployed via WARREN_DB_URL) has Row Level Security DISABLED on public.conversations and public.messages. Supabase's advisor flags this as critical: anyone with the project anon/publishable key can read or modify every row in those two tables (full transcript + steering message contents).

All other warren tables (runs, events, plots, plan_runs, etc.) have RLS enabled; conversations + messages are the only gaps (they were created with rls_enabled=false).

Fix: enable RLS on both. Warren connects via a direct Postgres role (WARREN_DB_URL), which bypasses RLS, so enabling RLS with no anon policies is safe and will NOT break warren's own access — it only closes the anon-key read/write hole.

  ALTER TABLE public.conversations ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
  ALTER TABLE public.messages ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;

Verify warren can still read/write conversations after the change (it should, via the service/owner role). Consider also auditing why these two tables shipped without RLS while the rest have it (schema/migration parity) so new tables don't regress. Ref: Supabase advisor rls_disabled, https://supabase.com/docs/guides/database/postgres/row-level-security

title: Resumable plan-runs: POST /plan-runs/:id/resume to re-drive a failed row after a merge-timeout (Option A)
description:
PROBLEM
A plan-run fails terminally when a child PR cannot auto-merge in time. Seen live on plan pl-da54 (plan-run plnr_z1bmdqkbhazh): step 2 child warren-7f62 opened PR #361, which touches Article IX constitution-protected paths (.warren/triggers.yaml, .canopy/prompts.jsonl, agents/*.md), so .github/workflows/auto-merge.yml deliberately did NOT enable auto-merge. The PR sat open awaiting a human merge; the coordinator's merge clock (src/plan-runs/merge-gate.ts mergeDeadlineExceeded, default 30m from run.endedAt) elapsed, flipping the child to failed (child_pr_merge_timeout) and the plan-run to terminal 'failed'. Steps 3-5 (warren-4240, warren-6022, warren-05ef) never ran.

Why current 'resume' is insufficient: 'failed' is terminal and repos.planRuns.listActive() only returns queued/running, so the coordinator never re-touches the row. The SPEC §11.P re-dispatch resume (a fresh POST /plan-runs with the same planId that skips closed children) doesn't fit the merge-timeout case: the failed child's seed (warren-7f62) is already CLOSED while its PR is still unmerged, so a plain re-dispatch would skip it (its work hasn't landed on main) and bypass the per-child merge gate.

OPTION A (chosen)
Add POST /plan-runs/:id/resume that re-drives the SAME plan-run row instead of creating a new one:
- Allow a 'failed' -> 'running' transition (src/db/repos/plan-runs.ts ALLOWED_TRANSITIONS; today failed:[]). Gate the endpoint so only plan-runs whose failureReason is a merge-timeout are resumable.
- Reset the merge-timed-out child from 'failed' back to 'pr_open', preserving its runId/prUrl, and RE-ARM its merge clock (the deadline derives from run.endedAt which is now stale, so it must be rebaselined - e.g. bump a per-child merge-wait baseline or clear/reset endedAt usage in mergeDeadlineExceeded - so the coordinator doesn't immediately re-timeout). The coordinator then re-polls the EXISTING PR: if the human merged it, advance to the next child; if still open, wait again within a fresh budget.
- Cover BOTH timeout shapes: child child_pr_merge_timeout (coordinator.ts handleInFlight) AND the parent gate parent_pr_merge_timeout (merge-gate.ts checkParentRunMerged / failParentGate). For the parent case, resume re-arms the parent gate clock and re-polls the parent run's PR.

Out of scope (deferred): pr_closed_without_merge and dispatch_failed / child_seed_not_found failures (genuine failures, not human-merge stalls); Option B's non-terminal 'stalled' auto-resume state.

ACCEPTANCE
- POST /plan-runs/:id/resume on a child-merge-timeout plan-run whose PR has since merged advances to the next pending child (no new run dispatched for the already-completed child; no skip of unmerged work).
- Same on a parent_pr_merge_timeout plan-run.
- Resume on a plan-run that is not in a merge-timeout failure state returns a typed 4xx (e.g. 409/422), no state change.
- Resume while the PR is still open re-arms the merge clock so it does NOT immediately re-fail on the stale run.endedAt.
- New route documented in docs/http-api.md + docs/openapi.yaml (gen:docs / gen:openapi), unit tests for the handler + coordinator reset path, and SPEC §11.P 'Resume semantics' updated to describe same-row resume alongside the existing re-dispatch flow.

CODE ANCHORS
- src/plan-runs/coordinator.ts (handleInFlight: child merge-timeout -> plan_failed)
- src/plan-runs/merge-gate.ts (mergeDeadlineExceeded, checkParentRunMerged, failParentGate)
- src/db/repos/plan-runs.ts (ALLOWED_TRANSITIONS, transitionTo, updateChild, listActive)
- src/db/schema/columns.ts (PLAN_RUN_STATES, PLAN_RUN_TERMINAL_STATES)
- src/server/handlers/plan-runs.ts (cancelPlanRunHandler is the closest template for a state-changing :id action) + route table in src/server/handlers/index.ts
- SPEC §11.P 'Resume semantics on re-dispatch.'

title: Auditor-autonomy-promotion recommendations: implement tastewatch precision-table reader and structured promotion-proposal emitter for the weekly warden digest
description:
Deferred non-goal from warren-d0ed / warren-05ef (pl-da54 step 5 close-out).

CONTEXT
The warden digest design (LEVERET.md §0.15) specifies that tastewatch's weekly
digest may *recommend* autonomy promotions for auditors whose precision table
shows a track record of high-signal findings. That recommendation surface was
explicitly deferred as a non-goal of the thin-slice (pl-da54).

WORK
1. Define the tastewatch precision table schema: one record per auditor
   (gatewatch, ratchetwatch) tracking: findings_filed, findings_acted_on,
   rolling_precision (acted_on / filed, 4-week window), promotion_recommended_at.
   Storage: a .mulch expertise record updated by tastewatch each digest run
   (no new DB table; mulch is the right home for agent-maintained longitudinal
   data).

2. Update the tastewatch system prompt (.canopy/prompts.jsonl, version bump) to:
   a. Read the mulch precision record for each auditor.
   b. Compute rolling precision.
   c. When precision >= threshold (initially 0.8 over >= 4 weekly runs with
      findings), emit a structured promotion proposal: a seed proposing an
      Article IX change (auto_plan_run: true on the auditor's triggers.yaml
      entry + canopy agent update) and deliver it to the warden conversation.
   d. Update the mulch precision record with this week's counts.

3. The promotion proposal MUST: be filed as a seed (not auto-applied),
   reference Article IX, and carry enough evidence (precision table rows,
   specific finding examples) for the human reviewer.

ACCEPTANCE
- After N >= 4 weekly runs where an auditor has filed >= 1 finding and
  >= 80% were acted on, tastewatch's digest includes a promotion proposal
  seed citing the precision evidence.
- When precision is below threshold, no proposal is emitted.
- The mulch precision record is updated each digest run regardless of
  whether a proposal is emitted.
- Article IX protected: the generated seed does not auto-merge; it requires
  explicit human review (the proposal is a seed, not an in-run mutation).
- The promotion recommendation surface is documented in LEVERET.md §0.15
  (amendment or addendum to the precision-table paragraph).

DEPENDENCIES
- Requires the warden thin-slice (pl-da54) to be fully closed and operational.
- Tastewatch's existing canopy entry (version 3) is the starting point;
  bump to version 4.

title: Add check:client-contract gate — assert UI client request surface matches the server OpenAPI/ROUTE_TABLE
description:
MOTIVATION: The retired brainstorm/interactive/formalize surface (warren-d622) left dead UI client code that check:all could not catch — none of the gates span the client<->server HTTP seam. plotsApi.startBrainstorm POSTs to /brainstorm (not in ROUTE_TABLE -> 404) and runsApi.createInteractive sends mode:'interactive' (no longer in RUN_MODES) yet both are internally type-consistent, so lint/typecheck/knip/coverage all pass. gen:openapi:check validates server ROUTE_TABLE <-> docs/openapi.yaml but faces the wrong way (never checks the client).

PROPOSAL: A new ratchet gate (scripts/check-client-contract.ts, added to GATES in scripts/check-all.ts and CI) that statically extracts every request path+method the UI API client issues (src/ui/src/api/client.ts request<>() call sites) and asserts each one exists in the generated docs/openapi.yaml (derived from ROUTE_TABLE). Fail on any client call to a path/method the server does not expose. Stretch: also assert shared enums the UI redeclares (e.g. run mode) match the server's canonical union, or better, have the UI import the canonical RUN_MODES contract instead of redeclaring it.

NOTES: Reuse the same brace-counting/extraction approach gen:openapi already uses for ROUTE_TABLE. Keep it byte-quiet per the check-all output contract. This is the class of bug the gatewatch/ratchetwatch auditors are meant to surface — wiring it as a gate closes the loop. Found while planning pl-0008 (Workspace collapse), parent warren-dde5.

After the Workspace collapse lands (steps 1-13, ending warren-3f94 with check:all green), cut a release by running the .claude/commands/release.md flow with an explicit MINOR bump: review git log since the last tag, bump the version in BOTH package.json ("version") and src/index.ts (export const VERSION) to the next minor (X.Y+1.0 -> e.g. patch/minor reset), move CHANGELOG.md [Unreleased] items into the new dated version section, update CLAUDE.md/README.md if command counts or structure changed, then commit. The release workflow (.github/workflows/release.yml) fails if package.json and src/index.ts disagree, so keep them in sync. This is a minor (not patch) bump because the Workspace collapse is a user-facing surface change (Leveret + Plots merged into one tabbed Workspace, brainstorm/formalize path removed).

title: gatewatch: 4 Article IX-protected merges in window lack human sign-off record
description:
Constitution Article IX requires that any merged change to docs/CONSTITUTION.md, the gatewatch/ratchetwatch/tastewatch entries in .canopy/, or audit entries in .warren/triggers.yaml carry EXPLICIT human review, recorded in the seed tracker (the warren-ea20 ratification-record pattern: 'explicitly human-reviewed and human-merged by the operator... This seed is the sign-off evidence gatewatch's Article IX check looks for'). Four PRs merged in the last 36h touch protected paths and NONE has a corresponding ratification/sign-off seed. The auto-merge.yml Article IX gate (intact, untouched in window) would have BLOCKED auto-merge on these, implying a human merge occurred — but with no recorded ratification, gatewatch cannot verify the gate was honest. Per gatewatch mandate item 6, file priority 1.

EVIDENCE (each modifies its own mandate / the constitution):
- SHA 7d3cf95e (PR #364): docs/CONSTITUTION.md — adds the 'Audit Warden boundary' section (ingestion rule, digest cadence, meta-Plot, autonomy-promotion constraints). Closes warren-05ef (a WORK seed, not a sign-off record).
- SHA e2d88409 (PR #362): .warren/triggers.yaml — adds the 'warden-digest' cron audit entry (0 5 * * 0). The constitution explicitly names warden-digest as Article IX-protected. Closes warren-4240 (work seed).
- SHA f8ead2ff (PR #361): .canopy/prompts.jsonl — bumps gatewatch (v2), ratchetwatch (v2), tastewatch (v3) auditor system prompts to add warden-delivery instructions; ALSO edits the three audit prompt entries in .warren/triggers.yaml. This is the audit population editing its own running mandate (including this very gatewatch prompt). Work seed warren-7f62, not a ratification record.
- SHA 5f41fef5 (PR #338): .canopy/prompts.jsonl — repins the tastewatch agent model claude-fable-5 -> claude-opus-4-8 (tastewatch entry v1 -> v2). Modifies a protected auditor .canopy entry. No ratification record. (Same PR also adds a biome.json formatter-exempt override carrying tracker pl-cf2a — Article II satisfied there; the Article IX gap is the .canopy auditor change.)

WHAT ARTICLE IX REQUIRES: a recorded human sign-off (warren-ea20-style) per protected-path merge, OR revert. The gate forcing human-merge is necessary but not sufficient — the constitution treats the recorded ratification seed as the audit evidence.

REMEDIATION IS NON-MECHANICAL (no plan attached): the fix is a human decision — file a ratification record for each PR (#364, #362, #361, #338) attesting the operator reviewed and merged it, or revert. A mechanical plan-run cannot self-grant this, and any plan touching these protected paths would itself re-trigger Article IX. Routed to the warden for human triage.

Dedupe: not covered by warren-d5e5 (PR #270), warren-4c8d (PR #340), or warren-ea20 (PR #337 only). warden delivery: undeliverable this patrol (WARREN_API_TOKEN unset -> /conversations 401); seed is the durable record.

title: tastewatch digest: 2026-06-14
description:
tastewatch weekly taste digest — 2026-06-14 (window 2026-06-07 → 06-14, 96 non-merge commits; first digest, no prior baseline).

## 1. Verdict table (stratified sample, n=10)

| sha | subject (trunc) | verdict | article |
|-----|-----------------|---------|---------|
| 3713d7ba | Split projects.settings.test.ts into siblings (#353) | conforms | II/IV |
| 84e339fe | Decompose plots.list.test.ts (700→siblings) (#345) | conforms | II |
| c61172fb | Implement the Shape tab (Workspace) (#378) | diverges | I |
| 646ddf3e | Collapse nav + routing → Workspace (#382) | diverges | I |
| 1d062183 | Per-agent/per-trigger spend caps (#370) | diverges | I |
| 8ef32cb4 | pi per-agent tool allow/denylist (#371) | conforms | IV |
| 119c8fa4 | auto_plan_run string/bool trap fix (#365) | diverges | I |
| 14464837 | Default new agents to pi runtime (#368) | conforms | I |
| c3544556 | Normalize co-author identities (#367) | conforms | VII |
| d7d0002d | Harmonize app-side truthy env parsers (#340) | diverges | I |

Sampling basis this week: 3 largest by churn (3713d7ba 1390, 84e339fe 1069, c61172fb 973); 3 mid-distribution chosen by "feature commits carrying branching logic near the churn median" (8ef32cb4, 119c8fa4, 14464837 — basis varied to stay unpredictable); 2 releases (see §3); 2 patrol-produced (c3544556 Article VII normalization, d7d0002d ratchet/harmonize child). Two releases sampled separately in §3.

Substance note: the five "diverges" verdicts are NOT five independent quality failures. 1d062183 and 119c8fa4 in particular carry exemplary work — 1d062183 ships adversarial budget/cost-cap tests (budget.test +109, cost-cap.test +73, schema.triggers.test), and 119c8fa4 is a model Article IV fix (asserts boolean true, string "true", " TRUE ", "false", and malformed frontmatter). All five diverge for a single shared reason: each commit drags an unrelated `.pi/sessions/*.jsonl` agent transcript into its diff (see §3).

## 2. Divergence rate

This week: 5/10 = 50%. Last week: no prior digest exists — this is the first tastewatch digest, so there is no baseline to compare against. Caveat: the rate is inflated by one systemic cause; on substance (Article IV/V/VII/idiom) the sample is clean.

## 3. Most important divergence — tracked `.pi/sessions/` transcripts ride unrelated commits

`.pi/sessions/` is absent from `.gitignore`; 41 agent-session transcripts are tracked, and at least 19 of the 96 window commits each append one to an otherwise-unrelated diff. Evidence: 1d062183 (spend caps) adds `.pi/sessions/2026-06-14T00-42-35-784Z_019ec394-...jsonl` at +196 lines — the single largest file in that diff, dwarfing the actual feature files (budget.ts +111). c61172fb, 646ddf3e, 119c8fa4, and d7d0002d each carry the same stray artifact. This is an Article I scope violation (the merge contains substantial content its title does not describe) and an Article VIII-grade evidence trail. gatewatch flagged exactly ONE instance (warren-4c8d, PR #340 = d7d0002d) as a stray, naming it a one-off; it is in fact population-wide and recurring nightly. The right fix is mechanical and one-line-cheap (gitignore + a guard), so the cost of leaving it is pure noise accretion in history and review.

Secondary (fix-on-fix) finding: five "Harmonize … parser" commits — a2ed4c98 (06-10), d7d0002d + a3b6bf18 (06-12), e110675a + 02503ea2 (06-13) — patch the same env/enum-parsing class one call site at a time within ~72h. Missing-test-class / missing-seam signal: there is no single canonical `parseBoolEnv` / `parseEnumEnv` helper, so each call site drifted independently and is being harmonized retail. The class to test once and centralize: case-insensitive truthy/allow-list env parsing with a `; got <raw>` error suffix.

## 4. Auditor-population precision

Window = all auditor seeds to date (no prior digest to bound it).

- **ratchetwatch:** 12 filed, 7 closed — ALL 7 closed-as-fixed (warren-a715, warren-6d84, warren-8ff2, warren-8e04, warren-400d, warren-1339, warren-785b; landed via merged PRs #353/#345/#344), 0 wontfix, 5 still open (warren-db9a, warren-70d7, warren-889a, warren-60ee, warren-c65d). Precision of closed findings = 7/7 = 100% fixed. Strong, actionable signal.
- **gatewatch:** 3 filed (warren-d426 p1, warren-4c8d p2, warren-d5e5 p2), 0 closed → precision not yet measurable (no closures). Note: warren-4c8d under-scoped the session-leak class as a single-PR stray (§3); when re-judged its precision should be assessed on the systemic framing, not the instance.

Autonomy-promotion read: ratchetwatch shows the high-precision track record the warden boundary (CONSTITUTION "Autonomy-promotion recommendations") would key on, but only over a partial window with no rolling baseline. RECOMMENDATION: hold promotion one more weekly cycle to establish a ≥2-digest trend before recommending any auto_plan_run grant. No promotion recommended this week. (Advisory only — any change to `.canopy/` / `.warren/triggers.yaml` requires human review per Article IX.)

## 5. Proposed gate (one; propose-only per Article IX)

Add a `check:no-session-transcripts` gate to `scripts/check-all.ts` (and CI), paired with a `.gitignore` entry:

```
# .gitignore
.pi/sessions/
```

Gate behavior: fail any commit/PR whose diff adds or modifies `.pi/sessions/*.jsonl` unless the commit is an explicit session-archive/`seeds: sync`-class chore. Concretely: `git diff --name-only <base>..<head>` filtered to `^\.pi/sessions/` must be empty for any commit whose subject is not in the archival allowlist. This converts the manual Article I judgment in §3 into an executable gate (the constitution's stated aspiration: "every article aspires to become a gate"). Optional amendment companion: a one-line addendum to Article I — "generated agent-session transcripts are not part of a feature diff." Proposed, not applied.

## 6. Trajectory

Holding — substantive craft is strong (adversarial tests, consistent idiom, both releases consumer-observable, Article VII actively enforced), but a systemic hygiene leak is accreting unflagged; tightening is gated on the one-line fix in §5.

title: gatewatch: PR #375 (678f6284) deletion-titled, comments-only diff — Article I title/diff mismatch
description:
Article I ('Merges are truthful — a PR's title and description describe its diff') violation: the squashed PR title claims file deletions the diff does not contain.

EVIDENCE:
- SHA 678f6284 (PR #375), merged 2026-06-13 19:07 -0700.
- Squashed subject: 'Delete the dead UI panels. Remove src/ui/src/pages/plot-detail/interactive-panel.tsx and its usage in PlotDetail.tsx (InteractivePanel — Start brainstorming / Run planner / Formalize ...). Remove StartBrainstormDialog + the Start brainstorming button from src/ui/src/pages/Plots.tsx ...'
- Actual diff (git show --stat 678f6284): 7 files changed, 15 insertions(+), 15 deletions(-). NO file deletion. Touches only Chat.tsx, DefaultLanding.tsx, PlotDetail.tsx, plot-detail/helpers.ts, plot-detail/run-plan.tsx (comment/reference sweep) + .seeds/.mulch bookkeeping.
- interactive-panel.tsx (526 lines) and Plots.tsx StartBrainstormDialog (163 lines) named in this title were actually deleted in the PRIOR PR #374 / SHA b74a11ab. The commit body admits this: 'The dead interactive-panel.tsx and Plots.tsx StartBrainstormDialog were already removed by the coupled warren-b265 API-client sweep (b74a11ab).'

WHAT ARTICLE I REQUIRES: the title describes the diff. A deletion-titled merge must contain the deletion. Here the squashed PR title (the canonical surface in merged history/changelog) claims specific file removals that are absent from its diff; the work landed under a different PR's title. The honest body does not cure the mismatched subject.

ROOT CAUSE (shared with companion finding for b74a11ab/#374): pl-0008 plan steps 3 (warren-b265, client sweep) and 4 (warren-af60, 'Delete the dead UI panels') executed with their content swapped — step 4's deletions landed inside step 3's PR, and step 4's PR title (this one) shipped only a comment sweep. Two coupled Article I findings, one root cause; under threshold for a remediation plan.

DELIVERY: warden conversation undeliverable this patrol (GET /conversations -> 401, no WARREN_API_TOKEN in sandbox; same condition as warren-d426). This seed is the durable record.

title: gatewatch: PR #374 (b74a11ab) 'API-client sweep' title hides 689-line UI panel deletion — Article I
description:
Article I ('Merges are truthful') violation, inverse shape: a diff carrying major changes the title hides.

EVIDENCE:
- SHA b74a11ab (PR #374), merged 2026-06-13 19:01 -0700.
- Squashed subject scopes itself to client types only: 'UI API-client sweep — remove the dead client methods and types: plotsApi.startBrainstorm (+ StartBrainstormInput/Response), runsApi.createInteractive, and plotsApi.formalize (+ formalize request/response types) from src/ui/src/api/client.ts and src/ui/src/api/types.ts.'
- Actual diff (git show --stat b74a11ab) ALSO deletes whole UI panels the title never mentions:
    src/ui/src/pages/plot-detail/interactive-panel.tsx | 526 ------------ (full file delete)
    src/ui/src/pages/Plots.tsx                          | 163 +------ (StartBrainstormDialog gutted)
  Confirmed via: git log --diff-filter=D -- src/ui/src/pages/plot-detail/interactive-panel.tsx -> deleted in b74a11ab.
- That ~689-line UI-panel removal is exactly the scope of the SEPARATE plan step 4 (warren-af60, 'Delete the dead UI panels'), whose own PR #375 (678f6284) then shipped only a comment sweep — see companion finding warren-a19d.

WHAT ARTICLE I REQUIRES: the title describes the diff. An 'API-client sweep — remove dead client methods and types' subject must not silently carry a 689-line cross-file UI component deletion belonging to a different tracked unit of work. Reviewers (and the auto-merge gate's only honest signal — the title) cannot see step-4 deletions under a step-3 title.

ROOT CAUSE: pl-0008 steps 3 (warren-b265) and 4 (warren-af60) executed with content swapped. Coupled with warren-a19d; one root cause, two merged commits, both Article I. Under the 3-finding threshold for a remediation plan — remediation is non-mechanical (history is merged; the fix is process discipline, not a revert).

DELIVERY: warden conversation undeliverable this patrol (GET /conversations -> 401, no WARREN_API_TOKEN; same as warren-d426). Seed is the durable record.

title: gatewatch: PR #399 (0b21150b) fix-titled merge ships zero code — dropped warren-5755 fix, phantom-shipped in 0.9.1 — Article I
description:
Article I (Merges are truthful): a fix-titled PR must contain the fix. PR #399 (SHA 0b21150b, merged 2026-06-15 20:05 -0700) carries the verbatim title of bug seed warren-5755 — 'Fix unbounded /workspaces growth: bound the conversation card's ancestor height in src/ui/src/pages/conversation-detail/conversation-surface.tsx (min-h-[60vh] -> a bounded height)...' — but its diff contains NO source change at all. `git show --name-only 0b21150b` = only warren state churn: .plot/plot-b11c5999.events.jsonl (1+), .plot/plot-fbe17883.events.jsonl (3+), .seeds/issues.jsonl (6 +/-). Zero src/ files.

EVIDENCE the fix never landed: conversation-surface.tsx STILL has min-h-[60vh] on lines 82 and 247 (`grep -n 'min-h-\[60vh\]' src/ui/src/pages/conversation-detail/conversation-surface.tsx`); the file has had no commit since c61172fb (#378, 2026-06-13). Meanwhile the same #399 seeds-churn flipped warren-5755 from open->closed with a run id (run_90fqekdeztrt, lastRunAt 2026-06-16T02:57:44Z) — the tracker was marked done while the code change was dropped (a dropped_commit landed as a 'merge').

DOWNSTREAM IMPACT (compounds, not a separate Article III finding): the 0.9.1 release commit ea0ae2ca (#403) CHANGELOG.md [0.9.1] line lists 'bounded transcript scrolling (warren-5755)' as a shipped consumer-observable change. That change does not exist in the codebase, so the release notes claim a fix the diff does not contain. #403 still ships real changes (warren-f4b8 chat ordering/dedup #398, warren-4c8d transcript removal #401), so the release is Article III-meaningful — but the warren-5755 changelog line is phantom.

WHAT ARTICLE I REQUIRES: the merged diff matches the title. Here a fix-titled merge contains no fix, the tracking seed was closed anyway, and the phantom fix propagated into the release notes. Remediation is the actual conversation-surface.tsx bound + reopen warren-5755, or correct the title/changelog — a human/work decision, no mechanical plan attached.

DEDUPE: not covered by warren-d426 (#364/#362/#361/#338, Article IX), warren-172e (#374), warren-a19d (#375), warren-4c8d (closed, #340), or warren-d513 (.pi/sessions systemic). Novel to this patrol.

WARDEN DELIVERY: undeliverable this patrol — GET/POST /conversations on http://localhost:8080 returns 401 (no Authorization header; WARREN_API_TOKEN and all token vars unset), same condition warren-d426 recorded. This seed is the durable record.

title: Auditor sandboxes lack WARREN_API_TOKEN — warden-conversation delivery (POST /conversations/:id/messages) returns 401 for gatewatch/ratchetwatch/tastewatch
description:
<!-- seeds:plan-backref:start -->
Adopted into plan pl-c34d.

Parent seed: warren-68c9 — Cluster C burndown: self-contained warren bugs + chores (audit 2026-06-21)
Plan template: feature

Run `sd plan show pl-c34d` for the full plan (context, alternatives, sibling steps, acceptance criteria).
<!-- seeds:plan-backref:end -->

Evidence (ratchetwatch patrol 2026-06-17):
- The warren API is reachable from the auditor burrow at http://localhost:8080 (e.g. GET/POST /conversations responds), but every request returns: {"error":{"code":"unauthorized","message":"missing Authorization header"}}.
- src/server/auth.ts requires a bearer token from WARREN_API_TOKEN; that env var is NOT present in the auditor sandbox (env shows only ANTHROPIC_API_KEY and WARREN_QUALITY_GATE). No token file is mounted (~/.warren absent; /data unreadable; no *.token anywhere outside node_modules).
- Result: the operating contract added by warren-7f62 (post each finding to the standing 'Audit Warden' conversation over POST /conversations/:id/messages, 202 steering channel) cannot be fulfilled by ANY auditor — gatewatch, ratchetwatch, tastewatch — because they cannot authenticate or even resolve the conversation id (GET /conversations is also 401-gated).
- This is a runtime/provisioning gap, not a prompt gap: warren-7f62 wired the prompts but the auditor runs are not given a credential (or a loopback no-auth exemption, or a pre-resolved conversation id) to reach the channel.
Suggested fix directions (for a human/operator to choose): inject a scoped WARREN_API_TOKEN into auditor burrow env via composeRunEnv, OR expose a loopback no-auth path for the conversation-message endpoint, OR pass the resolved warden conversation id + token through the rendered agent context. Until then, auditors file seeds/plans correctly (this patrol filed warren-1c0d + plan pl-7c4f) but the warden transcript stays empty.

title: Cross-repo plan-runs: route each plan child to its own execution repo workspace
description: Plan-runs are welded to a single project (planRun.projectId). Enable a cross-repo plan whose children live in a meta-repo's .seeds/ but each execute against a different child repo, tagged via extensions.repo. Warren bridges: agent runs in the child repo workspace, warren reads/updates the seed and mirrors plot against the meta-repo (coordination project) host-side. Split spawnRun's single projectId into execution project (workspace) vs seedProjectId (coordination), inline seed content into the prompt so the executor needs no .seeds/ access, and keep the existing serial merge-gate model. No seeds core change; routing knowledge lives in the meta-repo's canopy planner role + custom plan template, backed by a warren pre-dispatch guardrail.

title: Add a `repo` key to the warren-namespaced seeds extensions schema. In src/seeds-cli/warren-extensions.ts add an optional `repo: z.string().min(1)` field to WarrenExtensionsSchema (the project slug or git remote URL a seed's work targets) and export a typed reader (e.g. readTargetRepo(extensions): string | undefined). Confirm src/seeds-cli/schema.ts SeedShowIssue.extensions (z.record(z.string(), z.unknown())) already round-trips `extensions.repo` through `sd show --json`; add a parse case proving it. No seeds CLI change — this is the warren-owned namespace only. Verify: `bun test src/seeds-cli/warren-extensions.test.ts src/seeds-cli/show.test.ts` green with new present/absent/empty-string cases; `bun run typecheck` clean.

title: Wire per-child execution-project routing into the plan-run coordinator + dispatch. In src/plan-runs/dispatch.ts createPlanRunSpawn: for each child read extensions.repo from the seed (showSeed already returns extensions), resolve it to an execution project via resolveTargetProject (fallback to planRun.projectId when the tag is absent), and call spawnRun with projectId = resolved execution project AND seedProjectId = planRun.projectId; load the execution project to derive ref/defaultBranch. Keep coordinator.ts showSeed/close/skip and the plot-append pointed at planRun.projectId (coordination). The merge gate is unchanged (polls the child run's prUrl). LEGIBILITY: include the resolved execution project/repo in the plan_run.dispatched/advanced/merged event payloads AND the Plot mirror, so warren's event log and the coordination Plot are self-describing (a human tailing events or an agent reading Plot can see which repo each child targeted without cross-referencing the run row). Verify: `bun test src/plan-runs/` green incl. new cases for (a) routed child clones the child repo while seed reads hit the meta-repo, (b) untagged child falls back to planRun.projectId, (c) unresolved repo fails that child with a typed reason via the existing plan_failed path, (d) the execution repo appears in the emitted plan_run.* event payloads; `bun run typecheck` clean.
