title: Arm the heartbeat watchdog by default (warren-285d): WARREN_RUN_HEARTBEAT_TIMEOUT_MS is unset on warren-deployed, so silent-but-busy runs never get reaped
description:
## Summary
The heartbeat watchdog (src/runs/watchdog.ts, warren-285d) already does exactly what's needed: each tick scans 'running' runs, computes idle time from the newest event ts (fallback startedAt), and force-fails any run idle past the budget via reapRun(outcome=failed, failureReason=timed_out) — which cancels the burrow run and destroys the workspace (killing the runaway bwrap tree). But it is OPT-IN and only arms when WARREN_RUN_HEARTBEAT_TIMEOUT_MS > 0, and warren-deployed does NOT set it.

## Impact / how found
Run run_kbwwbwqhz8hs (project jayminwest/seeds, plan-run plnr_dhk21sry3ffa) hung on a 100%-CPU seeds busy-spin (`sd plan list --json | grep`, see seeds-18dc). Last event 19:22:55Z; bridge_stalled fired at 19:37:15Z; the run then sat 'running' with a runaway bun process burning ~100% CPU for ~2h until it was killed by hand. Had the watchdog been armed it would have reaped the run automatically. (Killing the hung tool subprocess actually unblocked the agent, which finished and opened PR #96 — but that was manual.)

## Ask
1. Set WARREN_RUN_HEARTBEAT_TIMEOUT_MS to a generous default on warren-deployed (e.g. 30-45 min — well above any legitimately slow-but-silent tool like a cold bun install), via fly secrets / deploy config.
2. Consider making the watchdog on-by-default in code with a generous built-in budget + opt-out env, mirroring the conversation-idle coordinator pattern, so a fresh deploy is protected without an explicit env var.
3. Note: 'conversation' mode runs are already correctly skipped (warren-c770); keep that.

## Pointers
- src/runs/watchdog.ts (loadWatchdogConfigFromEnv, bootWatchdog, tickWatchdog, computeIdleMs)
- detector-wiring.ts (where bootWatchdog should be wired at boot, alongside the idle coordinator)

title: gatewatch: PR #436 (fba09fff) grandfathers newborn src/plan-runs/in-flight.ts into biome.json complexity override at birth — Article II
description:
SHA fba09fff2134eb52ecf3674c3dfc9c6ebf65c175 (PR #436, 'Wire per-child execution-project routing into the plan-run coordinator', warren-d9f3 / pl-fb43 step 5).

EVIDENCE:
- git show --name-status fba09fff -- src/plan-runs/in-flight.ts => 'A' (file created in this same PR; extracted handleInFlight + execution helpers from coordinator.ts).
- biome.json diff (line 82): + "src/plan-runs/in-flight.ts" added to the first overrides block, which sets linter.rules.complexity.noExcessiveCognitiveComplexity = "off". Net change to that grandfather list: +1 entry, no removals (coordinator.ts remains listed).

ARTICLE II requires: ratchets only tighten; 'refactor offenders OUT of the list rather than adding new entries' (CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md cognitive-complexity ratchet, warren-d3a6); 'Nothing is grandfathered at birth.' The complexity-override grandfather list grew by one and the new entry is a file born in the same PR.

WHAT HAPPENED: the extraction landed a new file over the cognitive-complexity-15 ceiling and exempted it at write time instead of simplifying the extracted function below the threshold. Tracker-ref clause IS satisfied (warren-d9f3/pl-fb43 in the diff) and the file-size budget strictly improved (coordinator.ts removed from scripts/file-size-budgets.json) — mitigating context — but the at-birth grandfather of a complexity exemption is independently a finding. Remediation: confirm handleInFlight/in-flight.ts is under complexity 15 and drop the biome.json entry, or split further; do not carry the override.
closeReason: Remediated on main: PR #475 (1466ae81) refactored src/plan-runs/in-flight.ts under cc15 and removed its biome.json complexity override (now lists only coordinator.ts)

- biome.json diff (line 98): + "src/runs/pr-checks.ts" added to the first overrides block (complexity.noExcessiveCognitiveComplexity = "off"). Net change to that grandfather list: +1 entry, no removals (pr.ts remains listed).

ARTICLE II requires: ratchets only tighten; refactor offenders OUT of the grandfather list rather than adding new entries (cognitive-complexity ratchet warren-d3a6); 'Nothing is grandfathered at birth.'

WHAT HAPPENED: commit body states 'mergePullRequest's grandfathered complexity override follows it to pr-checks.ts' — i.e. a pre-existing grandfathered function was relocated and the exemption re-keyed onto a newborn file, growing the complexity-override list by one rather than retiring the exemption. Mitigating context: tracker ref warren-db9a/pl-88bb is in the diff (tracker-ref clause satisfied) and this is a genuine decomposition that drops pr.ts 659->361 lines. But per the bright-line at-birth rule the newborn-file grandfather entry is a finding. Remediation: simplify mergePullRequest below cognitive-complexity 15 and drop the override entry rather than carrying it forward.

title: burndown-watcher: scheduled agent that clusters open seeds into plans and dispatches them (inverse of the filing auditors)
description:
Problem: the audit fleet (nightwatch, gatewatch, ratchetwatch, bugwatch, tastewatch) FILES findings on a cron, but nothing systematically BURNS DOWN the backlog. The 2026-06-21 open-issue audit found ~49 open seeds; most are loose audit findings or sequenced plan steps that never get re-dispatched. The dispatch machinery already exists — plan-runs (SPEC §11.P), the ready-to-dispatch surface (warren-9d6f / SPEC §11.R), and auto_plan_run authority already used by gatewatch + ratchetwatch — but it is never pointed at warrens OWN backlog. The filing loop has no draining counterpart.

Proposal: a new scheduled canopy role (working name "burndown-watcher"; alt: harvestwatch / reapwatch) that runs 1-2x/week as the INVERSE of the filing auditors. Instead of producing findings it CONSUMES the open backlog and turns it into dispatchable work.

Procedure (mirror the audit-agent shape: a role in .canopy/prompts.jsonl + a .warren/triggers.yaml cron entry):
1. Read the open set: sd list / sd ready / sd blocked (+ sd plan list) — exactly like the auditors read history.
2. Cluster open seeds by theme into a few LARGE, multi-step plans via sd plan create + sd plan adopt (link-only; never spawn duplicates). Respect existing plan membership (skip seeds already carrying a plan_id), live blocker edges, and label-based exclusions.
3. Dispatch the safe clusters as serial plan-runs (auto_plan_run authority): one PR per child, merge-gated, resuming from the next open child — exactly how gatewatch/ratchetwatch already dispatch their tightenings.
4. Report the burndown summary to the standing "Audit Warden" conversation over POST /conversations/:id/messages (the existing 202 steering channel; no new endpoint or dispatch path), so a human sees what got clustered and dispatched.

Guardrails (this agent has dispatch authority — treat it like gatewatch/ratchetwatch under docs/CONSTITUTION.md):
- Only auto-cluster + dispatch SELF-CONTAINED code work (bugs/chores in this repo).
- NEVER auto-dispatch: deferred-by-design items (labels roadmap/backlog/vision-v1/R-20, priority P4), governance/Article-IX findings where remediation is a human decision, or cross-repo work blocked on other repos (e.g. Leveret / ../plot).
- Dedupe against open plans; never adopt a seed already in a plan; never re-dispatch a plan that already has an active plan-run (reuse computeReadyPlans / GET /projects/:id/ready-plans as the dedup primitive).
- Article IX: edits to the audit population stay human-gated; for ambiguous/taste clusters, mirror tastewatchs report-only posture — PROPOSE a plan, do not dispatch.
- Cap size: at most N plans (and N children) dispatched per run.

Implementation surface:
- New canopy role prompt in .canopy/prompts.jsonl (system + burrow_config + auto_plan_run frontmatter; auto_plan_run_agent pi or claude-code).
- New .warren/triggers.yaml cron entry (e.g. weekly, off-peak), with the Article IX human-review note since it joins the audit population.
- Reuses the existing plan-run dispatch path and ready-plans readiness logic (src/plan-runs/ready-plans.ts); no new server primitive required for v1.

This closes the self-healing loop: filing auditors + burndown-watcher = a backlog that drains itself. pl-c34d (the cluster-C plan this audit just hand-built) is a worked example of the clustering this agent should automate.

Refs: SPEC §11.P, §11.R; docs/CONSTITUTION.md (Article IX audit population); .warren/triggers.yaml; .canopy/prompts.jsonl; warren-9d6f; pl-c34d.

title: warden-digest cron fails every 60s on deployed instance (agent not found)
description:
Observed live on warren-deployed.fly.dev (project prj_z07y6ssfjfpk): scheduler emits scheduler.cron_failed every ~60s with reason 'spawnRun failed: agent not found: warden-digest'.

Root cause: .warren/triggers.yaml entries (warden-digest, nightwatch, bugwatch, gatewatch, ratchetwatch, tastewatch) reference canopy/audit agent roles that are NOT inline built-ins (only claude-code + sapling ship inline). On a deployed instance without those agents registered (e.g. CANOPY_REPO_URL unset or library missing warden-digest), every due tick fails 'agent not found'.

Secondary concern worth confirming: the failure repeats every tick (60s) rather than respecting no-catch-up cron semantics. A cron of '0 5 * * 0' should fire weekly, not every minute. Suspect either (a) a past-due scheduledFor seed extension that never clears because dispatch fails before the clear, re-dispatching each tick, or (b) the cron 'due' evaluation mis-firing. Needs investigation in src/triggers/dispatch.ts + tick.ts.

Impact: log spam + wasted scheduler ticks; masks real signal. Also a motivating example for the observability/healing work (a healing agent should catch exactly this).

Fix options: (1) register the audit agents on the deployed instance / set CANOPY_REPO_URL, (2) make scheduler skip+warn-once for unknown roles with backoff instead of every-tick retry, (3) validate trigger roles against the agent registry at config load.

title: Phase 2: closed-loop alert intake -> healer agent dispatch (pi runtime)
description:
Builds on Phase 1 observability instrumentation (Sentry + /metrics + fly.toml, shipped). Goal: wake a healing agent when the codebase breaks.

DECISIONS (from design review):
- Stacks: both Sentry (errors) + Fly managed Grafana/Prometheus (dashboards/trend alerts).
- Healer agent: DEDICATED agent on the pi runtime (NOT claude-code). Ships inline alongside claude-code/sapling, or via canopy.
- Alert -> project mapping: static mapping in .warren/config.yaml WITH payload fallback (infer repo/project from alert context when no config match).
- Pattern: extend the proven ci-fixer loop (src/ci-fixer/poller.ts + src/triggers/ci-fixer-pass.ts), reusing its guard rails.

SCOPE:
1. POST /alerts/heal endpoint (token-gated) in ROUTE_TABLE. Two thin webhook adapters (Sentry alert webhook + Grafana alert webhook) normalizing to one internal HealAlert shape { fingerprint, title, culprit, detail, links, source }.
2. Dispatch a healer run via the existing spawnRun seam (same path createRunHandler / ci-fixer use) with a prompt composed from alert context.
3. Guard rails copied from ci-fixer: dedupe by fingerprint, cooldownMinutes, maxRetries. Persist attempts as a new heal.dispatched system event for idempotency + observability.
4. Per-project opt-in: healer block in .warren/config.yaml (enabled, role, cooldownMinutes, maxRetries, projectMapping) mirroring the ciFixer schema block in src/warren-config/schema.ts.
5. Dedicated pi-runtime healer agent definition + heal prompt template.
6. gen:docs + gen:openapi regen for the new route; tests (adapters, guard rails, dispatch) in the ci-fixer test style; bun run check:all green.

DEPENDS ON: Phase 1 (done, pending commit). Related: warren-c36a (warden-digest cron bug is a motivating heal target).

WHATS LEFT / FOLLOW-UPS (Phase 1 wrap):
- Deploy activation: fly secrets set SENTRY_DSN=... then fly deploy ([[metrics]] takes effect on deploy).
- Provision Sentry project + DSN; wire Sentry alert rule -> POST /alerts/heal (Phase 2).
- Build Grafana dashboards on fly-metrics.net (run-state/cost/token gauges + warren_log_messages_total rate) and alert rules.
- Optional later: Sentry tracing/profiling (tracesSampleRate currently 0); more domain-specific Prometheus counters (watchdog kills, worker_unreachable, cron_failed by trigger).

title: gatewatch: PR #461 (44123b3e) grandfathers newborn src/diagnostics/checks-config.ts into biome.json complexity override at birth — Article II
description:
SHA: 44123b3e3c3252d7a9355114aa5cfea06ef425c4 (PR #461, merged 2026-06-21 04:05 -0700).

Article II: 'Nothing is grandfathered at birth.' The procedure rule: a file added to a grandfather list in the same PR that creates it is grandfathering-at-birth — always a finding.

What happened: the PR decomposed src/diagnostics/checks.ts (616 lines) into a barrel + three NEW sibling modules. git show --stat shows checks-config.ts as added (A). In the same diff, biome.json swaps the noExcessiveCognitiveComplexity:off override entry 'src/diagnostics/checks.ts' -> 'src/diagnostics/checks-config.ts' (biome.json line 80, inside the complexity override includes block). The newborn checks-config.ts is thus exempted from the cognitive-complexity gate at write time rather than being decomposed below the threshold.

Evidence:
- git show 44123b3e --name-status: 'A  src/diagnostics/checks-config.ts'
- git show 44123b3e -- biome.json: line '-"src/diagnostics/checks.ts"' / '+"src/diagnostics/checks-config.ts"' in the noExcessiveCognitiveComplexity override list
- biome.json:80 (current) confirms checks-config.ts sits in the complexity-off override block (lines 62-127).

Same mechanical root cause as warren-eb77 (PR #391, pr-checks.ts) and warren-2484 (PR #436, in-flight.ts): a complexity exemption riding a moved function into a newborn file during a size-decomposition. The size ratchet went down correctly; the complexity exemption was carried at birth instead of refactored out.

The tracker ref warren-5bf4 in the diff covers the size decomposition, not a sanctioned complexity exemption — the override entry has no separate justification.
closeReason: Remediated on main: PR #476 (warren-5f1b) refactored src/diagnostics/checks-config.ts under cc15 and dropped its biome.json complexity override (now lists only coordinator.ts)

title: gatewatch: recurring grandfather-at-birth — decomposition PRs carry cognitive-complexity exemptions into newborn sibling files (Article II)
description:
Parent for a recurring mechanical root cause across three merged decomposition PRs. When a >500-line file carrying a noExcessiveCognitiveComplexity:off override is split, the complex function moves into a NEW sibling file and the biome.json override entry is renamed onto that newborn file instead of the function being refactored below the complexity ceiling. The file-size ratchet tightens correctly each time, but the complexity exemption is grandfathered at birth — violating Article II.

Instances (all biome.json noExcessiveCognitiveComplexity override block):
- warren-eb77 — PR #391 (938d21bc): src/runs/pr-checks.ts (mergePullRequest)
- warren-2484 — PR #436 (fba09fff): src/plan-runs/in-flight.ts
- warren-04dc — PR #461 (44123b3e): src/diagnostics/checks-config.ts

Remediation (see attached plan): for each newborn file, refactor the carried function under cognitive-complexity 15 and remove its biome.json override entry — the ratchet only goes down. No release step.
closeReason: Plan pl-80a1 done; all 3 children (warren-05bb/00c8/5f1b) closed; checks-config.ts refactored, override removed

title: gatewatch: PR #475 (1466ae81) merges with red-gate rationalization — --no-verify bypass over 'pre-existing, unrelated' failure (Article I)
description:
Article I (Merges are truthful): 'A merge is never rationalized past a red gate ("this failure is unrelated" is a finding, not a waiver).'

EVIDENCE:
- SHA 1466ae81 (PR #475), merged 2026-06-22 10:30 UTC, default branch. Subject: 'Refactor src/plan-runs/in-flight.ts under cognitive-complexity 15 and remove its biome.json noExcessiveCognitiveComplexity override entry'.
- Commit body verbatim: 'Pre-commit verify hook bypassed (--no-verify): the only failing gate is a pre-existing, unrelated failure in src/runs/spawn/plot-append.test.ts that reproduces on a clean tree at HEAD. lint, typecheck, and the plan-runs test suite all pass.'
- This is exactly the bypass language Article I names as a finding: a merge rationalized past a red gate ('pre-existing', 'unrelated', verify hook '--no-verify' bypassed).

WHY A FINDING REGARDLESS OF TRUTH: Per gatewatch mandate item 2, 'The claim may even be true; the merge is still a finding.' Article I requires the gate be fixed or the change be fixed — not waived in the commit message. Note the claim does appear to have been independently true: the named flaky test was subsequently repaired in SHA 84d6a3f4 (PR #481, 'Make spawn plot-append env assertions deterministic', 2026-06-23). That later fix does not retroactively sanction merging green-by-bypass on 06-22; the honest path was to land #481-style determinism first, then merge #475 on a green gate.

DIFF NOTE: The diff itself is otherwise compliant — it tightens a ratchet (removes the in-flight.ts noExcessiveCognitiveComplexity override from biome.json, carrying tracker warren-00c8) and the subject matches the diff (Article II / Article I title-truth both satisfied). The finding is solely the red-gate rationalization in the message.

DEDUPE: No open audit/gatewatch seed covers PR #475 / 1466ae81 / the plot-append --no-verify bypass (warren-27d3/72b9/8f4c are closed and concern reap bookkeeping hooks, not this agent-authored merge). Not covered by warren-d426 (Article IX, PRs #364/#362/#361/#338, out of window).

REMEDIATION: non-mechanical — process finding, no source fix to write. Single occurrence in window, so no cluster plan.

title: Reap: terminal turn with stopReason 'error' (e.g. Anthropic 'credit balance too low' 400) must fail the run, not succeed
description:
## Problem

When an agent's final turn ends with `stopReason: "error"` and a provider `errorMessage` (e.g. Anthropic `400 invalid_request_error`: "Your credit balance is too low to access the Anthropic API"), warren currently reaps the run as **succeeded**: it auto-commits only the seeds/mulch bookkeeping (`reap.seeds_committed`, filesStaged: 2 -> 'chore(warren): seeds state'), opens a PR with no agent code, marks state=succeeded, advances/merges the plan-run, and closes the seed. The agent's uncommitted source edits are then discarded by `reap.workspace_destroyed`.

## Evidence (warren-dogfood, plan pl-e381, gh #419)

- run_vhmmbsv0p3hr (warren-1053, PR #509): agent made 10 `edit` calls + ran check:all, then the next model turn returned 400 credit-exhausted error at 2026-06-25T17:24:41. Event id 1311891/1311892: `stopReason: error`, errorMessage 'Your credit balance is too low...'. 0 git commits. PR #509 shipped only the seeds-state commit; code lost.
- run_hj207hyzz8hv (warren-6207, PR #510): very first turn returned the same 400 (event 1312853/1312854), 0 tokens, 0 tool calls. Reaped succeeded, PR #510 = seeds state only.
- Contrast: runs that shipped (#506/#507/#508) all ran git add + git commit and ended on a normal stop.

## Expected

On a terminal turn with `stopReason === 'error'` and a non-empty `errorMessage`:
- mark the run **failed**, set failure_reason to the provider message;
- do NOT open a PR or close the seed;
- halt (not advance) the enclosing plan-run so it can resume after the cause (e.g. credit top-up) is fixed.

## Why this signal (not a diff-shape heuristic)

A 'bookkeeping-only' PR check would false-positive on legitimate runs that genuinely only need seeds/mulch changes (they end on a normal stop/end_turn). The `stopReason: error` + errorMessage signal is explicit and unambiguous, so it fails exactly the hard-error runs without punishing legitimate no-op-code runs.

## Notes

- Separately, run_vhmmbsv0p3hr's check:all was failing in-sandbox with 'Cannot find module' across many files (node_modules not installed) + a real warren-api-prefixes-stay-in-sync failure. Likely a workspace dep-install gap worth its own seed.
- Found via fly (app warren-deployed) + supabase MCP (project biaxgkadzaruhrqsjnmi) investigation of runs/events tables.

| 44123b3e | Decompose src/diagnostics/checks.ts (#461) | diverges | II |
| 1466ae81 | Refactor in-flight.ts under cog-complexity 15 (#475) | diverges | I |

Sampling basis this week: 3 largest by churn (63739975 2754 — almost entirely generated migration snapshots; 78881f65 1473; 1515380b 1364); 3 mid-distribution chosen by "feature commits carrying branching/seam logic near the 400–650 churn band" (23470206, 0dc4c979, 85e5cd3d — basis varied from last week's median-feature pick to stay unpredictable); 2 releases (§3 / §6); 2 patrol-produced (44123b3e ratchetwatch decomposition child of warren-5bf4; 1466ae81 gatewatch-remediation child of warren-00c8). Both releases judged separately for Article III.

Substance notes: craft is strong. 1515380b ships genuinely adversarial Article IV tests (corrupt `lastAttemptAt` never strands; `max_retries` precedence over `cooldown`; cooldown boundary at/after window) — verification, not theater. 23470206 pairs a 264-line renderer with a 258-line test. 85e5cd3d's new `bridge-reconnect.ts` lines are a MODIFICATION of a pre-existing file (verified `git cat-file -e 85e5cd3d^:src/server/bridge-reconnect.ts`), not a silent new module — no scope creep. The two "diverges" are isolated process findings, both already independently caught by gatewatch (warren-04dc/warren-6a91; warren-3c4d) — a precision signal, not new failures.

PRIOR-DIGEST FOLLOW-UP (resolved): last week's single systemic divergence — tracked `.pi/sessions/*.jsonl` transcripts riding unrelated commits (5/10 of the 06-14 sample) — is GONE. Zero session transcripts appear in any of this week's 72 commits or 10-sample diffs; the `.gitignore` fix (#401) landed. The 06-14 rate was inflated by that one cause; with it fixed the real divergence floor drops sharply (see §2).

## 2. Divergence rate

This week: 2/10 = 20%. Last week: 5/10 = 50%. The drop is real, not cosmetic: last week's 5 all shared ONE now-fixed systemic cause (`.pi/sessions` leak); this week's 2 are unrelated, isolated, and already population-flagged. Substantive craft was clean in both windows — the headline number moved because the systemic leak was closed.

## 3. Most important divergence — grandfather-at-birth in decomposition PRs (Article II), a recurring fix-on-fix class

44123b3e (#461, 06-21) decomposed `src/diagnostics/checks.ts`; in `biome.json` (override list ~line 80) it MOVED the `noExcessiveCognitiveComplexity` exemption from `checks.ts` to the NEWBORN `src/diagnostics/checks-config.ts` — a file that did not exist before the PR — directly violating Article II ("Nothing is grandfathered at birth: a new file written over the limit is decomposed before merge, not exempted at write time"). This is the THIRD identical instance in the run-up: 938d21bc (#391) birthed `pr-checks.ts` exempted, fba09fff (#436) birthed `in-flight.ts` exempted. gatewatch caught all three (warren-eb77, warren-2484, warren-04dc) and meta-flagged the pattern (warren-6a91); each was then remediated by a same-window complexity refactor within ~24–48h (fb04b24f, 6fba588b, and 1466ae81 — all 06-22). That is a textbook fix-on-fix chain. Named class: **decompose-then-grandfather-then-refactor** — the decomposition procedure carries complexity/size overrides FORWARD onto newborn siblings instead of refactoring inline, guaranteeing a follow-up refactor PR per split. The missing piece is a single executable gate (§5).

Secondary divergence (sample row 1466ae81, Article I): the very commit that remediated in-flight.ts merged green-by-bypass — body verbatim "Pre-commit verify hook bypassed (--no-verify): the only failing gate is a pre-existing, unrelated failure in src/runs/spawn/plot-append.test.ts". Article I names exactly this ("'this failure is unrelated' is a finding, not a waiver"). gatewatch filed it with full evidence (warren-3c4d, open); the flaky test was independently repaired the next day in 84d6a3f4 (#481), which does not retroactively sanction the bypass. Folded here, not re-filed — gatewatch owns it.

## 4. Auditor-population precision (findings filed since the 06-14 digest, 06-15 → 06-28)

- **ratchetwatch:** ~37 filed (12 weekly "tightening: <date>" rollups + ~25 concrete decompose/extract/floor tasks). 34 closed, **all closed-as-fixed** (their `[plan done]` decompositions landed: warren-5bf4→44123b3e, warren-895b→8c65ce62, warren-5f55/c2dc→e46e5a6f/0c3a740d, warren-ac7f→f31ad593, etc.), 0 closed-wontfix, 3 still open (warren-afe0/1b11/2dae — this week's rollups awaiting their children). **Closed-precision 34/34 = 100% fixed.** Second consecutive 100%-fixed digest.
- **gatewatch:** 8 findings filed (warren-172e, a19d, 9705, 2484, eb77, 04dc, 6a91, 3c4d). 4 closed — **all closed-as-fixed** (the grandfather-at-birth class warren-2484/eb77/04dc + meta warren-6a91, remediated by the 06-22 refactors), 0 wontfix, 4 still open (warren-172e/a19d/9705 title↔diff mismatches + warren-3c4d red-gate, awaiting human/process triage — none are stale-false, they are unactioned process findings). **Closed-precision 4/4 = 100% fixed.** Note the quality: gatewatch independently pre-flagged BOTH of this digest's sampled divergences (§3) before tastewatch sampled them — zero false positives observed this window.

Autonomy-promotion read: this is the SECOND consecutive digest with both auditors at 100% closed-as-fixed, 0 wontfix — the ≥2-digest trend the 06-14 digest said to wait for. ratchetwatch's signal is mechanical and consistently landed; gatewatch's is process/judgment and consistently true. RECOMMENDATION (advisory only, per Article IX / warden "Autonomy-promotion recommendations"): tastewatch now supports advancing ratchetwatch toward a scoped autonomy increment (its output is deterministic decomposition with a perfect landing record); hold gatewatch one more cycle only because its open findings are process-class (title/diff, red-gate) that need human disposition, not a precision concern. Any change to `.canopy/` or `.warren/triggers.yaml` requires explicit human review — delivered to the warden for routing, not applied.

## 5. Proposed gate (one; propose-only per Article IX)

Add `check:no-grandfather-at-birth` to `scripts/check-all.ts` (and CI). For each path newly added to `biome.json`'s `noExcessiveCognitiveComplexity` override list OR to `scripts/file-size-budgets.json` in a PR, fail if that path did not exist on the merge base — concretely, `git cat-file -e <base>:<path>` must succeed (the file pre-existed) for any added exemption entry; a newborn path in either exemption list is a hard fail. This converts Article II's manual "nothing grandfathered at birth" judgment — and gatewatch's recurring warren-6a91 finding — into the executable gate the constitution says every article aspires to, and it structurally ends the decompose-then-grandfather-then-refactor fix-on-fix chain (§3). Optional one-line Article II addendum: "a decomposition that exempts a newborn sibling is grandfathering-at-birth and is rejected; refactor the split inline." Proposed, not applied.

## 6. Trajectory

Tightening — the one systemic leak from 06-14 is closed, divergence halved to isolated process findings, both releases consumer-observable (v0.9.7 feat(dispatch)/feat(alerts)/feat(config); v0.9.8 a real plot-staging behavior fix), test craft is adversarial, and the audit population is running at 100% closed-as-fixed precision with gatewatch pre-catching tastewatch's own sample.

WARREN_API_TOKEN is now present (warren-f248 fixed), so the 401 blocker from the 06-14 digest is resolved; the remaining gap is the missing warden conversation. Per report-only mandate I did not create the conversation (that would be a new path). This seed is the durable record.

title: Dispatch (run or plan-run) should refresh repo before spawning
description: Dispatching a run or plan-run should refresh the project's host clone (fetch + update default branch) at the core dispatch path, not just via a UI action. Today after pushing a seed you must manually refresh the repo before dispatching so the agent sees the latest state. Make repo refresh an intrinsic part of how runs and plan-runs dispatch so the freshly-pushed seed/branch state is always present in the sandbox without manual intervention.

title: Runs sweep an unintended .canopy/agent.json provider/model flip into PRs, blocking Article IX auto-merge
description:
A run for an unrelated seed (warren-1d4c, a server eslint-comment chore) shipped PR jayminwest/warren#543 with a stray .canopy/agent.json diff flipping the builtin agent from anthropic/claude-opus-4-8 to zai/glm-5.2. Because .canopy/ is a constitution-protected path, auto-merge.yml's Article IX guard refused to arm auto-merge ("auto-merge will NOT be enabled: .canopy/agent.json"), so the PR needs a human merge and plan-run plnr_1bw1cw8ch4n6 (plan pl-4d2e) stalled. Every cleanup-batch child PR carries this same protected-path diff.

ROOT CAUSE (traced):
Write site: src/runs/seed.ts buildSeedFiles() unconditionally pushes a workspace file at path ".canopy/agent.json" containing the rendered AgentDefinition envelope { name, version, sections, resolvedFrom, frontmatter }. The serialized frontmatter carries the EFFECTIVE provider/model resolved in src/runs/spawn/dispatch.ts (resolveOverride over defaultProvider/defaultModel), which is now zai/glm-5.2 after the provider-default switch.

Collision: the warren repo TRACKS .canopy/agent.json at HEAD as the builtin pi envelope (provider anthropic, model claude-opus-4-8). When warren operates on itself, the seed drop overwrites that tracked file with zai/glm-5.2, leaving a modified tracked file in the working tree.

Leak into the PR: PR #543 has two commits -
  - 136ea508f  author "Warren" (the agent's WARREN_GIT_AUTHOR identity), "chore(server): remove stale eslint-disable comment (#541)" -> touches .canopy/agent.json, .seeds/issues.jsonl, .seeds/plans.jsonl, src/server/main/index.ts
  - ee9978343  author warren <warren@os-eco.dev> (reap bookkeeping bot), .seeds/* only
The stray .canopy/agent.json flip is in the AGENT's commit, not warren's reap commit. The agent (system prompt: "Use git as you normally would. Commit your changes") ran a broad git add -A / git commit -a and swept the warren-seeded .canopy/agent.json modification in alongside its intended one-line edit. Reap staging (src/runs/reap/stage.ts) uses scoped git add -- <pathspec>, so reap is NOT the culprit.

FIX OPTIONS:
1. At provision, write the seed drop paths (.canopy/, .mulch/, .seeds/workflow.txt, .pi/) into the worktree .git/info/exclude so the agent's git add -A cannot stage them. NOTE: exclude does not cover an already-tracked file.
2. For repos that already track a seed path (warren-on-warren, canopy-library repos): skip seeding that path, or run git update-index --assume-unchanged on it post-seed so the seeded clobber never shows as a working-tree modification.
3. Combine 1+2: exclude for untracked drops, assume-unchanged for tracked ones.

Refs: plan-run plnr_1bw1cw8ch4n6, PR jayminwest/warren#543, src/runs/seed.ts, src/runs/spawn/dispatch.ts, .github/workflows/auto-merge.yml (Article IX guard).

title: Plan-run merge gate mis-maps transient GitHub 401/403/429 to pr_closed_without_merge
description: src/plan-runs/merge-gate.ts isFatalHttpError() returns true for ANY 4xx (status >= 400 && < 500). src/plan-runs/in-flight.ts:319 (and merge-gate.ts:114) then treat a fatal http_error identically to closed_unmerged and call failChild(..., 'pr_closed_without_merge'). A transient GitHub 401 (confirmed intermittent: 1 probe 401 then 6/6 200, /user 200, rate limit healthy) on the merge-status poll killed plan-run plnr_1bw1cw8ch4n6 at seq 1 even though PR #543 was OPEN/CLEAN/MERGEABLE (plan_run.failed event carried httpStatus:401). Only 404/410 genuinely mean the PR is gone; 401/403/429 are auth/throttle = 'cannot verify', should keep waiting. Also src/plan-runs/pr-merge.ts only retries status 0 or 5xx, so a single 4xx is returned immediately with no retry. Fix: narrow isFatalHttpError to 404/410 and/or add 401/403/429 to the pr-merge retry set.

title: Guard against warren provider-default outpacing the deployed burrow-cli (zai burrow_unreachable wave)
description: Before the v95 deploy, warren config defaulted agents to provider=zai/glm-5.2 while the deployed image still ran burrow-cli 0.3.12, which lacks zai env-passthrough. pi got --provider zai but no ZAI_API_KEY -> 'No API key found for zai' -> the dead run hung until burrow_unreachable (~50 min) and was reaped failed (run_sgg1t1wkvws8, run_tfhp1dmhz79w, gatewatch/nightwatch crons). v95 ships burrow-cli 0.3.14 (ZAI_API_KEY forwarded when frontmatter.provider==='zai') and ZAI_API_KEY is present, so it's resolved. Follow-up: (a) consider failing fast when the sandbox emits 'No API key found' instead of hanging to burrow_unreachable, and (b) treat the burrow-cli pin + provider-default as a coupled change so a provider switch can't deploy ahead of the runtime that supports it.
